Give your agent Superpowers: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Codex App, Factory Droid, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Cursor, GitHub Copilot CLI.

Corpus spine (000)

navigation + canonical map

Overview

Corpus spine (000) — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • This repository is a comparative research corpus for coding agents and harnesses. Use this map to decide where to read first without physically moving existing data.
  • For reversible old-to-new bucket proposals, see 000/02_corpus_move_candidates.md.
  • For the canonical relationship between 000/ and 000_overview/, see 000/03_overview_canonical_map.md.
  • | Purpose | Path | Classification |
  • | Root overview and synthesis | 000/ | overview-index |
  • | Deep harness overview docs | 000_overview/ | deep-overview |
  • | TUI design axis | 001_tui-design/ | canonical-doc-axis |
  • | Prompt/context axis | 002_prompt-context/ | canonical-doc-axis |
  • | Tool/runtime axis | 003_tool-runtime/ | canonical-doc-axis |
  • | Skill system axis | 004_skill-system/ | canonical-doc-axis |
  • | Subagents/collaboration axis | 005_subagents-collab/ | canonical-doc-axis |
  • | App/server/client surfaces axis | 006_app-server-client-surfaces/ | canonical-doc-axis |

Corpus Navigation Map

This repository is a comparative research corpus for coding agents and harnesses. Use this map to decide where to read first without physically moving existing data.

For reversible old-to-new bucket proposals, see 000/02_corpus_move_candidates.md.

For the canonical relationship between 000/ and 000_overview/, see 000/03_overview_canonical_map.md.

Physical Rename Rule

Before a folder is moved, add an explicit mapping row using:

old_path -> proposed_path | classification | move_status | evidence

For gitlinks, also reconcile .gitmodules before moving.

Top-Level Triage

Use this when scanning the root directory. Status is intentionally conservative: keep-current means the current path remains the source of truth for now.

Current Path Triage Status
`.agen

000/03_overview_canonical_map.md

Overview Canonical Map

This map resolves the apparent duplication between 000/ and 000_overview/ without deleting or moving files.

Canonical Rule

  • 000/ is the root overview index and short-entry reading spine.
  • 000_overview/ is the deep overview source set.
  • If the same filename exists in both folders and content differs, keep both:
    • read 000/<name> first for orientation,
    • read 000_overview/<name> for detailed analysis.
  • If content is identical, treat 000/<name> as the navigation copy and 000_overview/<name> as the deep-overview location until a later link audit decides whether deduplication is safe.

Paired Overview Files

Filename 000_overview/ bytes 000/ bytes Equal Rule
00_ag_overview.md 27224 15790 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_ai_overview.md 25716 25716 yes duplicate content; defer dedupe until link audit
00_ay_overview.md 3815 798 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_cc_overview.md 25990 27537 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_co_overview.md 41569 17061 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_cp_overview.md 23525 6854 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_cr_overview.md 3216 595 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_ge_overview.md 47345 35414 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_gr_overview.md 3310 546 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_ki_overview.md 3360 692 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_oc_overview.md 43650 33628 no keep both: orientation plus detail

Deep-Overview Only Files

These exist under 000_overview/ without same-name files under 000/:

000/00_harness-index.md (excerpt)

Harness Source Index

This file is the non-conflicting 000/ index for source-repository harnesses.

The canonical deep overview files stay under 000_overview/. Files in 000 named 00_harness-index_* are short routing/index notes only. They intentionally avoid the canonical 00_<prefix>_overview.md basename so wiki/backlink indexers do not see two files with the same 00_*_overview identity.

Routing

Prefix Index stub Canonical overview Classification
hermes 000/00_harness-index_hermes.md 000_overview/00_hermes_overview.md standalone source-based agent runtime
omc 000/00_harness-index_omc.md 000_overview/00_omc_overview.md Claude Code host-dependent orchestration harness
omx 000/00_harness-index_omx.md 000_overview/00_omx_overview.md Codex host-dependent control plane
omo 000/00_harness-index_omo.md 000_overview/00_omo_overview.md OpenCode host-dependent harness plus Codex Light adapter
lc n/a 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md LazyCodex / Codex Light distribution wrapper for OmO
sp n/a 000_overview/00_sp_overview.md Superpowers multi-harness skills/bootstrap methodology layer

Harness at a glance

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Harness at a glance — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • | 항목 | 수치 / 경로 |
  • | GitHub repo | obra/superpowers |
  • | Local checkout | 162_superpowers/ |
  • | Version | 5.1.0 (locked across manifests) |
  • | npm package | superpowers — no bin field |
  • | First-party skills | 14 skills/*/SKILL.md |
  • | Harness adapters | 6 (Codex, Claude, Cursor, Gemini, OpenCode, Copilot) |
  • | MCP servers | 0 |
  • | Executable lines (hooks/scripts) | ~200 (bootstrap + sync + brainstorming server) |
  • | Axis docs T2 bar | 14/14 ≥12 KB (WP54) |
  • | License | MIT |

05

Agent workflow

Agent workflow

Overview

Agent workflow — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • Daily edit, tool, and commit loop for this harness.

  • 162_superpowers/README.md

    1. brainstorming - Activates before writing code. Refines rough ideas through questions, explores alternatives, presents design in sections for validation. Saves design document.
    1. using-git-worktrees - Activates after design approval. Creates isolated workspace on new branch, runs project setup, verifies clean test baseline.
    1. writing-plans - Activates with approved design. Breaks work into bite-sized tasks (2-5 minutes each). Every task has exact file paths, complete code, verification steps.
    1. subagent-driven-development or executing-plans - Activates with plan. Dispatches fresh subagent per task with two-stage review (spec compliance, then code quality), or executes in batches with human checkpoints.
    1. test-driven-development - Activates during implementation. Enforces RED-GREEN-REFACTOR: write failing test, watch it fail, write minimal code, watch it pass, commit. Deletes code written before tests.
    1. requesting-code-review - Activates between tasks. Reviews against plan, reports issues by severity. Critical issues block progress.
    1. finishing-a-development-branch - Activates when tasks complete. Verifies tests, presents options (merge/PR/keep/discard), cleans up worktree.
  • The agent checks for relevant skills before any task. Mandatory workflows, not suggestions.

Topics covered

  • Agent workflow
  • The Basic Workflow

Source snippets

162_superpowers/README.md

Quickstart

Give your agent Superpowers: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Codex App, Factory Droid, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Cursor, GitHub Copilot CLI.

Main analysis

The daily edit/tool/commit loop for this harness.

162_superpowers/README.md

The Basic Workflow

  1. brainstorming - Activates before writing code. Refines rough ideas through questions, explores alternatives, presents design in sections for validation. Saves design document.

  2. using-git-worktrees - Activates after design approval. Creates isolated workspace on new branch, runs project setup, verifies clean test baseline.

  3. writing-plans - Activates with approved design. Breaks work into bite-sized tasks (2-5 minutes each). Every task has exact file paths, complete code, verification steps.

  4. subagent-driven-development or executing-plans - Activates with plan. Dispatches fresh subagent per task with two-stage review (spec compliance, then code quality), or executes in batches with human checkpoints.

  5. test-driven-development - Activates during implementation. Enforces RED-GREEN-REFACTOR: write failing test, watch it fail, write minimal code, watch it pass, commit. Deletes code written before tests.

  6. requesting-code-review - Activates between tasks. Reviews against plan, reports issues by severity. Critical issues block progress.

  7. finishing-a-development-branch - Activates when tasks complete. Verifies tests, presents options (merge/PR/keep/discard), cleans up worktree.

The agent checks for relevant skills before any task. Mandatory workflows, not suggestions.

Superpowers Methodology

Skills-based development workflow (gitlink).

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Korean corpus (reference)

에이전트 루프

이 하네스의 일상적인 편집·도구·커밋 루프.

162_superpowers/README.md

The Basic Workflow

  1. brainstorming - Activates before writing code. Refines rough ideas through questions, explores alternatives, presents design in sections for validation. Saves design document.

  2. using-git-worktrees - Activates after design approval. Creates isolated workspace on new branch, runs project setup, verifies clean test baseline.

  3. writing-plans - Activates with approved design. Breaks work into bite-sized tasks (2-5 minutes each). Every task has exact file paths, complete code, verification steps.

  4. subagent-driven-development or executing-plans - Activates with plan. Dispatches fresh subagent per task with two-stage review (spec compliance, then code quality), or executes in batches with human checkpoints.

  5. test-driven-development - Activates during implementation. Enforces RED-GREEN-REFACTOR: write failing test, watch it fail, write minimal code, watch it pass, commit. Deletes code written before tests.

  6. requesting-code-review - Activates between tasks. Reviews against plan, reports issues by severity. Critical issues block progress.

  7. finishing-a-development-branch - Activates when tasks complete. Verifies tests, presents options (merge/PR/keep/discard), cleans up worktree.

The agent checks for relevant skills before any task. Mandatory workflows, not suggestions.

Superpowers 방법론

스킬 기반 개발 워크플로 (gitlink).

Overview §1

DEV

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Overview §1 — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • Superpowers README positioning:

  • A composable skills framework that makes coding agents more disciplined through planning, TDD, debugging, and collaboration workflows.

  • | Responsibility | Owner |
  • | Terminal TUI, transcript | Host |
  • | Model loop, streaming, compaction | Host |
  • | Tool dispatch (bash, apply_patch, etc.) | Host |
  • | Sandbox, permissions, approvals | Host |
  • | spawn_agent / Task / subagent isolation | Host |
  • | MCP servers, web search | Host |
  • | SKILL.md corpus + bootstrap injection | Superpowers |
  • | Workflow prompt templates + gates | Superpowers skills |
  • | Multi-harness install manifests | Superpowers adapters |

Topics covered

    1. Identity — methodology plugin, not agent runtime
    1. Identity — methodology plugin, not agent runtime

Main analysis

1. Identity — methodology plugin, not agent runtime

1. Identity — methodology plugin, not agent runtime

Superpowers README positioning:

A composable skills framework that makes coding agents more disciplined through planning, TDD, debugging, and collaboration workflows.

Responsibility Owner
Terminal TUI, transcript Host
Model loop, streaming, compaction Host
Tool dispatch (bash, apply_patch, etc.) Host
Sandbox, permissions, approvals Host
spawn_agent / Task / subagent isolation Host
MCP servers, web search Host
SKILL.md corpus + bootstrap injection Superpowers
Workflow prompt templates + gates Superpowers skills
Multi-harness install manifests Superpowers adapters

Negative claim — no binary:

// 162_superpowers/package.json:1-6
{
  "name": "superpowers",
  "version": "5.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": ".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"
}

Distribution is a plugin/skills bundle, not an executable with its own event loop.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 000_overview/00_sp_overview.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

08

Overview §2

DEV

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Overview §2 — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • Each skill: skills/<kebab-name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter:
  • name: using-superpowers
  • description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills...
  • Host discovery uses name + description for triggering. No central skill VM — loading semantics are host-dependent.
  • Core rules (skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md):
  • | Section | Lines | Rule |
  • | Mandatory invocation | :10-16 | Invoke relevant skill before ANY action including clarifying questions |
  • | Instruction priority | :18-26 | User AGENTS.md > Superpowers skills > default system prompt |
  • | Platform Skill tool | :28-36 | Host must expose skill read/invoke |
  • | Skill flow DOT graph | :48-75 | Decision tree for which skill to load |

Topics covered

    1. Skill system — primary product (004)
    1. Skill system — primary product (004)
  • 2.1 SKILL.md schema
  • 2.2 Bootstrap: using-superpowers
  • 2.3 First-party inventory (14 skills)
  • 2.4 Codex exposure

Source code snippets

README / docs

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills...
---

README / docs

// 162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:23
"skills": "./skills/"

Main analysis

2. Skill system — primary product (004)

2. Skill system — primary product (004)

2.1 SKILL.md schema

Each skill: skills/<kebab-name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter:

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills...
---

Host discovery uses name + description for triggering. No central skill VM — loading semantics are host-dependent.

2.2 Bootstrap: using-superpowers

Core rules (skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md):

Section Lines Rule
Mandatory invocation :10-16 Invoke relevant skill before ANY action including clarifying questions
Instruction priority :18-26 User AGENTS.md > Superpowers skills > default system prompt
Platform Skill tool :28-36 Host must expose skill read/invoke
Skill flow DOT graph :48-75 Decision tree for which skill to load
Red-flag rationalization :78-95 "This is simple enough" → still invoke skill
Subagent skip gate :6-8 Subagents must follow same skill rules

Embedded wholesale by SessionStart hook (hooks/session-start:17-35).

2.3 First-party inventory (14 skills)

Category Skill Purpose
Meta using-superpowers Bootstrap + invocation rules
Meta writing-skills Author/test new skills
Process brainstorming Design-before-code HARD-GATE
Process systematic-debugging 4-phase root cause (Iron Law)
Process verification-before-completion Evidence before "done"
Implementation test-driven-development RED-GREEN-REFACTOR
Implementation writing-plans Bite-sized tasks with paths
Implementation executing-plans Batch execution + checkpoints
Implementation using-git-worktrees Isolated workspace
Implementation finishing-a-development-branch Merge/PR/keep/discard
Collaboration subagent-driven-development Fresh subagent + 2-stage review
Collaboration dispatching-parallel-agents Parallel investigations
Collaboration requesting-code-review Pre-review checklist
Collaboration receiving-code-review Feedback response discipline

README workflow order (README.md:154-170):

brainstorming → worktrees → writing-plans → (SDD | executing-plans) → TDD → code review → finishing branch.

2.4 Codex exposure

// 162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:23
"skills": "./skills/"

No hooks in Codex manifest — native skill discovery only. Marketplace UI metadata (interface.displayName, defaultPrompt, logos) affects Codex App plugin picker, not terminal layout.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 000_overview/00_sp_overview.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Overview §3

DEV

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Overview §3 — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • Superpowers does not construct provider API payloads, token budgets, or repo maps.
  • flowchart TD
  • US["using-superpowers/SKILL.md"] --> SS["hooks/session-start"]
  • US --> OC["OpenCode messages.transform"]
  • US --> GEM["GEMINI.md @imports"]
  • US --> Codex["Native skill path"]
  • SS --> HostCtx["Host context slot"]
  • OC --> FirstUser["First user message prefix"]
  • GEM --> GeminiMerge["Gemini context merge"]
  • HostCtx --> Rule["Skill-before-action mandate"]
  • Instruction priority (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26):
    1. User explicit instructions (AGENTS.md, direct chat) — highest

Topics covered

    1. Prompt / context surface (002)
    1. Prompt / context surface (002)
  • SessionStart injection (Claude/Cursor/Copilot path)
  • OpenCode path

Source code snippets

README / docs

flowchart TD
  US["using-superpowers/SKILL.md"] --> SS["hooks/session-start"]
  US --> OC["OpenCode messages.transform"]
  US --> GEM["GEMINI.md @imports"]
  US --> Codex["Native skill path"]
  SS --> HostCtx["Host context slot"]
  OC --> FirstUser["First user message prefix"]
  GEM --> GeminiMerge["Gemini context merge"]
  HostCtx --> Rule["Skill-before-action mandate"]

README / docs

// 162_superpowers/hooks/hooks.json:2-14
"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{
    "type": "command",
    "command": "\"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.cmd\" session-start",
    "async": false
  }]
}]

Main analysis

3. Prompt / context surface (002)

3. Prompt / context surface (002)

Superpowers does not construct provider API payloads, token budgets, or repo maps.

flowchart TD
  US["using-superpowers/SKILL.md"] --> SS["hooks/session-start"]
  US --> OC["OpenCode messages.transform"]
  US --> GEM["GEMINI.md @imports"]
  US --> Codex["Native skill path"]
  SS --> HostCtx["Host context slot"]
  OC --> FirstUser["First user message prefix"]
  GEM --> GeminiMerge["Gemini context merge"]
  HostCtx --> Rule["Skill-before-action mandate"]

Instruction priority (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26):

  1. User explicit instructions (AGENTS.md, direct chat) — highest
  2. Superpowers skills — override default system on conflict
  3. Default system prompt — lowest

Example: user says "skip TDD" → follow user even if test-driven-development says always TDD. This is policy in markdown, not code enforcement.

SessionStart injection (Claude/Cursor/Copilot path)

// 162_superpowers/hooks/hooks.json:2-14
"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{
    "type": "command",
    "command": "\"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.cmd\" session-start",
    "async": false
  }]
}]

hooks/session-start:17-35 reads using-superpowers/SKILL.md and emits host-specific JSON (additionalContext or Claude hook output format).

OpenCode path

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js uses experimental.chat.messages.transform to prepend bootstrap to first user message — different injection slot than Claude SessionStart.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 000_overview/00_sp_overview.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

3. Prompt / context surface (002)

3. Prompt / context surface (002)

Superpowers does not construct provider API payloads, token budgets, or repo maps.

flowchart TD
  US["using-superpowers/SKILL.md"] --> SS["hooks/session-start"]
  US --> OC["OpenCode messages.transform"]
  US --> GEM["GEMINI.md @imports"]
  US --> Codex["Native skill path"]
  SS --> HostCtx["Host context slot"]
  OC --> FirstUser["First user message prefix"]
  GEM --> GeminiMerge["Gemini context merge"]
  HostCtx --> Rule["Skill-before-action mandate"]

Instruction priority (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26):

  1. User explicit instructions (AGENTS.md, direct chat) — highest
  2. Superpowers skills — override default system on conflict
  3. Default system prompt — lowest

Example: user says "skip TDD" → follow user even if test-driven-development says always TDD. This is policy in markdown, not code enforcement.

SessionStart injection (Claude/Cursor/Copilot path)

// 162_superpowers/hooks/hooks.json:2-14
"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{
    "type": "command",
    "command": "\"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.cmd\" session-start",
    "async": false
  }]
}]

hooks/session-start:17-35 reads using-superpowers/SKILL.md and emits host-specific JSON (additionalContext or Claude hook output format).

OpenCode path

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js uses experimental.chat.messages.transform to prepend bootstrap to first user message — different injection slot than Claude SessionStart.

10

Overview §4

DEV

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Overview §4 — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • Superpowers registers zero tools. Skill examples reference host tool names; docs/codex-tools.md maps Claude/OpenCode names to Codex:
  • | Skill example (generic) | Codex mapping |
  • | TodoWrite | Host todo/plan tool |
  • | Task / subagent spawn | spawn_agent |
  • | Bash | shell / host exec |
  • | File read/write | Host native file tools |
  • Gemini path: GEMINI.md contains parallel tool name table for Gemini CLI.
  • Negative finding: Grep of 162_superpowers/ shows no tool registry, no JSON-RPC server, no permission evaluator — only markdown tables and skill prose.

Topics covered

    1. Tool runtime — mapping only (003)
    1. Tool runtime — mapping only (003)

Bash shell / host exec
File read/write Host native file tools

Gemini path: GEMINI.md contains parallel tool name table for Gemini CLI.

11

Overview §5

DEV

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Overview §5 — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • Superpowers defines workflows in SKILL.md, not a subagent scheduler.
  • skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:
  • Principle (:10-14): Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality)
  • When to use (:16-40): Plan exists + independent tasks + same session
  • Controller discipline: Never let subagents inherit full session history; paste complete task text inline
  • Review stages: Spec compliance reviewer → code quality reviewer
  • skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md — independent domain investigations in parallel, then integrate + run full test suite.
  • | Capability | Owner |
  • | spawn_agent / Task dispatch | Host |
  • | Context isolation | Host (best-effort) |
  • | Parallel scheduler | Host |
  • | Review prompt content | Superpowers templates |

Topics covered

    1. Subagents & collaboration (005)
    1. Subagents & collaboration (005)
  • 5.1 Subagent-driven development (SDD)
  • 5.2 Parallel dispatch
  • 5.3 Host delegation table

Source code snippets

162_superpowers/README.md

Quickstart

Give your agent Superpowers: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Codex App, Factory Droid, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Cursor, GitHub Copilot CLI.

Main analysis

5. Subagents & collaboration (005)

5. Subagents & collaboration (005)

Superpowers defines workflows in SKILL.md, not a subagent scheduler.

5.1 Subagent-driven development (SDD)

skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:

  • Principle (:10-14): Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality)
  • When to use (:16-40): Plan exists + independent tasks + same session
  • Controller discipline: Never let subagents inherit full session history; paste complete task text inline
  • Review stages: Spec compliance reviewer → code quality reviewer

5.2 Parallel dispatch

skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md — independent domain investigations in parallel, then integrate + run full test suite.

5.3 Host delegation table

Capability Owner
spawn_agent / Task dispatch Host
Context isolation Host (best-effort)
Parallel scheduler Host
Review prompt content Superpowers templates
Controller workflow rules Superpowers skills

Superpowers cannot guarantee technical isolation — it instructs the controller via skill text.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 000_overview/00_sp_overview.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

5. Subagents & collaboration (005)

5. Subagents & collaboration (005)

Superpowers defines workflows in SKILL.md, not a subagent scheduler.

5.1 Subagent-driven development (SDD)

skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:

  • Principle (:10-14): Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality)
  • When to use (:16-40): Plan exists + independent tasks + same session
  • Controller discipline: Never let subagents inherit full session history; paste complete task text inline
  • Review stages: Spec compliance reviewer → code quality reviewer

5.2 Parallel dispatch

skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md — independent domain investigations in parallel, then integrate + run full test suite.

5.3 Host delegation table

Capability Owner
spawn_agent / Task dispatch Host
Context isolation Host (best-effort)
Parallel scheduler Host

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Overview §7 — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • | Feature | Codex (co) | Superpowers (sp) |
  • | TUI crate/module | codex-rs/tui | None |
  • | bin CLI | codex binary | Absent |
  • | Terminal widgets | Ratatui | None |
  • | Session chrome | Host | Host renders; SP injects bootstrap text |
  • skills/brainstorming/ includes optional local HTTP/WebSocket server (server.cjs) that opens HTML mockups in browser — adjunct to host TUI, not replacement. Used during design phase only.
  • | Surface | Role |
  • | skills/ directory | Methodology corpus |
  • | Hook scripts | Bootstrap injection |
  • | .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js | OpenCode registration |
  • | Plugin manifests (6 hosts) | Install/discovery metadata |
  • | docs/codex-tools.md | Tool name mapping reference |

Overview §8

DEV

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Overview §8 — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • No --sandbox, seatbelt, allowlist, or command classifier in repo. Skills reference host sandbox expectations (e.g., run tests before claiming done) but do not implement isolation.
  • skills/using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md guides isolated workspace creation via host git/worktree commands — policy, not enforcement.
  • Configuration = plugin manifests + host config after install:
  • Codex: .codex-plugin/plugin.json — skills path, UI metadata
  • Gemini: gemini-extension.json + GEMINI.md tool map
  • No layered settings merge engine, no .superpowersrc
  • Install paths documented per host in README (Claude marketplace, Codex plugin install, Cursor plugin, etc.).
  • Negative finding: No .mcp.json, no stdio MCP server, no OAuth broker, no multi-server orchestration. MCP in other harnesses (if any) comes from host or other plugins — not Superpowers.
  • Skills are stateless markdown. No:
  • Vector index or embeddings
  • Session fact extraction pipeline
  • MEMORY.md or cross-session replay DB

Topics covered

    1. Sandbox, config, MCP, memory (007–010, 009)
    1. Sandbox, config, MCP, memory (007–010, 009)
  • Sandbox (007)
  • Config (008)
  • MCP (009)
  • Memory (010)

Source code snippets

162_superpowers/README.md

Quickstart

Give your agent Superpowers: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Codex App, Factory Droid, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Cursor, GitHub Copilot CLI.

Main analysis

8. Sandbox, config, MCP, memory (007–010, 009)

8. Sandbox, config, MCP, memory (007–010, 009)

Sandbox (007)

No --sandbox, seatbelt, allowlist, or command classifier in repo. Skills reference host sandbox expectations (e.g., run tests before claiming done) but do not implement isolation.

skills/using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md guides isolated workspace creation via host git/worktree commands — policy, not enforcement.

Config (008)

Configuration = plugin manifests + host config after install:

  • Codex: .codex-plugin/plugin.json — skills path, UI metadata
  • Gemini: gemini-extension.json + GEMINI.md tool map
  • No layered settings merge engine, no .superpowersrc

Install paths documented per host in README (Claude marketplace, Codex plugin install, Cursor plugin, etc.).

MCP (009)

Negative finding: No .mcp.json, no stdio MCP server, no OAuth broker, no multi-server orchestration. MCP in other harnesses (if any) comes from host or other plugins — not Superpowers.

Memory (010)

Skills are stateless markdown. No:

  • Vector index or embeddings
  • Session fact extraction pipeline
  • MEMORY.md or cross-session replay DB
  • Compaction-aware memory hooks

Persistence is delegated to host session storage + user artifacts (plan docs at docs/superpowers/specs/, git history). verification-before-completion skill mandates evidence artifacts, not automated recall.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 000_overview/00_sp_overview.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

skills/using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md guides isolated workspace creation via host git/worktree commands — policy, not enforcement.

Config (008)

Configuration = plugin manifests + host config after install:

  • Codex: .codex-plugin/plugin.json — skills path, UI metadata
  • Gemini: gemini-extension.json + GEMINI.md tool map
  • No layered settings merge engine, no .superpowersrc

Install paths documented per host in README (Claude marketplace, Codex plugin install, Cursor plugin, etc.).

MCP (009)

Negative finding: No .mcp.json, no stdio MCP server, no OAuth broker, no multi-server orchestration. MCP in other harnesses (if any) comes from host or other plugins — not Superpowers.

Memory (010)

Skills are stateless markdown. No:

  • Vector index or embeddings
  • Session fact extraction pipeline
  • MEMORY.md or cross-session replay DB
  • Compaction-aware memory hooks

Persistence is delegated to host session storage + user artifacts (plan docs at docs/superpowers/specs/, git history). verification-before-completion skill mandates evidence artifacts, not automated recall.

15

Overview §9

DEV

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Overview §9 — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • Superpowers has no permission engine. "Guardian" behavior is methodology gates in skill text:
  • | Gate | Mechanism | Enforced by |
  • | Pre-tool file/shell approval | Host permission modes | Host |
  • | brainstorming HARD-GATE | <HARD-GATE> blocks implementation until design approved | Skill text |
  • | TDD Iron Law | "No production code before failing test" | Skill text |
  • | systematic-debugging Iron Law | "No fixes before root cause investigation" | Skill text |
  • | verification-before-completion | Evidence checklist before "done" | Skill text |
  • | SDD two-stage review | Spec reviewer → quality reviewer | Skill + host subagents |
  • skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md — requires verifiable command output, not narration, before completion claims.
  • Contrast LazyCodex: LC has executable PostToolUse decision: block hooks; Superpowers relies on agent discipline via skills.

Topics covered

    1. Guardian / approval (012)
    1. Guardian / approval (012)

systematic-debugging Iron Law "No fixes before root cause investigation" Skill text

Contrast LazyCodex: LC has executable PostToolUse decision: block hooks; Superpowers relies on agent discipline via skills.

Overview §10

DEV

000_overview/00_sp_overview.md

Overview

Overview §10 — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • No web-search MCP, browser automation, or search CLI in Superpowers repo.
  • Research workflows in skills instruct host tools when needed:
  • systematic-debugging — search error strings via host
  • subagent-driven-development — librarian-style research delegated to host subagent tools
  • No dedicated librarian.toml — unlike LazyCodex bundled agent
  • Verdict: classify sp as host-delegated web/search — methodology may require lookup; runtime is 100% host.

Axis 01: TUI design

AXIS

001_tui-design/01_sp_tui.md

Overview

Axis 01: TUI design — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0 checkout). 독립 CLI/TUI 없음 — 모든 터미널 렌더링은 호스트 에이전트(Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI)에 위임. Superpowers는 SessionStart 훅과 SKILL.md 부트스트랩으로 호스트

  • Superpowers는 방법론을 Markdown 스킬 + SessionStart 훅으로 패키징한다. package.json에 bin 필드가 없고, Ratatui/curses/blessed 등 터미널 위젯 스택이 전혀 없다. 사용자가 보는 "TUI"는 100% 호스트 소유이며, Superpowers는 그 세션의 첫 컨텍스트 슬라이스와 스킬 카탈로그만 제공한다.
  • 유일한 "화면"에 가까운 컴포넌트는 brainstorming visual companion — 로컬 HTTP/WebSocket으로 HTML mockup을 브라우저에 띄우는 adjunct UI이며, 호스트 TUI를 대체하지 않는다.
  • flowchart TB
  • subgraph SP["162_superpowers (no TUI)"]
  • Skills["skills/*/SKILL.md"]
  • Hook["hooks/session-start"]
  • OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"]
  • Brain["brainstorm server.cjs"]
  • end
  • subgraph HostTUI["Host-owned terminal UI"]
  • CodexTUI["Codex TUI / App"]

Topics covered

  • Architecture
    1. Negative claim: no first-party terminal renderer
    1. Host routing — who renders the terminal
    1. SessionStart hook — bootstrap without terminal code
    1. OpenCode — first-user-message injection (TUI-adjacent)
    1. Skill activation vs TUI — using-superpowers
    1. Closest non-terminal UI: brainstorm visual companion
  • 6.1 Decision rule (visual-companion.md:7-25)
  • 6.2 Session lifecycle (visual-companion.md:33-48)
  • 6.3 Platform-specific process survival (visual-companion.md:50-81)
  • 6.4 Server implementation sketch (server.cjs)
    1. Cursor manifest drift (TUI-relevant)
    1. Parity matrix vs Codex (co)
    1. Security / UX notes

Source code snippets

README / docs

flowchart TB
  subgraph SP["162_superpowers (no TUI)"]
    Skills["skills/*/SKILL.md"]
    Hook["hooks/session-start"]
    OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"]
    Brain["brainstorm server.cjs"]
  end

  subgraph HostTUI["Host-owned terminal UI"]
    CodexTUI["Codex TUI / App"]
    CCTUI["Claude Code TUI"]
    CursorTUI["Cursor Agent chat"]
    OCTUI["OpenCode TUI"]
  end

  Skills --> Hook
  Hook -->|JSON context injection| CCTUI
  Hook -->|additional_context| CursorTUI
  Skills --> CodexTUI
  OC -->|first-user-message prepend| OCTUI
  Brain -.->|optional browser tab| Browser["Browser mockups"]
  HostTUI --> User["User reads/writes in host chrome"]

README / docs

// 162_superpowers/package.json:1-6
{
  "name": "superpowers",
  "version": "5.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": ".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"
}

README / docs

// 162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:23-45
"skills": "./skills/",
"interface": {
  "displayName": "Superpowers",
  "shortDescription": "Planning, TDD, debugging, and delivery workflows for coding agents",
  "capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"],
  "defaultPrompt": [
    "I've got an idea for something I'd like to build.",
    "Let's add a feature to this project."
  ],
  "composerIcon": "./assets/superpowers-small.svg",
  "logo": "./assets/app-icon.png"
}

README / docs

// 162_superpowers/hooks/hooks.json:2-14
"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{
    "type": "command",
    "command": "\"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.cmd\" session-start",
    "async": false
  }]
}]

README / docs

// 162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:111-133
// Inject bootstrap into the first user message of each session.
// Using a user message instead of a system message avoids:
//   1. Token bloat from system messages repeated every turn (#750)
//   2. Multiple system messages breaking Qwen and other models (#894)
'experimental.chat.messages.transform': async (_input, output) => {
  ...
  firstUser.parts.unshift({ ...ref, type: 'text', text: bootstrap });
}

README / docs

// 162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:28-36
**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool...
**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool...
**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool.
**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation...

README / docs

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

README / docs

scripts/start-server.sh --project-dir /path/to/project
# Returns JSON: port, url, screen_dir, state_dir

README / docs

// 162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json:21-24
"skills": "./skills/",
"agents": "./agents/",
"commands": "./commands/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks-cursor.json"

Main analysis

Superpowers (sp) — TUI / Terminal UI Design

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0 checkout). No independent CLI/TUI — All terminal rendering delegated to host agents (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI). Superpowers only injects methodology text on top of the host TUI with the SessionStart hook and SKILL.md bootstrap.

Superpowers packages its methodology as a Markdown skill + SessionStart hook. There is no bin field in package.json, and there is no terminal widget stack such as Ratatui/curses/blessed at all. The "TUI" you see is 100% host-owned, and Superpowers only provides the first context slice and skill catalog for that session.

The only component that is close to a "screen" is the brainstorming visual companion — an adjunct UI that floats an HTML mockup in the browser over a local HTTP/WebSocket, and does not replace the host TUI.

Architecture

flowchart TB
  subgraph SP["162_superpowers (no TUI)"]
    Skills["skills/*/SKILL.md"]
    Hook["hooks/session-start"]
    OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"]
    Brain["brainstorm server.cjs"]
  end

  subgraph HostTUI["Host-owned terminal UI"]
    CodexTUI["Codex TUI / App"]
    CCTUI["Claude Code TUI"]
    CursorTUI["Cursor Agent chat"]
    OCTUI["OpenCode TUI"]
  end

  Skills --> Hook
  Hook -->|JSON context injection| CCTUI
  Hook -->|additional_context| CursorTUI
  Skills --> CodexTUI
  OC -->|first-user-message prepend| OCTUI
  Brain -.->|optional browser tab| Browser["Browser mockups"]
  HostTUI --> User["User reads/writes in host chrome"]

1. Negative claim: no first-party terminal renderer

Feature Codex (co) Superpowers (sp)
Dedicated TUI crate/module codex-rs/tui — panes, status, composer None
bin CLI entry codex binary Absent — package.json only sets OpenCode main
Terminal widgets Ratatui layouts, scroll regions None
Session chrome ownership Codex renders transcript + tool cards Host renders; SP injects bootstrap text
Progress/status UI Host + update_plan / tool output Skill text only (TodoWrite → host mapping)

Evidence — no binary:

// 162_superpowers/package.json:1-6
{
  "name": "superpowers",
  "version": "5.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": ".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"
}

Superpowers distribution is a plugin/skills bundle, not an executable with its own event loop or frame buffer.

2. Host routing — who renders the terminal

Host Entry manifest Bootstrap mechanism TUI owner
Codex CLI / App .codex-plugin/plugin.json Native skill discovery via "skills": "./skills/" Codex
Claude Code .claude-plugin/ + hooks/hooks.json SessionStart → session-start Claude Code
Cursor .cursor-plugin/plugin.json + hooks/hooks-cursor.json SessionStart (Cursor hook schema) Cursor
Copilot CLI marketplace plugin + hooks additionalContext (SDK standard) Copilot CLI
OpenCode .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js experimental.chat.messages.transform OpenCode
Gemini CLI gemini-extension.json + GEMINI.md @import bootstrap + tool map Gemini CLI

Codex plugin exposes marketplace UI metadata (display name, icons, default prompts) but not terminal layout:

// 162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:23-45
"skills": "./skills/",
"interface": {
  "displayName": "Superpowers",
  "shortDescription": "Planning, TDD, debugging, and delivery workflows for coding agents",
  "capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"],
  "defaultPrompt": [
    "I've got an idea for something I'd like to build.",
    "Let's add a feature to this project."
  ],
  "composerIcon": "./assets/superpowers-small.svg",
  "logo": "./assets/app-icon.png"
}

These fields affect Codex App plugin picker / composer, not a Superpowers-owned pane.

3. SessionStart hook — bootstrap without terminal code

hooks/hooks.json registers SessionStart on startup|clear|compact:

// 162_superpowers/hooks/hooks.json:2-14
"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{
    "type": "command",
    "command": "\"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.cmd\" session-start",
    "async": false
  }]
}]

hooks/session-start (:1-57) performs:

  1. Resolve plugin root from script location (:6-8).
  2. Legacy skills warning if ~/.config/superpowers/skills still exists (:10-15) — tells agent to warn user once.
  3. Read skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md wholesale (:17-18).
  4. JSON-escape content via bash parameter substitution (:23-31) — performance fix vs char loop (issue #571).
  5. Wrap in <EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT> session fragment (:35).
  6. Platform-branch output (:46-55):
Condition JSON field Consumer
CURSOR_PLUGIN_ROOT set additional_context (snake_case) Cursor
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT && !COPILOT_CLI hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext Claude Code
else (Copilot / unknown) additionalContext (top-level) Copilot SDK standard

Claude Code reads both snake_case and nested forms without dedup — script emits only one to avoid double injection (:41-42).

TUI implication: injected text appears as host-managed context (system/additional context slot), not as a custom Superpowers panel. User still types in host composer; no SP status bar.

4. OpenCode — first-user-message injection (TUI-adjacent)

OpenCode plugin avoids system-message bloat and Qwen multi-system breakage:

// 162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:111-133
// Inject bootstrap into the first user message of each session.
// Using a user message instead of a system message avoids:
//   1. Token bloat from system messages repeated every turn (#750)
//   2. Multiple system messages breaking Qwen and other models (#894)
'experimental.chat.messages.transform': async (_input, output) => {
  ...
  firstUser.parts.unshift({ ...ref, type: 'text', text: bootstrap });
}

Design choice: bootstrap renders as if the user pasted methodology preamble — visible in OpenCode transcript as first user bubble prefix, not a separate SP sidebar.

Module-level _bootstrapCache (:49-53, :62-96) avoids re-reading SKILL.md every agent step when OpenCode reloads messages from DB.

5. Skill activation vs TUI — using-superpowers

Hosts expose native Skill tools; SP never draws UI for skill picker:

// 162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:28-36
**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool...
**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool...
**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool.
**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation...

Codex mapping (references/codex-tools.md:12):

Skill tool → Skills load natively — just follow the instructions

Parity: Codex TUI may show skill invocations as tool cards; Superpowers only supplies SKILL.md bodies — card chrome is Codex-owned.

Subagent guard in bootstrap (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:6-8):

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

Prevents nested bootstrap noise in worker TUI sessions.

6. Closest non-terminal UI: brainstorm visual companion

Explicitly not a TUI — browser adjunct for mockups while agent continues in host chat.

6.1 Decision rule (visual-companion.md:7-25)

Use browser Use terminal (host TUI)
UI mockups, wireframes Requirements / scope questions
Architecture diagrams Conceptual A/B/C in prose
Side-by-side visual compare Tradeoff lists, API design
Look-and-feel polish Clarifying questions (word answers)

"A question about UI is not automatically visual" — wizard type → terminal; wizard layout pick → browser.

6.2 Session lifecycle (visual-companion.md:33-48)

scripts/start-server.sh --project-dir /path/to/project
# Returns JSON: port, url, screen_dir, state_dir
  • Persistence: --project-dir → .superpowers/brainstorm/<session>/
  • Ephemeral: default /tmp cleaned on stop
  • User action: open URL in browser; respond in terminal (primary feedback channel)

6.3 Platform-specific process survival (visual-companion.md:50-81)

Platform Issue Mitigation
Claude Code (Windows) foreground blocks tool run_in_background: true on Bash
Codex reaps background PIDs script auto-detects CODEX_CI, foreground mode
Gemini CLI process lifetime --foreground + is_background: true on shell tool
Remote/container localhost unreachable --host 0.0.0.0 --url-host localhost

These are host exec-policy interactions, not SP TUI features.

6.4 Server implementation sketch (server.cjs)

Component Lines (approx) Role
RFC6455 WebSocket :6-72 Live reload on new HTML
getNewestScreen() :116-125 Serve latest .html by mtime
Frame wrap :107-114, :112-114 Fragments → frame-template.html
Helper inject :101-103, :137-141 helper.js before </body>
Inactivity (later in file) Auto-exit ~30 min idle

Agent loop (visual-companion.md:94-126):

  1. Write HTML to screen_dir (Write tool — never cat/heredoc)
  2. Tell user URL + summary; end turn
  3. Next turn: read state_dir/events JSONL + terminal text
  4. Push waiting.html when returning to text-only steps

TUI boundary: terminal remains host chat; browser is parallel read-only viewport except click selections logged to JSONL.

7. Cursor manifest drift (TUI-relevant)

// 162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json:21-24
"skills": "./skills/",
"agents": "./agents/",
"commands": "./commands/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks-cursor.json"

agents/ and commands/ directories are referenced but absent in corpus checkout — Cursor may hide missing paths; no SP terminal commands ship from this repo.

8. Parity matrix vs Codex (co)

Concern Codex (co) Superpowers (sp)
Session chrome codex-rs/tui panes, composer, tool cards Host TUI only
Bootstrap system prompt + config.toml using-superpowers via hook or skill path
Status/progress update_plan, exec cells Skill-mapped host tools
Secondary display App UI, web surfaces Optional brainstorm browser tab
Plugin icons Codex marketplace composerIcon, logo in .codex-plugin
Input routing TUI keybindings Host keybindings unchanged
Visual mockups N/A in core TUI brainstorming HTTP server

9. Security / UX notes

  • No cursor overlay: SP does not draw pointer or highlight in terminal.
  • Browser companion binds localhost by default (server.cjs:76-78) — remote setups need explicit --host.
  • Legacy skills path warning appears in first agent reply after SessionStart — UX nudge, not TUI modal.
  • Gitignore reminder: .superpowers/ should be ignored when using project-dir persistence.

10. Evidence quality bar

  • Negative claim: no bin, no terminal widget code
  • SessionStart platform branching with line refs
  • OpenCode first-message injection rationale (#750, #894)
  • Visual companion vs terminal decision table
  • server.cjs serving model (newest HTML + WS reload)
  • Parity table vs Codex TUI

11. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
162_superpowers/package.json 1-6 No CLI binary
162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 23-45 Skills path + App UI metadata
162_superpowers/hooks/hooks.json 2-14 SessionStart registration
162_superpowers/hooks/session-start 1-57 Bootstrap JSON emission
162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js 49-133 Cached bootstrap + message transform
162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md 6-36 Skill tool + subagent stop
162_superpowers/skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md 1-284 Browser vs terminal routing
162_superpowers/skills/brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs 1-150 HTTP/WS server core
162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json 21-24 Cursor surface (manifest drift)

Related corpus docs

  • Codex TUI depth: 001_tui-design/01_co_tui.md
  • Prompt injection axis: 002_prompt-context/02_sp_prompt.md
  • Visual companion server surface: 006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_sp_surface.md
  • Overview: 000_overview/00_sp_overview.md
  • Harmonization: 000_overview/00_corpus_harmonization_status.md

Change history

date change
2026-06-27 WP52: ≥12 KB expansion — SessionStart branching, visual companion, OpenCode transform
2026-06-27 WP38: source citations + corpus links
2026-03-26 Initial sp TUI axis stub

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot (162_superpowers v5.1.0) Current
Independent TUI Still absent Host TUI evolution external
Cursor commands/ Manifest references missing dir Verify on Cursor plugin install
Codex hooks Removed per upstream (remove-codex-hooks branch history) Codex uses native skill path only
Brainstorm server server.cjs + platform launch notes Re-verify Codex PID reaping behavior on upgrade

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 001_tui-design/01_sp_tui.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers (sp) — TUI / Terminal UI Design

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0 checkout). 독립 CLI/TUI 없음 — 모든 터미널 렌더링은 호스트 에이전트(Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI)에 위임. Superpowers는 SessionStart 훅과 SKILL.md 부트스트랩으로 호스트 TUI 위에 방법론 텍스트만 주입한다.

Superpowers는 방법론을 Markdown 스킬 + SessionStart 훅으로 패키징한다. package.json에 bin 필드가 없고, Ratatui/curses/blessed 등 터미널 위젯 스택이 전혀 없다. 사용자가 보는 "TUI"는 100% 호스트 소유이며, Superpowers는 그 세션의 첫 컨텍스트 슬라이스와 스킬 카탈로그만 제공한다.

유일한 "화면"에 가까운 컴포넌트는 brainstorming visual companion — 로컬 HTTP/WebSocket으로 HTML mockup을 브라우저에 띄우는 adjunct UI이며, 호스트 TUI를 대체하지 않는다.

Architecture

flowchart TB
  subgraph SP["162_superpowers (no TUI)"]
    Skills["skills/*/SKILL.md"]
    Hook["hooks/session-start"]
    OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"]
    Brain["brainstorm server.cjs"]
  end

  subgraph HostTUI["Host-owned terminal UI"]
    CodexTUI["Codex TUI / App"]
    CCTUI["Claude Code TUI"]
    CursorTUI["Cursor Agent chat"]
    OCTUI["OpenCode TUI"]
  end

  Skills --> Hook
  Hook -->|JSON context injection| CCTUI
  Hook -->|additional_context| CursorTUI
  Skills --> CodexTUI
  OC -->|first-user-message prepend| OCTUI
  Brain -.->|optional browser tab| Browser["Browser mockups"]
  HostTUI --> User["User reads/writes in host chrome"]

1. Negative claim: no first-party terminal renderer

Feature Codex (co) Superpowers (sp)
Dedicated TUI crate/module codex-rs/tui — panes, status, composer None
bin CLI entry codex binary Absent — package.json only sets OpenCode main
Terminal widgets Ratatui layouts, scroll regions None
Session chrome ownership Codex renders transcript + tool cards Host renders; SP injects bootstrap text
Progress/status UI Host + update_plan / tool output Skill text only (TodoWrite → host mapping)

Evidence — no binary:

// 162_superpowers/package.json:1-6
{
  "name": "superpowers",
  "version": "5.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": ".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"
}

Superpowers distribution is a plugin/skills bundle, not an executable with its own event loop or frame buffer.

2. Host routing — who renders the terminal

Host Entry manifest Bootstrap mechanism TUI owner
Codex CLI / App .codex-plugin/plugin.json Native skill discovery via "skills": "./skills/" Codex
Claude Code .claude-plugin/ + hooks/hooks.json SessionStart → session-start Claude Code
Cursor .cursor-plugin/plugin.json + hooks/hooks-cursor.json SessionStart (Cursor hook schema) Cursor
Copilot CLI marketplace plugin + hooks additionalContext (SDK standard) Copilot CLI
OpenCode .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js experimental.chat.messages.transform OpenCode
Gemini CLI gemini-extension.json + GEMINI.md @import bootstrap + tool map Gemini CLI

Codex plugin exposes marketplace UI metadata (display name, icons, default prompts) but not terminal layout:

// 162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:23-45
"skills": "./skills/",
"interface": {
  "displayName": "Superpowers",
  "shortDescription": "Planning, TDD, debugging, and delivery workflows for coding agents",
  "capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"],
  "defaultPrompt": [
    "I've got an idea for something I'd like to build.",
    "Let's add a feature to this project."
  ],
  "composerIcon": "./assets/superpowers-small.svg",
  "logo": "./assets/app-icon.png"
}

These fields affect Codex App plugin picker / composer, not a Superpowers-owned pane.

3. SessionStart hook — bootstrap without terminal code

hooks/hooks.json registers SessionStart on startup|clear|compact:

// 162_superpowers/hooks/hooks.json:2-14
"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{
    "type": "command",
    "command": "\"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.cmd\" session-start",
    "async": false
  }]
}]

hooks/session-start (:1-57) performs:

  1. Resolve plugin root from script location (:6-8).
  2. Legacy skills warning if ~/.config/superpowers/skills still exists (:10-15) — tells agent to warn user once.
  3. Read skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md wholesale (:17-18).
  4. JSON-escape content via bash parameter substitution (:23-31) — performance fix vs char loop (issue #571).
  5. Wrap in <EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT> session fragment (:35).
  6. Platform-branch output (:46-55):
Condition JSON field Consumer
CURSOR_PLUGIN_ROOT set additional_context (snake_case) Cursor
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT && !COPILOT_CLI hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext Claude Code
else (Copilot / unknown) additionalContext (top-level) Copilot SDK standard

Claude Code reads both snake_case and nested forms without dedup — script emits only one to avoid double injection (:41-42).

TUI implication: injected text appears as host-managed context (system/additional context slot), not as a custom Superpowers panel. User still types in host composer; no SP status bar.

4. OpenCode — first-user-message injection (TUI-adjacent)

OpenCode plugin avoids system-message bloat and Qwen multi-system breakage:

// 162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:111-133
// Inject bootstrap into the first user message of each session.
// Using a user message instead of a system message avoids:
//   1. Token bloat from system messages repeated every turn (#750)
//   2. Multiple system messages breaking Qwen and other models (#894)
'experimental.chat.messages.transform': async (_input, output) => {
  ...
  firstUser.parts.unshift({ ...ref, type: 'text', text: bootstrap });
}

Design choice: bootstrap renders as if the user pasted methodology preamble — visible in OpenCode transcript as first user bubble prefix, not a separate SP sidebar.

Module-level _bootstrapCache (:49-53, :62-96) avoids re-reading SKILL.md every agent step when OpenCode reloads messages from DB.

5. Skill activation vs TUI — using-superpowers

Hosts expose native Skill tools; SP never draws UI for skill picker:

// 162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:28-36
**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool...
**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool...
**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool.
**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation...

Skill tool → Skills load natively — just follow the instructions

Parity: Codex TUI may show skill invocations as tool cards; Superpowers only supplies SKILL.md bodies — card chrome is Codex-owned.

If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

Prevents nested bootstrap noise in worker TUI sessions.

  4. Push waiting.html when returning to text-only steps

18

Axis 02: Prompt & context

AXIS

002_prompt-context/02_sp_prompt.md

Overview

Axis 02: Prompt & context — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is a prompt/context overlay, not a prompt runtime. It does not construct provider API payloads, compaction transcripts, token budgets, or repository conte

  • The core prompt-engineering product is using-superpowers — a meta-skill that mandates skill invocation before action — plus workflow skills that embed subagent prompt templates and context curation rules. Hosts differ in *wher
  • flowchart TD
  • subgraph Inject["Bootstrap injection"]
  • SS["hooks/session-start"]
  • OC["OpenCode messages.transform"]
  • GEM["GEMINI.md @imports"]
  • Codex[".codex-plugin skills path"]
  • end
  • US["using-superpowers/SKILL.md"] --> SS
  • US --> OC
  • US --> GEM

Topics covered

  • Architecture
    1. What Superpowers does NOT own (negative claim)
    1. Instruction priority stack
    1. Bootstrap skill: using-superpowers
  • 3.1 Mandatory invocation rule (:10-16, :44-46)
  • 3.2 Skill flow graph (:48-75)
  • 3.3 Red flags table (:78-95)
  • 3.4 Skill priority when multiple match (:97-105)
  • 3.5 Rigid vs flexible skills (:107-113)
  • 3.6 Subagent exemption (:6-8)
    1. SessionStart hook — Claude/Cursor/Copilot path
    1. OpenCode — first user message prepend
    1. Gemini — context file imports
    1. Codex — native skill path (no SessionStart hook in tree)

Source code snippets

README / docs

flowchart TD
  subgraph Inject["Bootstrap injection"]
    SS["hooks/session-start"]
    OC["OpenCode messages.transform"]
    GEM["GEMINI.md @imports"]
    Codex[".codex-plugin skills path"]
  end

  US["using-superpowers/SKILL.md"] --> SS
  US --> OC
  US --> GEM
  US --> Codex

  SS --> HostCtx["Host context slot"]
  OC --> FirstUser["First user message prefix"]
  GEM --> GeminiCtx["Gemini context file merge"]
  Codex --> NativeSkill["Native skill discovery"]

  HostCtx --> Rule["Mandatory skill-before-action"]
  FirstUser --> Rule
  GeminiCtx --> Rule
  NativeSkill --> Rule

  Rule --> WF["Workflow skills"]
  WF --> Sub["Subagent prompt templates"]
  WF --> Plan["Plan/design doc paths"]

README / docs

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply...
YOU MUST USE IT.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

README / docs

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent... skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

README / docs

using_superpowers_content=$(cat "${PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md" ...)
session_context="<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>\nYou have superpowers.\n\n**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill...**\n\n${using_superpowers_escaped}\n..."

README / docs

_bootstrapCache = `<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.
**IMPORTANT: ... Do NOT use the skill tool to load "using-superpowers" again ...**
${content}  // body after frontmatter strip
${toolMapping}
</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>`;

README / docs

config.skills.paths.push(superpowersSkillsDir);

README / docs

@./skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md
@./skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md

README / docs

"skills": "./skills/"

README / docs

# codex-tools.md:20-23
[features]
multi_agent = true

README / docs

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code... until design approved.
</HARD-GATE>

Main analysis

Superpowers — Prompt / Context Surface

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is a prompt/context overlay, not a prompt runtime. It does not construct provider API payloads, compaction transcripts, token budgets, or repository context maps. It injects or registers bootstrap skill text, then relies on the host to load skills/*/SKILL.md bodies on demand.

The core prompt-engineering product is using-superpowers — a meta-skill that mandates skill invocation before action — plus workflow skills that embed subagent prompt templates and context curation rules. Hosts differ in where bootstrap lands (system hook vs first user message vs @import file).

Architecture

flowchart TD
  subgraph Inject["Bootstrap injection"]
    SS["hooks/session-start"]
    OC["OpenCode messages.transform"]
    GEM["GEMINI.md @imports"]
    Codex[".codex-plugin skills path"]
  end

  US["using-superpowers/SKILL.md"] --> SS
  US --> OC
  US --> GEM
  US --> Codex

  SS --> HostCtx["Host context slot"]
  OC --> FirstUser["First user message prefix"]
  GEM --> GeminiCtx["Gemini context file merge"]
  Codex --> NativeSkill["Native skill discovery"]

  HostCtx --> Rule["Mandatory skill-before-action"]
  FirstUser --> Rule
  GeminiCtx --> Rule
  NativeSkill --> Rule

  Rule --> WF["Workflow skills"]
  WF --> Sub["Subagent prompt templates"]
  WF --> Plan["Plan/design doc paths"]

1. What Superpowers does NOT own (negative claim)

Capability Codex / Aider / host Superpowers
System prompt assembly Host runtime None
Compaction / summarization Host None
Token budgeting Host + model None
Repo map / embeddings Host or Aider RepoMap None
Rules / AGENTS.md merge order Host Defers — user files beat skills
Provider message JSON Host API layer None

Superpowers shapes behavior through skill text once the host has loaded it. It is methodology injection, not a context window algorithm.

2. Instruction priority stack

using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26 defines a three-tier precedence:

  1. User explicit instructions — CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct chat requests (highest)
  2. Superpowers skills — override default system behavior on conflict
  3. Default system prompt — lowest

Example: user says "don't use TDD" → follow user even if test-driven-development skill says always TDD.

This is prompt conflict resolution policy embedded in bootstrap, not enforced by code.

3. Bootstrap skill: using-superpowers

3.1 Mandatory invocation rule (:10-16, :44-46)

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply...
YOU MUST USE IT.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

Invoke BEFORE any response or action — including clarifying questions (:85). Skill check precedes exploration.

3.2 Skill flow graph (:48-75)

DOT digraph encodes:

  • User message → "Might any skill apply?" → invoke Skill tool (even 1%)
  • Announce: "Using [skill] to [purpose]"
  • Checklist skills → TodoWrite per item (host-mapped)
  • EnterPlanMode path → brainstorming if not already brainstormed

3.3 Red flags table (:78-95)

Rationalization stop-list: "just a simple question", "need context first", "I'll explore codebase first" — all rejected; skills define how to explore.

3.4 Skill priority when multiple match (:97-105)

  1. Process skills first (brainstorming, debugging)
  2. Implementation skills second (domain-specific)

"Let's build X" → brainstorming before frontend skills.

3.5 Rigid vs flexible skills (:107-113)

TDD/debugging = rigid; pattern skills = adaptable. Declared inside each skill.

3.6 Subagent exemption (:6-8)

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent... skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

Workers should not re-load bootstrap — controller curates their prompt.

4. SessionStart hook — Claude/Cursor/Copilot path

hooks/session-start:17-35:

using_superpowers_content=$(cat "${PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md" ...)
session_context="<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>\nYou have superpowers.\n\n**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill...**\n\n${using_superpowers_escaped}\n..."

Full SKILL.md body (including frontmatter) embedded in session context — not a summary.

Platform output (:46-55):

Platform JSON shape
Cursor { "additional_context": "..." }
Claude Code { "hookSpecificOutput": { "hookEventName": "SessionStart", "additionalContext": "..." } }
Copilot / fallback { "additionalContext": "..." }

Triggered on startup|clear|compact (hooks/hooks.json:5) — re-injects after compaction clears context.

Legacy warning appended if ~/.config/superpowers/skills exists (session-start:12-15).

5. OpenCode — first user message prepend

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:85-93 builds bootstrap:

_bootstrapCache = `<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.
**IMPORTANT: ... Do NOT use the skill tool to load "using-superpowers" again ...**
${content}  // body after frontmatter strip
${toolMapping}
</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>`;

Frontmatter stripped via regex (:16-34) — injection is body-only plus OpenCode tool mapping (:76-83).

Why user message not system (:111-114):

  • Avoid system token repeat every turn (#750)
  • Avoid multiple system messages breaking Qwen (#894)

Guard against double injection (:126-129): skip if EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT already in first user parts.

Config hook registers skills path (:103-108):

config.skills.paths.push(superpowersSkillsDir);

6. Gemini — context file imports

GEMINI.md entire content:

@./skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md
@./skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md

Gemini CLI merges imported files into session context and maps Claude tool names → Gemini tools at load time. Subagent dispatch via @generalist documented in gemini-tools.md:19-39.

7. Codex — native skill path (no SessionStart hook in tree)

.codex-plugin/plugin.json:23:

"skills": "./skills/"

Codex discovers skills from plugin directory; bootstrap skill loaded via native skill mechanism. Tool mapping in references/codex-tools.md — Skill → follow instructions natively; subagents need:

# codex-tools.md:20-23
[features]
multi_agent = true

8. Workflow-level prompt engineering

8.1 Brainstorming — design-before-code gate

brainstorming/SKILL.md:12-14:

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code... until design approved.
</HARD-GATE>

Checklist (:22-32): context → visual companion offer → questions → approaches → design sections → spec doc → writing-plans.

Design docs path: docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md.

8.2 Writing plans — bite-sized tasks

Plans break work into 2–5 minute tasks with exact paths, complete code snippets, verification steps (README workflow step 3).

8.3 Subagent context curation (strongest SP prompt design)

subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:10-14:

Subagents should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need.

Controller duties:

  • Read plan once; extract full task text upfront
  • Provide scene-setting context per task
  • Never make subagent read plan file (:243)
  • Answer questions before implementation (:252-255)

Prompt templates:

File Role
implementer-prompt.md Task + context + self-review + escalation statuses
spec-reviewer-prompt.md Spec compliance only
code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md Structure/tests/maintainability

Implementer template (implementer-prompt.md:11-14):

[FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]

Status protocol (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:104-120):

  • DONE, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, NEEDS_CONTEXT, BLOCKED — controller must not ignore escalations.

8.4 Parallel investigation context

dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md:86-110 — agent prompts must be:

  1. Focused (one domain)
  2. Self-contained (errors pasted inline)
  3. Specific output (summary of root cause + fix)

No inherited session history.

9. Platform tool-name adaptation in prompts

Skills authored with Claude Code tool names. Reference files rewrite for hosts:

File Mappings
references/codex-tools.md Task→spawn_agent, TodoWrite→update_plan, Skill→native
references/gemini-tools.md Read→read_file, Skill→activate_skill, Task→@generalist
references/copilot-tools.md Read→view, Task→task, TodoWrite→sql todos table

OpenCode embeds mapping inline in bootstrap (superpowers.js:76-83).

Prompt implication: same skill body ships everywhere; adaptation layer prevents wrong tool invocation in prompt instructions.

10. Continuous execution prompt policy

subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:14:

Do not pause to check in with your human partner between tasks... "Should I continue?" prompts waste their time.

This is explicit prompt discipline for controller agents — reduces context churn in host transcript.

11. Parity matrix vs Codex (co)

Concern Codex (co) Superpowers (sp)
System prompt Rust-assembled, config-driven Host-owned; SP adds skills
Bootstrap AGENTS.md, instructions.md using-superpowers mandatory skill
Context ranking @files, search, embeddings None — skill text + user files
Compaction Host algorithm Re-bootstrap on SessionStart compact
Subagent prompts spawn_agent templates SKILL.md + markdown prompt files
Methodology packs User skills dir First-party skills/ library

12. Evidence quality bar

  • Negative claims: no compaction/token/repo-map
  • Instruction priority stack with line refs
  • All four host bootstrap paths documented
  • Subagent context curation rules + templates
  • OpenCode injection rationale with issue refs
  • Tool mapping reference files indexed

13. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md 6-117 Bootstrap rules, flow, red flags
162_superpowers/hooks/session-start 17-55 Full skill embed + JSON branches
162_superpowers/hooks/hooks.json 2-14 SessionStart triggers
162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js 16-133 Frontmatter strip, cache, transform
162_superpowers/GEMINI.md 1-2 @import bootstrap
162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 23 Skills directory
162_superpowers/skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md 12-32 HARD-GATE, checklist
162_superpowers/skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md 10-120, 236-250 Context curation, red flags
162_superpowers/skills/subagent-driven-development/implementer-prompt.md 1-80 Template structure
162_superpowers/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md 86-110 Parallel prompt shape
162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/references/codex-tools.md 1-60 Codex adaptation
162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md 1-51 Gemini adaptation

Korean corpus (reference)

Superpowers — Prompt / Context Surface

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is a prompt/context overlay, not a prompt runtime. It does not construct provider API payloads, compaction transcripts, token budgets, or repository context maps. It injects or registers bootstrap skill text, then relies on the host to load skills/*/SKILL.md bodies on demand.

The core prompt-engineering product is using-superpowers — a meta-skill that mandates skill invocation before action — plus workflow skills that embed subagent prompt templates and context curation rules. Hosts differ in where bootstrap lands (system hook vs first user message vs @import file).

Architecture

flowchart TD
  subgraph Inject["Bootstrap injection"]
    SS["hooks/session-start"]
    OC["OpenCode messages.transform"]
    GEM["GEMINI.md @imports"]
    Codex[".codex-plugin skills path"]
  end

  US["using-superpowers/SKILL.md"] --> SS
  US --> OC
  US --> GEM
  US --> Codex

  SS --> HostCtx["Host context slot"]
  OC --> FirstUser["First user message prefix"]
  GEM --> GeminiCtx["Gemini context file merge"]
  Codex --> NativeSkill["Native skill discovery"]

  HostCtx --> Rule["Mandatory skill-before-action"]
  FirstUser --> Rule
  GeminiCtx --> Rule
  NativeSkill --> Rule

  Rule --> WF["Workflow skills"]
  WF --> Sub["Subagent prompt templates"]
  WF --> Plan["Plan/design doc paths"]

1. What Superpowers does NOT own (negative claim)

Capability Codex / Aider / host Superpowers
System prompt assembly Host runtime None
Compaction / summarization Host None
Token budgeting Host + model None
Repo map / embeddings Host or Aider RepoMap None
Rules / AGENTS.md merge order Host Defers — user files beat skills
Provider message JSON Host API layer None

Superpowers shapes behavior through skill text once the host has loaded it. It is methodology injection, not a context window algorithm.

2. Instruction priority stack

using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26 defines a three-tier precedence:

  1. User explicit instructions — CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct chat requests (highest)
  2. Superpowers skills — override default system behavior on conflict
  3. Default system prompt — lowest

Example: user says "don't use TDD" → follow user even if test-driven-development skill says always TDD.

This is prompt conflict resolution policy embedded in bootstrap, not enforced by code.

3. Bootstrap skill: using-superpowers

3.1 Mandatory invocation rule (:10-16, :44-46)

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply...
YOU MUST USE IT.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

Invoke BEFORE any response or action — including clarifying questions (:85). Skill check precedes exploration.

3.2 Skill flow graph (:48-75)

DOT digraph encodes:

  • User message → "Might any skill apply?" → invoke Skill tool (even 1%)
  • Announce: "Using [skill] to [purpose]"
  • Checklist skills → TodoWrite per item (host-mapped)
  • EnterPlanMode path → brainstorming if not already brainstormed

3.3 Red flags table (:78-95)

Rationalization stop-list: "just a simple question", "need context first", "I'll explore codebase first" — all rejected; skills define how to explore.

3.4 Skill priority when multiple match (:97-105)

  1. Process skills first (brainstorming, debugging)
  2. Implementation skills second (domain-specific)

"Let's build X" → brainstorming before frontend skills.

3.5 Rigid vs flexible skills (:107-113)

TDD/debugging = rigid; pattern skills = adaptable. Declared inside each skill.

3.6 Subagent exemption (:6-8)

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent... skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

Workers should not re-load bootstrap — controller curates their prompt.

4. SessionStart hook — Claude/Cursor/Copilot path

hooks/session-start:17-35:

using_superpowers_content=$(cat "${PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md" ...)
session_context="<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>\nYou have superpowers.\n\n**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill...**\n\n${using_superpowers_escaped}\n..."

Full SKILL.md body (including frontmatter) embedded in session context — not a summary.

Platform JSON shape

Claude Code { "hookSpecificOutput": { "hookEventName": "SessionStart", "additionalContext": "..." } }
Copilot / fallback { "additionalContext": "..." }

Triggered on startup|clear|compact (hooks/hooks.json:5) — re-injects after compaction clears context.

5. OpenCode — first user message prepend

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:85-93 builds bootstrap:

_bootstrapCache = `<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.
**IMPORTANT: ... Do NOT use the skill tool to load "using-superpowers" again ...**
${content}  // body after frontmatter strip
${toolMapping}
</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>`;

Frontmatter stripped via regex (:16-34) — injection is body-only plus OpenCode tool mapping (:76-83).

Why user message not system (:111-114):

  • Avoid system token repeat every turn (#750)
  • Avoid multiple system messages breaking Qwen (#894)

Guard against double injection (:126-129): skip if EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT already in first user parts.

Config hook registers skills path (:103-108):

config.skills.paths.push(superpowersSkillsDir);

6. Gemini — context file imports

GEMINI.md entire content:

@./skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md
@./skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md

Gemini CLI merges imported files into session context and maps Claude tool names → Gemini tools at load time. Subagent dispatch via @generalist documented in gemini-tools.md:19-39.

7. Codex — native skill path (no SessionStart hook in tree)

.codex-plugin/plugin.json:23:

"skills": "./skills/"

Codex discovers skills from plugin directory; bootstrap skill loaded via native skill mechanism. Tool mapping in references/codex-tools.md — Skill → follow instructions natively; subagents need:

# codex-tools.md:20-23

8. Workflow-level prompt engineering

8.1 Brainstorming — design-before-code gate

brainstorming/SKILL.md:12-14:

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code... until design approved.
</HARD-GATE>

Checklist (:22-32): context → visual companion offer → questions → approaches → design sections → spec doc → writing-plans.

Design docs path: docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md.

8.2 Writing plans — bite-sized tasks

Plans break work into 2–5 minute tasks with exact paths, complete code snippets, verification steps (README workflow step 3).

8.3 Subagent context curation (strongest SP prompt design)

subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:10-14:

Subagents should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need.

Controller duties:

  • Read plan once; extract full task text upfront
  • Provide scene-setting context per task
  • Never make subagent read plan file (:243)
  • Answer questions before implementation (:252-255)

Prompt templates:

File Role
implementer-prompt.md Task + context + self-review + escalation statuses
spec-reviewer-prompt.md Spec compliance only
code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md Structure/tests/maintainability

Implementer template (implementer-prompt.md:11-14):

[FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]

Status protocol (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:104-120):

  • DONE, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, NEEDS_CONTEXT, BLOCKED — controller must not ignore escalations.

8.4 Parallel investigation context

dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md:86-110 — agent prompts must be:

  1. Focused (one domain)
  2. Self-contained (errors pasted inline)
  3. Specific output (summary of root cause + fix)

No inherited session history.

9. Platform tool-name adaptation in prompts

Skills authored with Claude Code tool names. Reference files rewrite for hosts:

File Mappings
references/codex-tools.md Task→spawn_agent, TodoWrite→update_plan, Skill→native
references/gemini-tools.md Read→read_file, Skill→activate_skill, Task→@generalist
references/copilot-tools.md Read→view, Task→task, TodoWrite→sql todos table

OpenCode embeds mapping inline in bootstrap (superpowers.js:76-83).

Prompt implication: same skill body ships everywhere; adaptation layer prevents wrong tool invocation in prompt instructions.

10. Continuous execution prompt policy

subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:14:

Do not pause to check in with your human partner between tasks... "Should I continue?" prompts waste their time.

This is explicit prompt discipline for controller agents — reduces context churn in host transcript.
