Axis 03: Tool runtime

AXIS

003_tool-runtime/03_sp_tool.md

Overview

Axis 03: Tool runtime — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is not an independent tool runtime. It does not own shell/file/search/MCP dispatch, approval gates, or structured function-call loops. Its runtime surface

  • flowchart LR
  • subgraph SP["Superpowers owns"]
  • Skills["skills/*/SKILL.md"]
  • Bootstrap["using-superpowers"]
  • Maps["references/*-tools.md"]
  • OCMap["OpenCode inline mapping"]
  • end
  • subgraph Host["Host owns"]
  • Loop["LLM tool loop"]
  • Catalog["Tool registry"]
  • Dispatch["Tool dispatcher"]

Topics covered

  • Architecture
    1. Classification — ownership table
    1. Skills as tool-usage curriculum
    1. Cross-host tool mapping reference
  • 3.1 Codex (references/codex-tools.md)
  • 3.2 Gemini (references/gemini-tools.md)
  • 3.3 Copilot (references/copilot-tools.md)
  • 3.4 OpenCode (inline, superpowers.js:76-83)
    1. Harness runtime paths (activation, not dispatch)
    1. Subagent tools — host-specific dispatch
    1. Brainstorm server — auxiliary process, not agent tools
    1. Tests as behavioral specification
    1. sync-to-codex-plugin script
    1. Parity matrix vs Codex (co)

Source code snippets

README / docs

flowchart LR
  subgraph SP["Superpowers owns"]
    Skills["skills/*/SKILL.md"]
    Bootstrap["using-superpowers"]
    Maps["references/*-tools.md"]
    OCMap["OpenCode inline mapping"]
  end

  subgraph Host["Host owns"]
    Loop["LLM tool loop"]
    Catalog["Tool registry"]
    Dispatch["Tool dispatcher"]
    Approval["Exec approval / sandbox"]
  end

  Skills --> Bootstrap
  Bootstrap --> Agent["Agent reads skill"]
  Agent --> Maps
  Maps --> HostTools["Host-native tool calls"]
  OCMap --> HostTools
  HostTools --> Dispatch
  Dispatch --> Loop

README / docs

[features]
multi_agent = true

README / docs

GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)

README / docs

`TodoWrite` → `todowrite`
`Task` → OpenCode subagent @mention
`Skill` → native `skill` tool

README / docs

Task("Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts failures")
// concurrent

Main analysis

Superpowers — Tool Runtime

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is not an independent tool runtime. It does not own shell/file/search/MCP dispatch, approval gates, or structured function-call loops. Its runtime surface is bootstrap policy + skill catalog + cross-host tool-name adaptation that teaches the active harness how to interpret Claude Code-oriented skill text.

Architecture

flowchart LR
  subgraph SP["Superpowers owns"]
    Skills["skills/*/SKILL.md"]
    Bootstrap["using-superpowers"]
    Maps["references/*-tools.md"]
    OCMap["OpenCode inline mapping"]
  end

  subgraph Host["Host owns"]
    Loop["LLM tool loop"]
    Catalog["Tool registry"]
    Dispatch["Tool dispatcher"]
    Approval["Exec approval / sandbox"]
  end

  Skills --> Bootstrap
  Bootstrap --> Agent["Agent reads skill"]
  Agent --> Maps
  Maps --> HostTools["Host-native tool calls"]
  OCMap --> HostTools
  HostTools --> Dispatch
  Dispatch --> Loop

1. Classification — ownership table

Layer Superpowers owns? Implementation
LLM request/response loop No Host harness
Tool catalog definition No Host (Read, Bash, spawn_agent, etc.)
Tool dispatch / routing No Host dispatcher
Tool result parsing No Host
MCP server bridge No Host or user config
Skill catalog (SKILL.md) Yes skills/ tree
Bootstrap invocation policy Yes using-superpowers
Tool-name adaptation docs Yes references/*.md, OpenCode JS
Behavioral "use TodoWrite" rules Yes In skill bodies (host executes)

Negative claim: Superpowers is not a structured function-call dispatcher, JSON schema registry, or plugin tool loader beyond declaring "skills": "./skills/" in host manifests.

2. Skills as tool-usage curriculum

using-superpowers/SKILL.md:30-36 — access patterns:

Host Skill invocation tool
Claude Code Skill
Copilot CLI skill
Gemini CLI activate_skill
Codex Native skill load — "just follow the instructions" (codex-tools.md:12)

Critical rule (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:30):

Never use the Read tool on skill files [in Claude Code].

Skill tool is the host's dynamic loader; Read bypasses activation metadata.

Flow when checklist present (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:57-74):

  1. Invoke skill
  2. Announce usage
  3. If checklist → create todo per item via host task tracker
  4. Follow skill exactly

3. Cross-host tool mapping reference

Skills written assuming Claude Code names. Adaptation files:

3.1 Codex (references/codex-tools.md)

Skill reference Codex equivalent
Task (subagent) spawn_agent (+ multi_agent feature)
Multiple Task Multiple spawn_agent
Task result wait_agent
Task complete close_agent
TodoWrite update_plan
Skill Native skills — follow text
Read/Write/Edit Native file tools
Bash Native shell tools

Multi-agent gate (codex-tools.md:18-25):

[features]
multi_agent = true

Required for dispatching-parallel-agents and subagent-driven-development.

Legacy note (codex-tools.md:27-30): pre-rust-v0.115.0 used wait; now wait is exec-cell resume — not subagent wait.

Environment detection (codex-tools.md:34-47): read-only git commands before worktree/branch skills:

GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)

Signals: linked worktree vs detached HEAD (sandbox).

3.2 Gemini (references/gemini-tools.md)

Skill reference Gemini equivalent
Read read_file
Write write_file
Edit replace
Bash run_shell_command
Grep grep_search
Glob glob
TodoWrite write_todos
Skill activate_skill
Task @agent-name (usually @generalist)

Parallel dispatch (gemini-tools.md:37-39): request multiple @generalist tasks in same prompt when independent.

Gemini-only extras: save_memory, ask_user, tracker_create_task, plan mode tools.

3.3 Copilot (references/copilot-tools.md)

Skill reference Copilot equivalent
Read view
Write create
Edit edit
Bash bash
Skill skill
Task task with agent_type
TodoWrite sql on built-in todos table
Task status read_agent, list_agents

Async shell sessions (copilot-tools.md:22-32): bash with async: true, write_bash, read_bash — no Claude equivalent; skills silent, mapping doc fills gap.

3.4 OpenCode (inline, superpowers.js:76-83)

`TodoWrite` → `todowrite`
`Task` → OpenCode subagent @mention
`Skill` → native `skill` tool

Embedded in bootstrap cache alongside stripped using-superpowers body.

4. Harness runtime paths (activation, not dispatch)

Host Mechanism File
Codex Plugin skills directory .codex-plugin/plugin.json:23
Claude/Cursor/Copilot SessionStart hook hooks/session-start
Gemini Extension + GEMINI.md GEMINI.md
OpenCode JS plugin config + transform .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js

OpenCode config hook (superpowers.js:103-108) mutates live config.skills.paths — skills discovered lazily without symlinks.

Not tool registration: paths tell OpenCode where SKILL.md files live; individual tools still come from OpenCode core.

5. Subagent tools — host-specific dispatch

Documented in skills, executed by host:

Sequential SDD (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:50-61):

  • Dispatch implementer → spec reviewer → code quality reviewer per task
  • Never parallel implementers (:242) — file conflict risk

Parallel debug (dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md:66-74):

Task("Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts failures")
// concurrent

Codex translation: parallel spawn_agent calls after multi_agent = true.

6. Brainstorm server — auxiliary process, not agent tools

skills/brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs is a local HTTP/WebSocket server for HTML mockups. Invoked via Bash in skill instructions — not registered as host MCP tool by Superpowers.

Agent uses Write tool → files on disk → server serves newest HTML. Tool chain: host Write + host Bash, orchestrated by skill prose.

Platform notes (visual-companion.md:67-72): Codex reaps background processes — script detects CODEX_CI for foreground mode.

7. Tests as behavioral specification

Test dir What it verifies
tests/skill-triggering/ Prompt fixtures trigger expected skills
tests/explicit-skill-requests/ Explicit skill invocation paths
tests/opencode/test-bootstrap-caching.mjs Bootstrap cache behavior
tests/claude-code/run-skill-tests.sh Claude harness integration
tests/codex-plugin-sync/ Plugin sync script

These validate skill loading/triggering, not a Superpowers-owned dispatcher.

Example skill-triggering prompts (tests/skill-triggering/prompts/):

  • test-driven-development.txt
  • systematic-debugging.txt
  • dispatching-parallel-agents.txt

8. sync-to-codex-plugin script

scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh copies skills/assets into Codex plugin layout for marketplace publish — packaging, not runtime.

9. Parity matrix vs Codex (co)

Concern Codex (co) Superpowers (sp)
Tool registry Rust codex-core tools Host-only
Shell execution shell, sandbox policy Skill prose → host Bash
Subagents spawn_agent, wait_agent Documented in codex-tools.md
Plan tracking update_plan Skill maps TodoWrite → update_plan
Skill tool Native load SKILL.md Provides SKILL.md files
MCP User/server config Not bundled
Approval prompts Codex exec policy External

10. Boundary statements

  • Do not describe Superpowers as executing commands — it instructs the host agent to execute.
  • Tool mappings are documentation, not runtime shims (except OpenCode bootstrap text).
  • Missing host feature (e.g. no multi_agent) → skill workflows cannot dispatch subagents; precondition failure is host-side.
  • EnterPlanMode / ExitPlanMode have no Copilot equivalent (copilot-tools.md:20) — skills assuming plan mode need controller judgment on Copilot.

11. Evidence quality bar

  • Negative claim: no owned tool loop
  • Full mapping tables for Codex/Gemini/Copilot/OpenCode
  • multi_agent config documented
  • Subagent sequential vs parallel tool patterns
  • Test dirs indexed
  • Brainstorm server clarified as Bash-orchestrated adjunct

12. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md 28-74 Skill tool + TodoWrite flow
162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/references/codex-tools.md 1-60 Codex mapping + git detect
162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md 1-51 Gemini mapping
162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/references/copilot-tools.md 1-43 Copilot mapping
162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js 76-108 OpenCode mapping + skills path
162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 23 Skills declaration
162_superpowers/skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md 242 No parallel implementers
162_superpowers/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md 66-74 Parallel Task pattern
162_superpowers/skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md 67-72 Codex process survival
162_superpowers/tests/skill-triggering/ — Trigger tests

Related corpus docs

  • Prompt/bootstrap: 002_prompt-context/02_sp_prompt.md
  • Skill catalog: 004_skill-system/04_sp_skill.md
  • Subagent workflows: 005_subagents-collab/05_sp_subagent.md

Change history

date change
2026-06-27 WP52: ≥12 KB — mapping tables, multi_agent, tests, boundaries
2026-06-27 WP38: source citations + corpus links
2026-05-31 Initial sp tool axis stub

13. Skill-orchestrated tool sequences (workflow examples)

Superpowers never registers these as APIs — they are ordered host tool calls prescribed in SKILL.md:

Workflow skill Typical tool chain (Claude names) Codex mapping
brainstorming Read → Skill → Write (spec doc) → optional Bash (start-server.sh) Same with native file/shell tools
writing-plans Read design → Write plan.md → Skill(plan reviewer) spawn_agent for review optional
subagent-driven-development TodoWrite → Task(impl) → Task(spec) → Task(quality) × N update_plan → spawn_agent × 3 × tasks
dispatching-parallel-agents Task × N parallel → Read summaries → Bash(test) spawn_agent × N → wait_agent each
test-driven-development Write(test) → Bash(test run) → Write(impl) → Bash(test) Identical pattern
using-git-worktrees Bash(git detect) → native Worktree OR Bash(worktree add) Sandbox may block worktree Bash
finishing-a-development-branch Bash(test) → Bash(git) → user App UI if sandbox blocks codex-tools.md handoff

Key insight: the "runtime" is the agent interpreting skill prose and calling host tools in sequence. No SP scheduler coordinates steps.

13.1 TodoWrite / update_plan as workflow state machine

using-superpowers/SKILL.md:57-74 mandates TodoWrite per checklist item when a skill defines a checklist. On Codex this becomes update_plan entries — the host plan UI becomes the visible state of skill progress, not an SP database.

Brainstorming checklist has 9 ordered items (brainstorming/SKILL.md:22-32); SDD controller creates todos for all plan tasks upfront before first implementer dispatch.

13.2 Explicit skill requests vs automatic triggering

tests/explicit-skill-requests/ validates user saying "use the test-driven-development skill" paths distinct from implicit triggering in tests/skill-triggering/. Both paths still end in host Skill/activate_skill — SP tests document expected harness behavior, not SP dispatch code.

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Codex wait vs wait_agent Documented in codex-tools.md Verify on Codex version bump
OpenCode tool names Inline bootstrap Host renames break mapping text
Copilot async bash Mapping doc only Copilot CLI tool surface evolves
Codex hooks removed Skills path activation No session-start for Codex in tree
Workflow tool chains Documented as skill-orchestrated Host tool renames require mapping doc updates

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 003_tool-runtime/03_sp_tool.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers — Tool Runtime

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is not an independent tool runtime. It does not own shell/file/search/MCP dispatch, approval gates, or structured function-call loops. Its runtime surface is bootstrap policy + skill catalog + cross-host tool-name adaptation that teaches the active harness how to interpret Claude Code-oriented skill text.

Architecture

flowchart LR
  subgraph SP["Superpowers owns"]
    Skills["skills/*/SKILL.md"]
    Bootstrap["using-superpowers"]
    Maps["references/*-tools.md"]
    OCMap["OpenCode inline mapping"]
  end

  subgraph Host["Host owns"]
    Loop["LLM tool loop"]
    Catalog["Tool registry"]
    Dispatch["Tool dispatcher"]
    Approval["Exec approval / sandbox"]
  end

  Skills --> Bootstrap
  Bootstrap --> Agent["Agent reads skill"]
  Agent --> Maps
  Maps --> HostTools["Host-native tool calls"]
  OCMap --> HostTools
  HostTools --> Dispatch
  Dispatch --> Loop

1. Classification — ownership table

Layer Superpowers owns? Implementation
LLM request/response loop No Host harness
Tool catalog definition No Host (Read, Bash, spawn_agent, etc.)
Tool dispatch / routing No Host dispatcher
Tool result parsing No Host
MCP server bridge No Host or user config
Skill catalog (SKILL.md) Yes skills/ tree
Bootstrap invocation policy Yes using-superpowers
Tool-name adaptation docs Yes references/*.md, OpenCode JS
Behavioral "use TodoWrite" rules Yes In skill bodies (host executes)

Negative claim: Superpowers is not a structured function-call dispatcher, JSON schema registry, or plugin tool loader beyond declaring "skills": "./skills/" in host manifests.

2. Skills as tool-usage curriculum

using-superpowers/SKILL.md:30-36 — access patterns:

Host Skill invocation tool
Claude Code Skill
Copilot CLI skill
Gemini CLI activate_skill
Codex Native skill load — "just follow the instructions" (codex-tools.md:12)

Critical rule (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:30):

Never use the Read tool on skill files [in Claude Code].

Skill tool is the host's dynamic loader; Read bypasses activation metadata.

Flow when checklist present (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:57-74):

  1. Invoke skill
  2. Announce usage
  3. If checklist → create todo per item via host task tracker
  4. Follow skill exactly

TodoWrite update_plan

Bash Native shell tools

Multi-agent gate (codex-tools.md:18-25):

13. Skill-orchestrated tool sequences (workflow examples)

Superpowers never registers these as APIs — they are ordered host tool calls prescribed in SKILL.md:

Workflow skill Typical tool chain (Claude names) Codex mapping
brainstorming Read → Skill → Write (spec doc) → optional Bash (start-server.sh) Same with native file/shell tools
writing-plans Read design → Write plan.md → Skill(plan reviewer) spawn_agent for review optional

using-git-worktrees Bash(git detect) → native Worktree OR Bash(worktree add) Sandbox may block worktree Bash
finishing-a-development-branch Bash(test) → Bash(git) → user App UI if sandbox blocks codex-tools.md handoff

Key insight: the "runtime" is the agent interpreting skill prose and calling host tools in sequence. No SP scheduler coordinates steps.

13.1 TodoWrite / update_plan as workflow state machine

using-superpowers/SKILL.md:57-74 mandates TodoWrite per checklist item when a skill defines a checklist. On Codex this becomes update_plan entries — the host plan UI becomes the visible state of skill progress, not an SP database.

Brainstorming checklist has 9 ordered items (brainstorming/SKILL.md:22-32); SDD controller creates todos for all plan tasks upfront before first implementer dispatch.

tests/explicit-skill-requests/ validates user saying "use the test-driven-development skill" paths distinct from implicit triggering in tests/skill-triggering/. Both paths still end in host Skill/activate_skill — SP tests document expected harness behavior, not SP dispatch code.

  subgraph Library["skills/ library"]
    US["using-superpowers (bootstrap)"]
    Proc["Process: brainstorming, debugging"]
    Impl["Implementation: TDD, plans, worktrees"]

    CodexP[".codex-plugin plugin.json"]

README / docs

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills...
---

README / docs

NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST

README / docs

NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST

README / docs

{
  "name": "superpowers",
  "version": "5.1.0",
  "skills": "./skills/",
  "keywords": ["brainstorming", "subagent-driven-development", "tdd", "debugging"],
  "interface": {
    "capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"],
    "defaultPrompt": ["I've got an idea...", "Let's add a feature..."]
  }
}

Main analysis

Superpowers — Skill System

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is primarily a skill system: a portable methodology library of skills/*/SKILL.md files plus harness-specific activation glue (SessionStart hooks, Codex plugin manifest, OpenCode JS plugin, Gemini @imports). There is no universal skill VM — loading, permissions, and UI are host-dependent.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  subgraph Library["skills/ library"]
    US["using-superpowers (bootstrap)"]
    Proc["Process: brainstorming, debugging"]
    Impl["Implementation: TDD, plans, worktrees"]
    Collab["Collaboration: SDD, parallel agents, review"]
    Meta["Meta: writing-skills"]
  end

  subgraph Activation["Host activation"]
    CodexP[".codex-plugin plugin.json"]
    Hook["hooks/session-start"]
    OC["OpenCode superpowers.js"]
    Gem["GEMINI.md imports"]
  end

  US --> Hook
  US --> OC
  US --> Gem
  Library --> CodexP

  CodexP --> HostLoad["Host skill loader"]
  Hook --> HostLoad
  OC --> HostLoad
  Gem --> HostLoad

  HostLoad --> Agent["Agent invokes skills per using-superpowers rules"]

1. Core model — SKILL.md schema

1.1 File layout

  • Each first-party skill: skills/<kebab-name>/SKILL.md
  • Colocated assets: prompt templates (*.md), scripts (scripts/), references (references/)
  • Observed YAML frontmatter:
---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills...
---

Required fields in practice: name, description (used for discovery/triggering).

1.2 Bootstrap skill

using-superpowers is the meta-skill:

  • Mandates skill invocation before any action (SKILL.md:10-16)
  • Defines instruction priority vs user AGENTS.md (:18-26)
  • Platform Skill tool access (:28-36)
  • Skill flow DOT graph (:48-75)
  • Red-flag rationalization table (:78-95)
  • Subagent skip gate (:6-8)

Embedded wholesale by hooks/session-start:17-35 on SessionStart.

1.3 Skill types (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:107-113)

Type Examples Agent behavior
Rigid TDD, systematic-debugging Follow exactly
Flexible Pattern skills Adapt principles

Declared per skill, not in central schema registry.

2. First-party skill inventory (14 skills)

Corpus checkout lists:

Category Skill Purpose
Meta using-superpowers Bootstrap + skill invocation rules
Meta writing-skills Author/test new skills (anthropic-best-practices, persuasion-principles refs)
Process brainstorming Design-before-code, HARD-GATE, visual companion
Process systematic-debugging 4-phase root cause (Iron Law: no fixes before investigation)
Process verification-before-completion Evidence before declaring done
Implementation test-driven-development RED-GREEN-REFACTOR, delete code written before tests
Implementation writing-plans Bite-sized tasks with paths + verification
Implementation executing-plans Batch execution with human checkpoints
Implementation using-git-worktrees Isolated workspace via native or git fallback
Implementation finishing-a-development-branch Merge/PR/keep/discard workflow
Collaboration subagent-driven-development Fresh subagent per task + 2-stage review
Collaboration dispatching-parallel-agents Parallel independent investigations
Collaboration requesting-code-review Pre-review checklist + reviewer prompt
Collaboration receiving-code-review Respond to feedback systematically

README also references workflow ordering (README.md:154-170):

brainstorming → worktrees → writing-plans → (SDD | executing-plans) → TDD during impl → code review → finishing branch.

3. Representative skill internals

3.1 brainstorming (skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md)

  • <HARD-GATE> blocks all implementation until design approved (:12-14)
  • 9-item checklist with TodoWrite per item (:22-32)
  • Spec output: docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md
  • Visual companion optional branch (visual-companion.md)
  • Server scripts: scripts/server.cjs, start-server.sh, stop-server.sh

3.2 test-driven-development

Iron Law (SKILL.md:33-35):

NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST

Write code before test → delete and restart (:37-45). Includes testing anti-patterns reference in tree.

3.3 systematic-debugging

Iron Law (SKILL.md:18-20):

NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST

Four phases: investigation → hypothesis → fix → verification. Sub-references: root-cause-tracing, defense-in-depth, condition-based-waiting.

3.4 subagent-driven-development

Support files:

File Role
implementer-prompt.md Full task text + escalation statuses
spec-reviewer-prompt.md Spec compliance review
code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md Quality review

Integration deps (SKILL.md:268-279): worktrees, writing-plans, requesting-code-review, finishing branch, TDD for implementers.

3.5 writing-plans

Includes plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md for plan quality review before execution.

3.6 writing-skills (meta)

References:

  • anthropic-best-practices.md
  • persuasion-principles.md

Contribution guide (README.md:207-217): new skills via writing-skills skill; must work cross-harness.

4. Activation surfaces by host

Host Surface Mechanism
Codex .codex-plugin/plugin.json "skills": "./skills/" — marketplace plugin v5.1.0
Claude Code .claude-plugin/ + hooks SessionStart injects bootstrap; skills in plugin bundle
Cursor .cursor-plugin/plugin.json "skills": "./skills/", hooks via hooks-cursor.json
Copilot CLI marketplace skill tool + SessionStart additionalContext
Gemini CLI extension + GEMINI.md @./skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md + gemini-tools
OpenCode superpowers.js config.skills.paths push + bootstrap in first user msg

4.1 Codex plugin metadata (.codex-plugin/plugin.json)

{
  "name": "superpowers",
  "version": "5.1.0",
  "skills": "./skills/",
  "keywords": ["brainstorming", "subagent-driven-development", "tdd", "debugging"],
  "interface": {
    "capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"],
    "defaultPrompt": ["I've got an idea...", "Let's add a feature..."]
  }
}

No skill runtime code in JSON — declarative path only.

4.2 Claude hooks (hooks/hooks.json)

SessionStart matcher: startup|clear|compact — skills re-bootstrap after compaction.

4.3 OpenCode plugin (most concrete in-repo adapter)

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:

  • extractAndStripFrontmatter() — local YAML parse without skills-core dep (:16-34)
  • _bootstrapCache — single read per session (:49-53)
  • config hook — append skills dir to config.skills.paths (:103-108)
  • experimental.chat.messages.transform — prepend bootstrap (:120-133)

Documented install: .opencode/INSTALL.md, docs/README.opencode.md.

4.4 Cursor manifest note

.cursor-plugin/plugin.json references ./agents/ and ./commands/ — directories absent in checkout; skills path valid.

5. Skill discovery and triggering policy

5.1 Mandatory check (using-superpowers)

Before any response including clarifications — invoke Skill tool if ≥1% applicability.

Priority when multiple skills (:97-105):

  1. Process (brainstorm, debug)
  2. Implementation

5.2 Brainstorming auto-path

If entering plan mode without prior brainstorm → invoke brainstorming first (flow graph :62-65).

5.3 Tests

Directory Purpose
tests/skill-triggering/ Automated prompt → skill trigger checks
tests/explicit-skill-requests/ Explicit user skill requests
tests/opencode/ OpenCode bootstrap caching
tests/claude-code/ SDD, code review, document review integration

Prompt fixtures: tests/skill-triggering/prompts/writing-plans.txt, executing-plans.txt, etc.

6. Legacy skills path migration

hooks/session-start:12-15 warns if ~/.config/superpowers/skills exists — superseded by host-native skill dirs (e.g. ~/.claude/skills). One-time agent message to user on first reply.

7. Packaging and distribution

Artifact Role
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json Claude marketplace registration
.cursor-plugin/plugin.json Cursor marketplace
gemini-extension.json Gemini extension manifest
scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh Publish sync to Codex plugin layout
RELEASE-NOTES.md Version history

package.json — OpenCode main only; no npm skill runtime.

8. What Superpowers does NOT implement

Feature Notes
Universal skill VM Host loads SKILL.md
Skill permission ACLs Host policy
Skill marketplace server GitHub + host marketplaces
Semantic skill routing code Text + agent judgment + tests
SKILL.md JSON schema validator Informal frontmatter only
Inactive/skills_ref split Single skills/ dir

OpenCode plugin is the most concrete adapter; others are manifest + hook + docs.

9. Parity matrix vs Codex (co) skills

Concern Codex (co) Superpowers (sp)
Skill storage ~/.codex/skills, project skills Plugin skills/ directory
SKILL.md format Compatible convention Source of truth for methodology
Activation Native skill tool + rules Bootstrap mandates invocation
Plugin manifest .codex-plugin Ships own .codex-plugin
Methodology depth User-dependent 14 first-party workflow skills
Cross-host Codex-primary Claude, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini, OpenCode

10. Evidence quality bar

  • Full 14-skill inventory with categories
  • SKILL.md schema + bootstrap role
  • Per-host activation table
  • OpenCode adapter functions cited
  • Support file colocation pattern
  • Test directories indexed
  • Negative claims: no universal runtime

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot v5.1.0 Current
Skill count 14 first-party README may add/remove on release
Codex hooks Removed upstream Skills-only activation for Codex
Cursor agents/commands Manifest refs, dirs missing Plugin packaging may catch up

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 004_skill-system/04_sp_skill.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers — Skill System

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is primarily a skill system: a portable methodology library of skills/*/SKILL.md files plus harness-specific activation glue (SessionStart hooks, Codex plugin manifest, OpenCode JS plugin, Gemini @imports). There is no universal skill VM — loading, permissions, and UI are host-dependent.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  subgraph Library["skills/ library"]
    US["using-superpowers (bootstrap)"]
    Proc["Process: brainstorming, debugging"]
    Impl["Implementation: TDD, plans, worktrees"]
    Collab["Collaboration: SDD, parallel agents, review"]
    Meta["Meta: writing-skills"]
  end

  subgraph Activation["Host activation"]
    CodexP[".codex-plugin plugin.json"]
    Hook["hooks/session-start"]
    OC["OpenCode superpowers.js"]
    Gem["GEMINI.md imports"]
  end

  US --> Hook
  US --> OC
  US --> Gem
  Library --> CodexP

  CodexP --> HostLoad["Host skill loader"]
  Hook --> HostLoad
  OC --> HostLoad
  Gem --> HostLoad

  HostLoad --> Agent["Agent invokes skills per using-superpowers rules"]

1. Core model — SKILL.md schema

1.1 File layout

  • Each first-party skill: skills/<kebab-name>/SKILL.md
  • Colocated assets: prompt templates (*.md), scripts (scripts/), references (references/)
  • Observed YAML frontmatter:
---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills...
---

Required fields in practice: name, description (used for discovery/triggering).

1.2 Bootstrap skill

using-superpowers is the meta-skill:

  • Mandates skill invocation before any action (SKILL.md:10-16)
  • Defines instruction priority vs user AGENTS.md (:18-26)
  • Platform Skill tool access (:28-36)
  • Skill flow DOT graph (:48-75)
  • Red-flag rationalization table (:78-95)
  • Subagent skip gate (:6-8)

Embedded wholesale by hooks/session-start:17-35 on SessionStart.

1.3 Skill types (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:107-113)

Type Examples Agent behavior
Rigid TDD, systematic-debugging Follow exactly
Flexible Pattern skills Adapt principles

Declared per skill, not in central schema registry.

Implementation writing-plans Bite-sized tasks with paths + verification
Implementation executing-plans Batch execution with human checkpoints
Implementation using-git-worktrees Isolated workspace via native or git fallback
Implementation finishing-a-development-branch Merge/PR/keep/discard workflow

Collaboration dispatching-parallel-agents Parallel independent investigations
Collaboration requesting-code-review Pre-review checklist + reviewer prompt
Collaboration receiving-code-review Respond to feedback systematically

brainstorming → worktrees → writing-plans → (SDD | executing-plans) → TDD during impl → code review → finishing branch.

Axis 05: Subagents

AXIS

005_subagents-collab/05_sp_subagent.md

Overview

Axis 05: Subagents — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers defines subagent collaboration workflows in SKILL.md, not a native subagent runtime. Spawn, lifecycle, context isolation, async completion, parallelism li

  • flowchart TB
  • Controller["Controller agent (main session)"]
  • subgraph SDD["subagent-driven-development (sequential)"]
  • Imp["Implementer subagent"]
  • SpecR["Spec reviewer"]
  • QualR["Code quality reviewer"]
  • end
  • subgraph Parallel["dispatching-parallel-agents"]
  • A1["Agent domain A"]
  • A2["Agent domain B"]
  • A3["Agent domain C"]

Topics covered

  • Architecture
    1. Negative claim — no SP subagent engine
    1. Core pattern: subagent-driven development (SDD)

  • 2.2 When to use vs alternatives (:16-40)
  • 2.3 Per-task pipeline (:44-86)
  • 2.4 Implementer status protocol (:104-120)
  • 2.5 Model selection (:89-102)
    1. Prompt templates — context curation

  • 3.2 Spec reviewer (spec-reviewer-prompt.md)
  • 3.3 Code quality reviewer (code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)
  • 3.4 Final reviewer

Source code snippets

README / docs

flowchart TB
  Controller["Controller agent (main session)"]

  subgraph SDD["subagent-driven-development (sequential)"]
    Imp["Implementer subagent"]
    SpecR["Spec reviewer"]
    QualR["Code quality reviewer"]
  end

  subgraph Parallel["dispatching-parallel-agents"]
    A1["Agent domain A"]
    A2["Agent domain B"]
    A3["Agent domain C"]
  end

  Controller --> Imp
  Imp --> SpecR

  QualR --> Controller

  Controller --> A1
  Controller --> A2
  Controller --> A3
  A1 --> Integrate["Review + merge + test suite"]
  A2 --> Integrate
  A3 --> Integrate

README / docs

Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    ## Task Description
    [FULL TEXT - don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context
    [Scene-setting: dependencies, architecture]

    ## Before You Begin
    Ask questions now...

    ## Your Job
    1. Implement exactly
    2. Write tests (TDD if specified)
    3. Verify, commit, self-review
    4. Report status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | NEEDS_CONTEXT | BLOCKED

README / docs

[features]
multi_agent = true

Main analysis

Superpowers — Subagents / Collaboration

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers defines subagent collaboration workflows in SKILL.md, not a native subagent runtime. Spawn, lifecycle, context isolation, async completion, parallelism limits, and model routing are delegated to the active host harness. SP's contribution is precise prompt templates, controller discipline, and host tool-name mapping.

Architecture

flowchart TB
  Controller["Controller agent (main session)"]

  subgraph SDD["subagent-driven-development (sequential)"]
    Imp["Implementer subagent"]
    SpecR["Spec reviewer"]
    QualR["Code quality reviewer"]
  end

  subgraph Parallel["dispatching-parallel-agents"]
    A1["Agent domain A"]
    A2["Agent domain B"]
    A3["Agent domain C"]
  end

  Plan["writing-plans output"] --> Controller
  Controller --> Imp
  Imp --> SpecR
  SpecR --> QualR
  QualR --> Controller

  Controller --> A1
  Controller --> A2
  Controller --> A3
  A1 --> Integrate["Review + merge + test suite"]
  A2 --> Integrate
  A3 --> Integrate

1. Negative claim — no SP subagent engine

Capability Owner
spawn_agent / Task / @generalist dispatch Host
Context isolation between workers Host (best-effort)
Subagent depth limits Host
Parallel execution scheduler Host
Worker transcript storage Host
Review prompt content Superpowers SKILL.md + templates
Controller workflow rules Superpowers

Superpowers cannot guarantee technical isolation — it requires the controller to never let subagents inherit session history and to paste full task text inline.

2. Core pattern: subagent-driven development (SDD)

skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md

2.1 Principle (:10-14)

Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

Why subagents (:10-11): delegate with constructed instructions; preserve controller context for coordination.

Continuous execution (:14): no "should I continue?" between tasks — only stop if BLOCKED, ambiguous, or done.

2.2 When to use vs alternatives (:16-40)

Decision graph:

Condition Choose
Plan + independent tasks + same session subagent-driven-development
Plan + parallel separate session executing-plans
No plan brainstorm / manual first

2.3 Per-task pipeline (:44-86)

  1. Controller reads plan once; extracts all tasks with full text; TodoWrite all
  2. Dispatch implementer (implementer-prompt.md)
  3. Implementer may ask questions before coding — controller answers (:252-255)
  4. Implementer implements, tests, commits, self-reviews
  5. Dispatch spec reviewer — spec compliance ONLY
  6. If fail → implementer fixes → re-review spec
  7. Only after spec ✅ → dispatch code quality reviewer
  8. If fail → fix → re-review quality
  9. Mark todo complete; next task

Order violation red flag (:249): never start quality review before spec compliance.

Parallelism red flag (:242): never dispatch multiple implementers concurrently — file conflicts.

2.4 Implementer status protocol (:104-120)

Status Controller action
DONE Proceed to spec review
DONE_WITH_CONCERNS Read concerns; address if correctness/scope
NEEDS_CONTEXT Provide missing context; re-dispatch
BLOCKED Context fix, bigger model, split task, or escalate human

Never retry same model without changes after BLOCKED.

2.5 Model selection (:89-102)

Task signal Model tier
1-2 files, complete spec Fast/cheap
Multi-file integration Standard
Architecture/review Most capable

Cost tradeoff (:230-234): more invocations (impl + 2 reviewers × N tasks) vs fewer late bugs.

3. Prompt templates — context curation

3.1 Implementer (implementer-prompt.md)

Structure:

Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    ## Task Description
    [FULL TEXT - don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context
    [Scene-setting: dependencies, architecture]

    ## Before You Begin
    Ask questions now...

    ## Your Job
    1. Implement exactly
    2. Write tests (TDD if specified)
    3. Verify, commit, self-review
    4. Report status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | NEEDS_CONTEXT | BLOCKED

Scene-setting mandatory (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:244).

Escalation (implementer-prompt.md:57-72): BLOCKED when architectural decisions, unbounded reading, plan mismatch.

File scope (:44-55): follow plan structure; report DONE_WITH_CONCERNS if files grow beyond intent.

3.2 Spec reviewer (spec-reviewer-prompt.md)

Verifies requirements met, nothing extra — before quality review.

3.3 Code quality reviewer (code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)

Structure, tests, maintainability — only after spec ✅.

3.4 Final reviewer

After all tasks — entire implementation review before finishing-a-development-branch.

4. Parallel pattern: dispatching-parallel-agents

skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md

4.1 Use when (:36-45)

  • 3+ test files / subsystems failing independently
  • No shared state between investigations
  • Domains understood without cross-context

Don't use when failures related, exploratory unknown root cause, or agents would edit same files.

4.2 Pattern (:47-82)

  1. Group failures by domain
  2. Craft focused prompts (scope, goal, constraints, output format)
  3. Dispatch parallel Tasks
  4. Integrate: read summaries, conflict check, full test suite

Example prompt shape (:91-110): paste failing test names, errors, constraints ("Do NOT just increase timeouts").

4.3 Real session example (:133-158)

6 failures → 3 agents → independent fixes → full green, zero conflicts.

Contrast with SDD: parallel investigation, not parallel implementation.

5. Host tool mapping for subagents

Host Dispatch Wait/result Parallel
Claude Code Task returns to session Multiple Task
Codex spawn_agent wait_agent, close_agent Multiple spawn (needs multi_agent)
Gemini @generalist + filled template agent completes in thread Same-prompt multi @
OpenCode @mention subagent host-specific host-specific
Copilot task + agent_type read_agent, list_agents Multiple task

From references/codex-tools.md:5-14:

[features]
multi_agent = true

From references/gemini-tools.md:25-31: named skill agents map to @generalist or @code-reviewer with filled template.

From references/copilot-tools.md:15-18: task with general-purpose or explore.

6. Integration with other skills

SDD required workflow skills (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:268-279):

Skill Role in collaboration
using-git-worktrees Isolated workspace before execution
writing-plans Produces plan SDD consumes
requesting-code-review Reviewer subagent templates
finishing-a-development-branch Post-SDD merge/PR
test-driven-development Implementer discipline

Alternative: executing-plans for parallel session with human checkpoints between batches.

Code review axis:

  • requesting-code-review — pre-review checklist, code-reviewer.md
  • receiving-code-review — systematic response to human review

7. SDD example narrative (SKILL.md:128-201)

Walkthrough: 5 tasks, implementer asks hook install level, spec reviewer catches extra --json flag, quality reviewer catches magic number — demonstrates review loops and re-dispatch same implementer for fixes.

8. Red flags summary (:236-265)

Never:

  • Skip spec OR quality review
  • Parallel implementers
  • Subagent reads plan file
  • Skip scene-setting context
  • Accept "close enough" on spec issues
  • Implementer self-review replaces human-style reviewer subagents
  • Move to next task with open review issues

If reviewer finds issues: implementer fixes → reviewer re-reviews until ✅.

If stuck: dispatch fix subagent — controller should not patch manually (context pollution).

9. Tests

Path Coverage
tests/claude-code/test-subagent-driven-development.sh SDD harness
tests/claude-code/test-subagent-driven-development-integration.sh Integration
tests/subagent-driven-dev/ go-fractals, svelte-todo fixtures
tests/claude-code/test-requesting-code-review.sh Review flow

Fixtures include plan.md, design.md, scaffold.sh per sample project.

10. Parity matrix vs Codex (co)

Concern Codex (co) Superpowers (sp)
Subagent runtime Rust spawn_agent pipeline Host tools only
Workflow spec User prompts subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md
Review stages User-defined Spec then quality (mandated order)
Parallel impl Host policy Forbidden by SDD skill
Parallel debug Host policy dispatching-parallel-agents skill
Context curation Caller responsibility Detailed template + red flags
Model routing Host config Skill guidance by task complexity

  • SDD full pipeline with line refs
  • Status protocol documented
  • Parallel vs sequential distinction
  • All three prompt templates indexed
  • Host mapping table
  • Integration skill deps
  • Test fixtures referenced

date change
2026-06-27 WP52: ≥12 KB — SDD pipeline, templates, parallel skills, tests
2026-06-27 WP38: source citations + corpus links
2026-05-31 Initial sp subagent axis stub

13. Controller anti-patterns (collaboration failures)

Documented red flags (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:236-265) with operational meaning:

Anti-pattern Why it breaks collaboration
Subagent reads plan file Worker wastes context tokens; may skim miss tasks
Controller patches after failed impl Pollutes controller context; violates fix-subagent dispatch
Parallel implementers Merge conflicts; reviewers cannot attribute changes
Skip spec review Over/under-build reaches quality review undetected
Quality review before spec Wrong gate order — aesthetic polish on wrong behavior
Ignore BLOCKED status Repeated failure without model/context/task change
"Should I continue?" mid-plan Violates continuous execution contract (:14)

dispatching-parallel-agents adds integration step (SKILL.md:168-173): after parallel return, controller must run full test suite — partial green is insufficient.

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Codex multi_agent Required for SDD on Codex Verify config.toml on upgrade
Host Task API Claude Task vs Codex spawn_agent Tool renames per release
SDD proportionality Branch drew/sup-333-2-sdd-proportionality Skill text may add triviality gates
Test fixtures go-fractals, svelte-todo May expand harness scenarios
Review dispatch sdd-review-dispatch branch history Reviewer prompt wiring may evolve

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 005_subagents-collab/05_sp_subagent.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers — Subagents / Collaboration

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers defines subagent collaboration workflows in SKILL.md, not a native subagent runtime. Spawn, lifecycle, context isolation, async completion, parallelism limits, and model routing are delegated to the active host harness. SP's contribution is precise prompt templates, controller discipline, and host tool-name mapping.

Architecture

flowchart TB
  Controller["Controller agent (main session)"]

  subgraph SDD["subagent-driven-development (sequential)"]
    Imp["Implementer subagent"]
    SpecR["Spec reviewer"]
    QualR["Code quality reviewer"]
  end

  subgraph Parallel["dispatching-parallel-agents"]
    A1["Agent domain A"]
    A2["Agent domain B"]
    A3["Agent domain C"]
  end

  Plan["writing-plans output"] --> Controller
  Controller --> Imp
  Imp --> SpecR
  SpecR --> QualR
  QualR --> Controller

  Controller --> A1
  Controller --> A2
  Controller --> A3
  A1 --> Integrate["Review + merge + test suite"]
  A2 --> Integrate
  A3 --> Integrate

1. Negative claim — no SP subagent engine

Capability Owner
spawn_agent / Task / @generalist dispatch Host
Context isolation between workers Host (best-effort)
Subagent depth limits Host
Parallel execution scheduler Host
Worker transcript storage Host
Review prompt content Superpowers SKILL.md + templates
Controller workflow rules Superpowers

Superpowers cannot guarantee technical isolation — it requires the controller to never let subagents inherit session history and to paste full task text inline.

2. Core pattern: subagent-driven development (SDD)

skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md

2.1 Principle (:10-14)

Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

Why subagents (:10-11): delegate with constructed instructions; preserve controller context for coordination.

Continuous execution (:14): no "should I continue?" between tasks — only stop if BLOCKED, ambiguous, or done.

2.2 When to use vs alternatives (:16-40)

Decision graph:

Condition Choose
Plan + independent tasks + same session subagent-driven-development
Plan + parallel separate session executing-plans
No plan brainstorm / manual first

2.3 Per-task pipeline (:44-86)

  1. Controller reads plan once; extracts all tasks with full text; TodoWrite all
  2. Dispatch implementer (implementer-prompt.md)
  3. Implementer may ask questions before coding — controller answers (:252-255)
  4. Implementer implements, tests, commits, self-reviews
  5. Dispatch spec reviewer — spec compliance ONLY
  6. If fail → implementer fixes → re-review spec
  7. Only after spec ✅ → dispatch code quality reviewer
  8. If fail → fix → re-review quality
  9. Mark todo complete; next task

Order violation red flag (:249): never start quality review before spec compliance.

Parallelism red flag (:242): never dispatch multiple implementers concurrently — file conflicts.

2.4 Implementer status protocol (:104-120)

Status Controller action
DONE Proceed to spec review
DONE_WITH_CONCERNS Read concerns; address if correctness/scope
NEEDS_CONTEXT Provide missing context; re-dispatch
BLOCKED Context fix, bigger model, split task, or escalate human

Never retry same model without changes after BLOCKED.

2.5 Model selection (:89-102)

Task signal Model tier
1-2 files, complete spec Fast/cheap
Multi-file integration Standard
Architecture/review Most capable

Cost tradeoff (:230-234): more invocations (impl + 2 reviewers × N tasks) vs fewer late bugs.

3. Prompt templates — context curation

3.1 Implementer (implementer-prompt.md)

Structure:

Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    ## Task Description
    [FULL TEXT - don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context
    [Scene-setting: dependencies, architecture]

    ## Before You Begin
    Ask questions now...

    ## Your Job
    1. Implement exactly
    2. Write tests (TDD if specified)
    3. Verify, commit, self-review
    4. Report status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | NEEDS_CONTEXT | BLOCKED

Scene-setting mandatory (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:244).

Escalation (implementer-prompt.md:57-72): BLOCKED when architectural decisions, unbounded reading, plan mismatch.

File scope (:44-55): follow plan structure; report DONE_WITH_CONCERNS if files grow beyond intent.

3.2 Spec reviewer (spec-reviewer-prompt.md)

Verifies requirements met, nothing extra — before quality review.

3.3 Code quality reviewer (code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)

Structure, tests, maintainability — only after spec ✅.

3.4 Final reviewer

After all tasks — entire implementation review before finishing-a-development-branch.

4. Parallel pattern: dispatching-parallel-agents

skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md

4.1 Use when (:36-45)

  • 3+ test files / subsystems failing independently
  • No shared state between investigations
  • Domains understood without cross-context

Don't use when failures related, exploratory unknown root cause, or agents would edit same files.

4.2 Pattern (:47-82)

  1. Group failures by domain
  2. Craft focused prompts (scope, goal, constraints, output format)
  3. Dispatch parallel Tasks
  4. Integrate: read summaries, conflict check, full test suite

Example prompt shape (:91-110): paste failing test names, errors, constraints ("Do NOT just increase timeouts").

4.3 Real session example (:133-158)

6 failures → 3 agents → independent fixes → full green, zero conflicts.

Contrast with SDD: parallel investigation, not parallel implementation.

Host Dispatch Wait/result Parallel
Claude Code Task returns to session Multiple Task
Codex spawn_agent wait_agent, close_agent Multiple spawn (needs multi_agent)
Gemini @generalist + filled template agent completes in thread Same-prompt multi @
OpenCode @mention subagent host-specific host-specific
Copilot task + agent_type read_agent, list_agents Multiple task

From references/codex-tools.md:5-14:

[features]
multi_agent = true

From references/gemini-tools.md:25-31: named skill agents map to @generalist or @code-reviewer with filled template.

From references/copilot-tools.md:15-18: task with general-purpose or explore.

006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_sp_surface.md

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is a multi-harness skills/plugin distribution, not a general app-server agent runtime. The host agent owns model calls, session storage, primary UI, tool

Main analysis

Superpowers — App Server / Client Surfaces

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is a multi-harness skills/plugin distribution, not a general app-server agent runtime. The host agent owns model calls, session storage, primary UI, tool execution, subagent dispatch, and permissions. SP exposes plugin manifests, SessionStart hooks, OpenCode JS plugin, and one optional local HTTP/WebSocket visual companion for brainstorming.

Architecture

flowchart TB
  subgraph Hosts["Host surfaces (primary)"]
    Codex["Codex CLI / App + plugin market"]
    CC["Claude Code plugin"]
    Cur["Cursor plugin"]
    Cop["Copilot CLI marketplace"]
    Gem["Gemini extension"]
    OC["OpenCode plugin JS"]
  end

  subgraph SP["Superpowers artifacts"]
    Skills["skills/"]
    Hooks["hooks/"]
    Manifests[".*-plugin/ plugin.json"]
    Brain["brainstorm server.cjs"]
  end

  Skills --> Manifests
  Hooks --> CC
  Hooks --> Cur
  Manifests --> Codex
  Manifests --> Cur
  OC --> OC
  Gem --> Gem
  Brain -.-> Browser["Browser tab (optional)"]

  Hosts --> User["User session"]
  Brain -.-> User

1. Negative claim — no agent app-server

Surface Present in SP?
A2A / JSON-RPC agent server No
MCP server bundled No
LLM transport / API proxy No
Durable session DB No
SDK for third-party clients No
Codex-style app-server crate No

Host provides all execution and transport. Superpowers is content + packaging.

2. Surface inventory by harness

Harness Manifest / entry Bootstrap Skills discovery
Codex CLI/App .codex-plugin/plugin.json v5.1.0 Native plugin skills "skills": "./skills/"
Claude Code .claude-plugin/plugin.json + marketplace hooks/hooks.json SessionStart Plugin bundle
Cursor .cursor-plugin/plugin.json hooks/hooks-cursor.json "skills": "./skills/"
Copilot CLI obra/superpowers-marketplace SessionStart SDK JSON Plugin install
Gemini CLI gemini-extension.json GEMINI.md @imports Extension pack
OpenCode package.json → superpowers.js Message transform + config hook config.skills.paths
Factory Droid README install only Host-specific Marketplace add

Install instructions per harness: README.md:31-152.

3. Codex plugin surface (.codex-plugin/plugin.json)

Full interface block for Codex App marketplace UI:

"interface": {
  "displayName": "Superpowers",
  "shortDescription": "Planning, TDD, debugging, and delivery workflows for coding agents",
  "longDescription": "Use Superpowers to guide agent work through brainstorming...",
  "capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"],
  "defaultPrompt": [
    "I've got an idea for something I'd like to build.",
    "Let's add a feature to this project."
  ],
  "brandColor": "#F59E0B",
  "composerIcon": "./assets/superpowers-small.svg",
  "logo": "./assets/app-icon.png"
}

Client impact: plugin picker, composer icon, default prompts — not a separate Superpowers window.

Codex install (README.md:63-87): /plugins → search superpowers → Install; App sidebar Plugins → Coding section.

Note: upstream removed Codex-specific hooks branch (remove-codex-hooks) — activation is skills-directory native, not SessionStart shell hook.

4. Claude Code surface

4.1 Plugin metadata (.claude-plugin/plugin.json)

Name, description, version 5.1.0, keywords — no skills path in this minimal JSON (skills ship in bundle layout per Claude conventions).

4.2 Hooks (hooks/hooks.json)

"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{
    "type": "command",
    "command": "\"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.cmd\" session-start"
  }]
}]

hooks/session-start emits platform-specific JSON (additionalContext / hookSpecificOutput).

4.3 Marketplaces

  • Official: /plugin install superpowers@claude-plugins-official
  • Superpowers marketplace: obra/superpowers-marketplace

5. Cursor surface (.cursor-plugin/plugin.json)

{
  "name": "superpowers",
  "displayName": "Superpowers",
  "version": "5.1.0",
  "skills": "./skills/",
  "agents": "./agents/",
  "commands": "./commands/",
  "hooks": "./hooks/hooks-cursor.json"
}

Manifest drift: agents/ and commands/ referenced but absent in corpus checkout — skills and hooks paths valid.

Install: /add-plugin superpowers or marketplace search (README.md:130-138).

6. Gemini CLI surface

  • Extension: gemini extensions install https://github.com/obra/superpowers
  • Context file GEMINI.md:
@./skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md
@./skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md

Extension manifest: gemini-extension.json (repo root).

Tool mapping loaded at session start; subagents via @generalist (gemini-tools.md).

7. OpenCode surface — richest in-repo adapter

7.1 Package entry

// package.json
"main": ".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"

7.2 Plugin exports (superpowers.js:55-134)

Hook Purpose
config Push skills/ into config.skills.paths
experimental.chat.messages.transform Prepend bootstrap to first user message

Install path: agent fetches .opencode/INSTALL.md from GitHub raw URL (README.md:122-126).

Detailed docs: docs/README.opencode.md.

7.3 Design constraints documented in source

  • Bootstrap cached module-level (#1202)
  • Skip double injection if EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT present (:126-129)
  • User message not system — Qwen + token bloat issues

8. Copilot CLI surface

Marketplace:

copilot plugin marketplace add obra/superpowers-marketplace
copilot plugin install superpowers@superpowers-marketplace

SessionStart uses top-level additionalContext when COPILOT_CLI=1 (session-start:52-54).

Tool mapping: references/copilot-tools.md — includes async bash sessions, sql todos, GitHub MCP tools.

9. Visual companion — local HTTP/WebSocket server

Only SP-owned network server in core repo. Not an agent execution service.

9.1 Purpose (visual-companion.md:1-25)

Browser mockups for brainstorming — agent stays in host chat; user opens localhost URL.

9.2 Start session (visual-companion.md:35-42)

scripts/start-server.sh --project-dir /path/to/project
# JSON: port, url, screen_dir, state_dir

Persistence: <project>/.superpowers/brainstorm/<session-id>/.

9.3 Server implementation (server.cjs)

Feature Implementation
Port 49152 + random or BRAINSTORM_PORT (:76)
Host 127.0.0.1 default (:77)
Content CONTENT_DIR — newest .html by mtime (:116-125)
Frame wrap Fragments → frame-template.html (:112-114)
WebSocket RFC6455 reload on file change (:6-72)
Helper inject helper.js before </body> (:137-141)
Idle timeout ~30 min inactivity (documented in visual-companion)

9.4 Interaction loop

  1. Agent Write → screen_dir/*.html
  2. User views browser; clicks logged to state_dir/events JSONL
  3. User replies in terminal — primary feedback
  4. Agent reads events + terminal text next turn

9.5 Platform process lifetime

Host Note
Codex Reaps background PIDs — foreground mode via CODEX_CI detect
Claude Windows run_in_background: true
Gemini --foreground + background shell flag

9.6 Tests

tests/brainstorm-server/:

  • server.test.js — HTTP behavior
  • ws-protocol.test.js — WebSocket
  • windows-lifecycle.test.sh — Windows lifecycle

docs/superpowers/plans/ Implementation plans (e.g. codex-app-compatibility)
docs/superpowers/specs/ Design specs (e.g. worktree-rototill)
CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md Contributor agent instructions

Design docs produced by brainstorming skill: docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-*-design.md.

Asset Use
assets/superpowers-small.svg Codex composerIcon
assets/app-icon.png Codex logo

Web UI Codex App / Web Host UI only

Session persistence Host DB Host DB

MCP Host/config Not bundled
Multi-client SDK Codex SDK None

droid plugin install superpowers@superpowers

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot v5.1.0 Current

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_sp_surface.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers — App Server / Client Surfaces

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is a multi-harness skills/plugin distribution, not a general app-server agent runtime. The host agent owns model calls, session storage, primary UI, tool execution, subagent dispatch, and permissions. SP exposes plugin manifests, SessionStart hooks, OpenCode JS plugin, and one optional local HTTP/WebSocket visual companion for brainstorming.

Architecture

flowchart TB
  subgraph Hosts["Host surfaces (primary)"]
    Codex["Codex CLI / App + plugin market"]
    CC["Claude Code plugin"]
    Cur["Cursor plugin"]
    Cop["Copilot CLI marketplace"]
    Gem["Gemini extension"]
    OC["OpenCode plugin JS"]
  end

  subgraph SP["Superpowers artifacts"]
    Skills["skills/"]
    Hooks["hooks/"]
    Manifests[".*-plugin/ plugin.json"]
    Brain["brainstorm server.cjs"]
  end

  Skills --> Manifests
  Hooks --> CC
  Hooks --> Cur
  Manifests --> Codex
  Manifests --> Cur
  OC --> OC
  Gem --> Gem
  Brain -.-> Browser["Browser tab (optional)"]

  Hosts --> User["User session"]
  Brain -.-> User

1. Negative claim — no agent app-server

Surface Present in SP?
A2A / JSON-RPC agent server No
MCP server bundled No
LLM transport / API proxy No
Durable session DB No
SDK for third-party clients No
Codex-style app-server crate No

Host provides all execution and transport. Superpowers is content + packaging.

2. Surface inventory by harness

Harness Manifest / entry Bootstrap Skills discovery
Codex CLI/App .codex-plugin/plugin.json v5.1.0 Native plugin skills "skills": "./skills/"
Claude Code .claude-plugin/plugin.json + marketplace hooks/hooks.json SessionStart Plugin bundle
Cursor .cursor-plugin/plugin.json hooks/hooks-cursor.json "skills": "./skills/"
Copilot CLI obra/superpowers-marketplace SessionStart SDK JSON Plugin install
Gemini CLI gemini-extension.json GEMINI.md @imports Extension pack
OpenCode package.json → superpowers.js Message transform + config hook config.skills.paths
Factory Droid README install only Host-specific Marketplace add

Install instructions per harness: README.md:31-152.

3. Codex plugin surface (.codex-plugin/plugin.json)

Full interface block for Codex App marketplace UI:

"interface": {
  "displayName": "Superpowers",
  "shortDescription": "Planning, TDD, debugging, and delivery workflows for coding agents",
  "longDescription": "Use Superpowers to guide agent work through brainstorming...",
  "capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"],
  "defaultPrompt": [
    "I've got an idea for something I'd like to build.",
    "Let's add a feature to this project."
  ],
  "brandColor": "#F59E0B",
  "composerIcon": "./assets/superpowers-small.svg",
  "logo": "./assets/app-icon.png"
}

Client impact: plugin picker, composer icon, default prompts — not a separate Superpowers window.

Codex install (README.md:63-87): /plugins → search superpowers → Install; App sidebar Plugins → Coding section.

Note: upstream removed Codex-specific hooks branch (remove-codex-hooks) — activation is skills-directory native, not SessionStart shell hook.

4. Claude Code surface

4.1 Plugin metadata (.claude-plugin/plugin.json)

Name, description, version 5.1.0, keywords — no skills path in this minimal JSON (skills ship in bundle layout per Claude conventions).

4.2 Hooks (hooks/hooks.json)

"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{
    "type": "command",
    "command": "\"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.cmd\" session-start"
  }]
}]

hooks/session-start emits platform-specific JSON (additionalContext / hookSpecificOutput).

4.3 Marketplaces

  • Official: /plugin install superpowers@claude-plugins-official
  • Superpowers marketplace: obra/superpowers-marketplace

5. Cursor surface (.cursor-plugin/plugin.json)

{
  "name": "superpowers",
  "displayName": "Superpowers",
  "version": "5.1.0",
  "skills": "./skills/",
  "agents": "./agents/",
  "commands": "./commands/",
  "hooks": "./hooks/hooks-cursor.json"
}

Manifest drift: agents/ and commands/ referenced but absent in corpus checkout — skills and hooks paths valid.

Install: /add-plugin superpowers or marketplace search (README.md:130-138).

6. Gemini CLI surface

  • Extension: gemini extensions install https://github.com/obra/superpowers
  • Context file GEMINI.md:
@./skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md
@./skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md

Extension manifest: gemini-extension.json (repo root).

Tool mapping loaded at session start; subagents via @generalist (gemini-tools.md).

7. OpenCode surface — richest in-repo adapter

7.1 Package entry

// package.json
"main": ".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"

7.2 Plugin exports (superpowers.js:55-134)

Hook Purpose
config Push skills/ into config.skills.paths
experimental.chat.messages.transform Prepend bootstrap to first user message

Install path: agent fetches .opencode/INSTALL.md from GitHub raw URL (README.md:122-126).

Detailed docs: docs/README.opencode.md.

7.3 Design constraints documented in source

  • Bootstrap cached module-level (#1202)
  • Skip double injection if EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT present (:126-129)
  • User message not system — Qwen + token bloat issues

Marketplace:

copilot plugin marketplace add obra/superpowers-marketplace
copilot plugin install superpowers@superpowers-marketplace

Tool mapping: references/copilot-tools.md — includes async bash sessions, sql todos, GitHub MCP tools.

Axis 07: Sandbox & policy

AXIS

007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_sp_sandbox.md

Overview

Axis 07: Sandbox & policy — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is a skills/bootstrap package, not an execution harness. It does not enforce OS process isolation, command approval UI, filesystem ACLs, network filte

Main analysis

Superpowers — Sandbox / Exec Policy

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is a skills/bootstrap package, not an execution harness. It does not enforce OS process isolation, command approval UI, filesystem ACLs, network filtering, PTY sandboxing, or shell policy evaluation. Actual execution, sandbox seatbelts, and approval prompts are host-owned. SP improves process safety through behavioral skills (TDD, debugging, worktrees, verification) — not kernel-level confinement.

Architecture

flowchart LR
  subgraph SP["Superpowers (behavioral)"]
    TDD["test-driven-development"]
    Debug["systematic-debugging"]
    WT["using-git-worktrees"]
    Verify["verification-before-completion"]
    Finish["finishing-a-development-branch"]
  end

  subgraph Host["Host (enforcement)"]
    Sandbox["OS sandbox / seatbelt"]
    Approval["Exec approval prompts"]
    Policy["allow/deny rules"]
    Shell["Shell/PTY execution"]
  end

  SP -->|skill text instructs| Agent["Agent"]
  Agent --> Shell
  Shell --> Sandbox
  Sandbox --> Policy
  Policy --> Approval

1. Ownership boundary table

Area Owner SP mechanism
Skill files & workflow prose Superpowers skills/*/SKILL.md
Bootstrap context injection Superpowers hooks / plugin
Shell/file/tool execution Host Native tools
Sandbox (landlock/seatbelt/etc.) Host Codex, Claude, etc.
Network egress policy Host —
Command allowlists Host —
Subagent isolation Host Best-effort
Git worktree isolation Git + skill guidance Not OS sandbox
"Don't skip TDD" discipline Superpowers skill Prompt-only

Absences verified: no seatbelt, bwrap, seccomp, landlock, chroot, firewall, or policy evaluator code in 162_superpowers/.

2. Codex plugin capabilities — not sandbox policy

.codex-plugin/plugin.json:30-34:

"capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"]

Marketplace capability labels for plugin listing — not exec policy rules. No sandbox configuration in plugin JSON.

3. Host exec policy delegation

3.1 Codex App sandbox constraints (codex-tools.md:49-59)

Documented as external host behavior in skill reference:

When the sandbox blocks branch/push operations (detached HEAD in externally managed worktree)...

Agent workflow:

  • Commit work locally
  • Inform user to use App UI: "Create branch" or "Hand off to local"
  • Still run tests, stage, suggest branch name / PR description

SP documents adaptation; Codex App enforces sandbox.

3.2 Environment detection before mutating git

codex-tools.md:34-47 — read-only git introspection:

GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)

Signals:

  • GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON → linked worktree (skip create)
  • BRANCH empty → detached HEAD (branch/push blocked in sandbox)

Used by using-git-worktrees Step 0 and finishing-a-development-branch Step 1.

4. using-git-worktrees — sandbox interaction

skills/using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md

4.1 Native-first (:47-57)

Prefer host native worktree tools (EnterWorktree, /worktree, --worktree) over manual git worktree add — avoids phantom state harness cannot manage.

4.2 Sandbox fallback (:112)

If `git worktree add` fails with a permission error (sandbox denial), tell the user
the sandbox blocked worktree creation and you're working in the current directory instead.

Explicit fail-soft: report sandbox block; continue in place — not bypass.

4.3 Safety verification (:85-97)

Project-local .worktrees/ must be gitignored before creation — prevents accidental commit of worktree contents. Behavioral guard, not sandbox.

4.4 Submodule guard (:26-31)

GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON also true in submodules — must check git rev-parse --show-superproject-working-tree before skipping worktree creation.

5. Behavioral safety skills (prompt-enforced)

5.1 test-driven-development

Iron Law (SKILL.md:33-35):

NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST

Delete any code written before tests (:37-45). Reduces reckless edits; does not restrict shell commands.

5.2 systematic-debugging

Iron Law (SKILL.md:18-20):

NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST

Four phases before fixes. Under time pressure — still mandatory (:34-44).

Techniques in tree: root-cause-tracing, defense-in-depth, condition-based-waiting — investigation discipline, not syscall filtering.

5.3 verification-before-completion

Ensures fixes verified with evidence before claiming done — reduces false "fixed" reports.

5.4 requesting-code-review / subagent-driven-development

Two-stage review (spec then quality) — prevents shipping spec violations; enforced by workflow, not permissions.

Red flags (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:236-250): skip reviews, parallel implementers, proceed on main without consent.

5.5 finishing-a-development-branch

Structured merge/PR/discard — coordinates git operations within host sandbox limits.

6. Brainstorm server — local network exposure

server.cjs:76-78:

const PORT = process.env.BRAINSTORM_PORT || (49152 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 16383));
const HOST = process.env.BRAINSTORM_HOST || '127.0.0.1';

Default localhost bind — not a sandbox, but reduces exposure. Remote setups: --host 0.0.0.0 --url-host localhost (visual-companion.md:83-92).

Agent instructed to use Write tool for HTML — not shell heredoc (visual-companion.md:100) — reduces shell injection noise, not security boundary.

Inactivity auto-shutdown ~30 min — resource limit, not policy engine.

7. Hooks and plugins — no exec side effects

Component Exec impact
hooks/session-start Reads SKILL.md, prints JSON — no subprocess spawn of user code
.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js Config + message transform only
hooks/hooks.json Invokes session-start via run-hook.cmd

No approval evaluator in hook chain.

8. OpenCode / Copilot / Gemini — policy external

  • OpenCode: skill path registration only — exec policy is OpenCode core
  • Copilot: bash with optional async: true — async session tools documented in copilot-tools.md:22-32; approval is Copilot's
  • Gemini: run_shell_command — Gemini CLI policy

Superpowers maps tool names; never intercepts command strings.

9. Subagent exec isolation

SP requires fresh subagent per task with curated prompt (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:10-11) — limits context leakage, not syscall access.

Workers inherit host sandbox of subagent runtime. SP cannot restrict subagent shell beyond host rules.

Forbidden: parallel implementers (:242) — reduces file conflict race, not security ACL.

10. What README claims vs exec

README.md:9-19 describes methodology (brainstorm → plan → SDD → review) — no mention of OS sandboxing, approvals, or network policy.

Philosophy (:199-203): TDD, systematic process, simplicity, evidence — all behavioral.

11. Parity matrix vs Codex (co) sandbox

Concern Codex (co) Superpowers (sp)
OS sandbox seatbelt/landlock in codex-core None
Approval modes ask-on-failure, never, etc. Host
Network filter Codex policy tables Host
Workspace-write vs read-only Codex flags Host
Exec policy TOML ~/.codex/config.toml Not SP
Detached HEAD handling Documented in codex-tools.md Skill adaptation text
TDD enforcement User/skills test-driven-development skill
Worktree isolation Git + Codex App using-git-worktrees skill

12. Failure modes and reporting obligations

Skills mandate reporting sandbox/precondition failures verbatim (AGENTS-style), not silent fallback:

  • Worktree permission denied → tell user (using-git-worktrees)
  • Detached HEAD blocks push → Codex App UI handoff (codex-tools.md)
  • BLOCKED subagent → escalate, don't retry blindly (subagent-driven-development)

Aligns with "fail fast" orchestration — behavioral analog to exec policy deny.

13. Evidence quality bar

  • Negative claim: no SP-owned sandbox engine
  • Codex App detached HEAD workflow cited
  • Worktree sandbox fallback documented
  • Behavioral skills indexed (TDD, debug, verify)
  • Brainstorm server bind defaults
  • Hooks/plugins no exec side effects
  • Parity table vs Codex sandbox

14. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
