Change history

date change
2026-06-27 WP52: ≥12 KB — ownership table, worktree fallback, behavioral skills, Codex App
2026-06-27 WP38: source citations + corpus links
2026-05-31 Initial sp sandbox axis stub

15. executing-plans vs SDD — human gate as exec policy analog

executing-plans skill activates when plan execution happens in a parallel session with human checkpoints between batches — an explicit human-in-the-loop approval between tool bursts. SDD (subagent-driven-development) removes inter-task human gates (:14) relying instead on automated reviewer subagents.

Neither mode changes OS sandbox; the difference is who approves continuation (human vs reviewer Task).

Mode Approval between tasks Isolation
executing-plans Human checkpoint Separate session
subagent-driven-development Spec + quality subagents Same session, fresh subagent context
Manual (no skill) Ad hoc User responsibility

15.1 Git operations under sandbox — finishing branch

finishing-a-development-branch coordinates test verification before merge/PR/discard. When host sandbox blocks git push or branch creation, skill defers to Codex App UI paths documented in codex-tools.md:49-59 — agent still allowed: stage, commit, run tests, emit suggested commands for user copy-paste.

15.2 No SP override of host "never" rules

Instruction priority (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26) ensures user AGENTS.md beats skills. If user configures host exec policy or rules file saying "never run destructive commands," Superpowers skills cannot override — behavioral TDD does not trump explicit user deny rules.

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot v5.1.0 Current
Codex sandbox API Described via codex-tools.md Re-verify on Codex release
App handoff UI "Create branch" / "Hand off to local" App UI strings may change
Native worktree tools Host-specific names in skill New harness flags may appear
Brainstorm bind 127.0.0.1 default Unchanged unless server.cjs edits
Human checkpoint mode executing-plans vs SDD documented Skill text may converge on proportionality gates

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_sp_sandbox.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers — Sandbox / Exec Policy

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers is a skills/bootstrap package, not an execution harness. It does not enforce OS process isolation, command approval UI, filesystem ACLs, network filtering, PTY sandboxing, or shell policy evaluation. Actual execution, sandbox seatbelts, and approval prompts are host-owned. SP improves process safety through behavioral skills (TDD, debugging, worktrees, verification) — not kernel-level confinement.

Architecture

flowchart LR
  subgraph SP["Superpowers (behavioral)"]
    TDD["test-driven-development"]
    Debug["systematic-debugging"]
    WT["using-git-worktrees"]
    Verify["verification-before-completion"]
    Finish["finishing-a-development-branch"]
  end

  subgraph Host["Host (enforcement)"]
    Sandbox["OS sandbox / seatbelt"]
    Approval["Exec approval prompts"]
    Policy["allow/deny rules"]
    Shell["Shell/PTY execution"]
  end

  SP -->|skill text instructs| Agent["Agent"]
  Agent --> Shell
  Shell --> Sandbox
  Sandbox --> Policy
  Policy --> Approval

1. Ownership boundary table

Area Owner SP mechanism
Skill files & workflow prose Superpowers skills/*/SKILL.md
Bootstrap context injection Superpowers hooks / plugin
Shell/file/tool execution Host Native tools
Sandbox (landlock/seatbelt/etc.) Host Codex, Claude, etc.
Network egress policy Host —
Command allowlists Host —
Subagent isolation Host Best-effort
Git worktree isolation Git + skill guidance Not OS sandbox
"Don't skip TDD" discipline Superpowers skill Prompt-only

Absences verified: no seatbelt, bwrap, seccomp, landlock, chroot, firewall, or policy evaluator code in 162_superpowers/.

2. Codex plugin capabilities — not sandbox policy

.codex-plugin/plugin.json:30-34:

"capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"]

Marketplace capability labels for plugin listing — not exec policy rules. No sandbox configuration in plugin JSON.

3. Host exec policy delegation

3.1 Codex App sandbox constraints (codex-tools.md:49-59)

Documented as external host behavior in skill reference:

When the sandbox blocks branch/push operations (detached HEAD in externally managed worktree)...

Agent workflow:

  • Commit work locally
  • Inform user to use App UI: "Create branch" or "Hand off to local"
  • Still run tests, stage, suggest branch name / PR description

SP documents adaptation; Codex App enforces sandbox.

3.2 Environment detection before mutating git

codex-tools.md:34-47 — read-only git introspection:

GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)

Signals:

  • GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON → linked worktree (skip create)
  • BRANCH empty → detached HEAD (branch/push blocked in sandbox)

Used by using-git-worktrees Step 0 and finishing-a-development-branch Step 1.

4. using-git-worktrees — sandbox interaction

skills/using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md

4.1 Native-first (:47-57)

Prefer host native worktree tools (EnterWorktree, /worktree, --worktree) over manual git worktree add — avoids phantom state harness cannot manage.

4.2 Sandbox fallback (:112)

If `git worktree add` fails with a permission error (sandbox denial), tell the user
the sandbox blocked worktree creation and you're working in the current directory instead.

Explicit fail-soft: report sandbox block; continue in place — not bypass.

4.3 Safety verification (:85-97)

Project-local .worktrees/ must be gitignored before creation — prevents accidental commit of worktree contents. Behavioral guard, not sandbox.

4.4 Submodule guard (:26-31)

GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON also true in submodules — must check git rev-parse --show-superproject-working-tree before skipping worktree creation.

5. Behavioral safety skills (prompt-enforced)

5.1 test-driven-development

Iron Law (SKILL.md:33-35):

NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST

Delete any code written before tests (:37-45). Reduces reckless edits; does not restrict shell commands.

5.2 systematic-debugging

Iron Law (SKILL.md:18-20):

NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST

Four phases before fixes. Under time pressure — still mandatory (:34-44).

Techniques in tree: root-cause-tracing, defense-in-depth, condition-based-waiting — investigation discipline, not syscall filtering.

5.3 verification-before-completion

Ensures fixes verified with evidence before claiming done — reduces false "fixed" reports.

5.4 requesting-code-review / subagent-driven-development

Two-stage review (spec then quality) — prevents shipping spec violations; enforced by workflow, not permissions.

Red flags (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:236-250): skip reviews, parallel implementers, proceed on main without consent.

5.5 finishing-a-development-branch

Structured merge/PR/discard — coordinates git operations within host sandbox limits.

6. Brainstorm server — local network exposure

server.cjs:76-78:

const PORT = process.env.BRAINSTORM_PORT || (49152 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 16383));
const HOST = process.env.BRAINSTORM_HOST || '127.0.0.1';

Default localhost bind — not a sandbox, but reduces exposure. Remote setups: --host 0.0.0.0 --url-host localhost (visual-companion.md:83-92).

Agent instructed to use Write tool for HTML — not shell heredoc (visual-companion.md:100) — reduces shell injection noise, not security boundary.

Inactivity auto-shutdown ~30 min — resource limit, not policy engine.

7. Hooks and plugins — no exec side effects

Component Exec impact
hooks/session-start Reads SKILL.md, prints JSON — no subprocess spawn of user code
.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js Config + message transform only
hooks/hooks.json Invokes session-start via run-hook.cmd

No approval evaluator in hook chain.

8. OpenCode / Copilot / Gemini — policy external

  • OpenCode: skill path registration only — exec policy is OpenCode core
  • Copilot: bash with optional async: true — async session tools documented in copilot-tools.md:22-32; approval is Copilot's
  • Gemini: run_shell_command — Gemini CLI policy

Superpowers maps tool names; never intercepts command strings.

9. Subagent exec isolation

SP requires fresh subagent per task with curated prompt (subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:10-11) — limits context leakage, not syscall access.

Workers inherit host sandbox of subagent runtime. SP cannot restrict subagent shell beyond host rules.

Forbidden: parallel implementers (:242) — reduces file conflict race, not security ACL.

10. What README claims vs exec

README.md:9-19 describes methodology (brainstorm → plan → SDD → review) — no mention of OS sandboxing, approvals, or network policy.

Philosophy (:199-203): TDD, systematic process, simplicity, evidence — all behavioral.

11. Parity matrix vs Codex (co) sandbox

Concern Codex (co) Superpowers (sp)
OS sandbox seatbelt/landlock in codex-core None
Approval modes ask-on-failure, never, etc. Host
Network filter Codex policy tables Host
Workspace-write vs read-only Codex flags Host
Exec policy TOML ~/.codex/config.toml Not SP
Detached HEAD handling Documented in codex-tools.md Skill adaptation text
TDD enforcement User/skills test-driven-development skill
Worktree isolation Git + Codex App using-git-worktrees skill

12. Failure modes and reporting obligations

Skills mandate reporting sandbox/precondition failures verbatim (AGENTS-style), not silent fallback:

  • Worktree permission denied → tell user (using-git-worktrees)
  • Detached HEAD blocks push → Codex App UI handoff (codex-tools.md)
  • BLOCKED subagent → escalate, don't retry blindly (subagent-driven-development)

Aligns with "fail fast" orchestration — behavioral analog to exec policy deny.

13. Evidence quality bar

  • Negative claim: no SP-owned sandbox engine
  • Codex App detached HEAD workflow cited
  • Worktree sandbox fallback documented
  • Behavioral skills indexed (TDD, debug, verify)
  • Brainstorm server bind defaults
  • Hooks/plugins no exec side effects
  • Parity table vs Codex sandbox

15. executing-plans vs SDD — human gate as exec policy analog

executing-plans skill activates when plan execution happens in a parallel session with human checkpoints between batches — an explicit human-in-the-loop approval between tool bursts. SDD (subagent-driven-development) removes inter-task human gates (:14) relying instead on automated reviewer subagents.

Neither mode changes OS sandbox; the difference is who approves continuation (human vs reviewer Task).

Mode Approval between tasks Isolation
executing-plans Human checkpoint Separate session
subagent-driven-development Spec + quality subagents Same session, fresh subagent context
Manual (no skill) Ad hoc User responsibility

15.1 Git operations under sandbox — finishing branch

finishing-a-development-branch coordinates test verification before merge/PR/discard. When host sandbox blocks git push or branch creation, skill defers to Codex App UI paths documented in codex-tools.md:49-59 — agent still allowed: stage, commit, run tests, emit suggested commands for user copy-paste.

15.2 No SP override of host "never" rules

Instruction priority (using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26) ensures user AGENTS.md beats skills. If user configures host exec policy or rules file saying "never run destructive commands," Superpowers skills cannot override — behavioral TDD does not trump explicit user deny rules.

Axis 08: Config system

AXIS

008_config-system/08_sp_config.md

Overview

Axis 08: Config system — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers는 중앙 settings.json 없이 호스트 manifest + bootstrap 훅 + OpenCode JS config 훅으로 설정을 분산한다. 인용: 162_superpowers/<path>:<line>.

  • graph TD
  • MAN["Host manifests"] --> SKILLS["skills/ corpus"]
  • HOOK["hooks/session-start"] --> BOOT["using-superpowers"]
  • OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"] --> PATHS["config.skills.paths"]
  • GEM["GEMINI.md"] --> BOOT
  • PRI["Instruction Priority SKILL.md:18-26"] --> AGENT["Agent behavior"]
  • | Harness | 파일 | Superpowers가 설정하는 것 |
  • | Codex | .codex-plugin/plugin.json | skills: "./skills/" (:23), interface UI (:24-45) |
  • | Claude | .claude-plugin/plugin.json, hooks/hooks.json | metadata; SessionStart (hooks.json:3-14) |
  • | Cursor | .cursor-plugin/plugin.json | skills/agents/commands/hooks paths (:21-24) |
  • | Gemini | gemini-extension.json, GEMINI.md | contextFileName (gemini-extension.json:5); @ imports (GEMINI.md:1-2) |

Main analysis

Superpowers Config System — Source Analysis

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers distributes settings via host manifest + bootstrap hook + OpenCode JS config hook without a central settings.json. Citation: 162_superpowers/<path>:<line>.

graph TD
    MAN["Host manifests"] --> SKILLS["skills/ corpus"]
    HOOK["hooks/session-start"] --> BOOT["using-superpowers"]
    OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"] --> PATHS["config.skills.paths"]
    GEM["GEMINI.md"] --> BOOT
    PRI["Instruction Priority SKILL.md:18-26"] --> AGENT["Agent behavior"]

1. Config Surface by host

Harness file What Superpowers Set
Codex .codex-plugin/plugin.json skills: "./skills/" (:23), interface UI (:24-45)
Claude .claude-plugin/plugin.json, hooks/hooks.json metadata; SessionStart (hooks.json:3-14)
Cursor .cursor-plugin/plugin.json skills/agents/commands/hooks paths (:21-24)
Gemini gemini-extension.json, GEMINI.md contextFileName (gemini-extension.json:5); @ imports (GEMINI.md:1-2)
OpenCode package.json:5, .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js main plugin; runtime path append
Copilot hooks/session-start additionalContext SDK format (session-start:52-54)

1.1 Codex manifest

162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:1-47 — version 5.1.0, keywords brainstorming/TDD/debugging (:13-21), no mcpServers, model routing, or auth keys.

Subagent dispatch requires host config documented in skills/using-superpowers/references/codex-tools.md:18-24:

[features]
multi_agent = true

Legacy note (codex-tools.md:27-30): wait_agent vs code-mode exec/wait — SP documents Codex tool name drift.

1.2 Claude manifest

162_superpowers/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:1-19 — metadata only. Skills loading follows Claude Code plugin convention (plugin-root skills/), not an explicit JSON key like Codex.

hooks/hooks.json:1-15 registers only SessionStart with matcher startup|clear|compact — no PreToolUse, no config mutation hooks.

1.3 Cursor manifest

162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json:21-24:

"skills": "./skills/",
"agents": "./agents/",
"commands": "./commands/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks-cursor.json"

Widest manifest surface among SP adapters.

1.4 Gemini

162_superpowers/gemini-extension.json:1-6 — minimal: name, version, contextFileName: "GEMINI.md".

GEMINI.md:1-2:

@./skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md
@./skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md

Gemini loads bootstrap + tool mapping at session context assembly — no separate SP config file.

1.5 OpenCode entry

162_superpowers/package.json:1-7 — "type": "module", "main": ".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js".

2. OpenCode Runtime Config (in-repo mutation)

162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js (135 lines).

2.1 Skills path injection (:103-108)

config: async (config) => {
  config.skills = config.skills || {};
  config.skills.paths = config.skills.paths || [];
  if (!config.skills.paths.includes(superpowersSkillsDir)) {
    config.skills.paths.push(superpowersSkillsDir);
  }
},
  • superpowersSkillsDir = path.resolve(__dirname, '../../skills') (:57)
  • Additive — never removes user paths
  • Relies on OpenCode Config singleton cache (:101-102 comment)

2.2 Bootstrap module cache (:49-96)

_bootstrapCache (:53) — undefined=not loaded, null=missing file.

getBootstrapContent():

  1. Read skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md (:67-71)
  2. extractAndStripFrontmatter (:16-34) — simple line parser, no yaml dependency
  3. Append OpenCode tool mapping (:76-83): TodoWrite→todowrite, Task→@mention subagents, Skill→native skill tool
  4. Wrap in <EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT> (:85-93)

Performance: eliminates redundant fs+regex per agent step (#1202).

2.3 Message transform (:120-133)

experimental.chat.messages.transform:

  • Finds first user message (:123-124)
  • Skips if EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT already present (:129)
  • Unshifts bootstrap text before first part (:131-132)

Rationale (:111-114): avoid system-message token bloat (#750) and Qwen multi-system breakage (#894).

2.4 Path normalization (:37-47)

normalizePath(p, homeDir) — trim, ~/ expand, path.resolve.

OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR override (:58-59).

3. SessionStart Hook

162_superpowers/hooks/session-start (bash).

3.1 Legacy deprecation (:11-14)

If $HOME/.config/superpowers/skills exists → inject warning to move skills to ~/.claude/skills.

3.2 JSON platform branches (:46-55)

Platform JSON field
Cursor (CURSOR_PLUGIN_ROOT) additional_context
Claude Code hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext
Copilot CLI / default additionalContext

Uses printf not heredoc — bash 5.3+ hang (#571, :44-45).

3.3 Content

Full using-superpowers/SKILL.md embedded in session context (:18, :35) — differs from OpenCode which strips frontmatter.

4. Instruction Priority (behavioral config)

162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26:

  1. User instructions (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md) — highest
  2. Superpowers skills — override defaults
  3. Default system prompt — lowest

Skill-before-action rule (:10-16, :46): invoke skills before clarifying questions.

Platform adaptation (:38-40): non-CC hosts read references/codex-tools.md, copilot-tools.md, auto-loaded gemini-tools.md.

Red flags table (:80-95) — rationalization patterns that mean "stop and invoke skill".

Skill priority order (:99-105): process skills (brainstorming, debugging) before implementation skills.

5. Sync Script Config Boundaries

162_superpowers/scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:39-77 — EXCLUDES for Codex marketplace sync:

Anchored patterns (:41-43) prevent accidental exclusion of skills/brainstorming/scripts/.

Excluded host adapters: .claude-plugin/, .cursor-plugin/, .opencode/, hooks/, GEMINI.md, gemini-extension.json.

Destination: prime-radiant-inc/openai-codex-plugins fork, path plugins/superpowers (:35-37).

Bootstrap mode (:23-25, :23): --bootstrap creates plugin dir if missing.

6. Verification Tests

Test Path
Bootstrap cache tests/opencode/test-bootstrap-caching.mjs
Plugin load tests/opencode/test-plugin-loading.sh
Priority tests/opencode/test-priority.sh
Tools mapping tests/opencode/test-tools.sh
Codex sync tests/codex-plugin-sync/test-sync-to-codex-plugin.sh

docs/testing.md:36-38 — integration tests need dev marketplace: "superpowers@superpowers-dev": true.

7. Absences

Config type Status
settings.json / .superpowersrc absent
SP-owned model routing absent
SP-owned auth absent
Feature flag engine absent — host flags only
Permission/sandbox config absent — host-owned
Deep merge engine absent — additive paths only

8. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
Config files config.toml, managed host files
Skills discovery config [skills] paths manifest + OpenCode hook
Bootstrap host prompt SessionStart + OpenCode transform
Sync target receives SP rsync upstream source

Source citations (162_superpowers)

Topic Citation
Codex skills path .codex-plugin/plugin.json:23
OpenCode config hook .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:103-108
Bootstrap cache .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:49-96
Message transform .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:120-133
Legacy skills warning hooks/session-start:11-14
Instruction priority skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26
Gemini bootstrap GEMINI.md:1-2
SessionStart hooks only hooks/hooks.json:3-14
Sync excludes scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:45-77
Codex multi_agent skills/using-superpowers/references/codex-tools.md:18-24

Appendix A — session-start line-by-line map

Line Content
hooks/session-start:6-8 Resolve SCRIPT_DIR, PLUGIN_ROOT from hook location
hooks/session-start:11-14 Build legacy ~/.config/superpowers/skills warning if dir exists
hooks/session-start:17-18 cat skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md into variable
hooks/session-start:23-30 escape_for_json — backslash, quote, newline, CR, tab
hooks/session-start:35 Assemble session_context with EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT wrapper
hooks/session-start:46-48 Cursor branch: additional_context top-level JSON
hooks/session-start:49-51 Claude Code: nested hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext
hooks/session-start:52-54 Copilot/default: additionalContext SDK standard
hooks/session-start:57 exit 0 — hook must not fail session start

Appendix B — OpenCode normalizePath edge cases

162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:37-47:

  • Empty string → null (:39-40)
  • ~/foo → path.join(homeDir, 'foo') (:41-42)
  • ~ alone → homeDir (:43-44)
  • Otherwise path.resolve(normalized) (:46)

OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR (:58) overrides default ~/.config/opencode (:59).

From 162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:24-45:

Field Value / role
displayName "Superpowers"
shortDescription Planning/TDD/debugging workflows
longDescription Full workflow enumeration
developerName Jesse Vincent
category Coding
capabilities Interactive, Read, Write
defaultPrompt Two seed prompts for new sessions
websiteURL github.com/obra/superpowers
privacyPolicyURL GitHub privacy statement
termsOfServiceURL GitHub terms
brandColor #F59E0B
composerIcon ./assets/superpowers-small.svg
logo ./assets/app-icon.png

None of these fields configure model, tools, or MCP — pure marketplace presentation.

Appendix D — README install surfaces (README.md:31-100 region)

162_superpowers/README.md documents per-harness install:

  • Claude Code: /plugin install superpowers@claude-plugins-official (:44)
  • Codex CLI: plugin marketplace path (see README Codex section)
  • OpenCode: .opencode/INSTALL.md reference
  • Cursor: plugin install via Cursor marketplace
  • Gemini: extension install
  • Copilot CLI: plugin path

Install is per harness — no unified SP installer (README.md:33).

File under 162_superpowers/tests/ Config aspect tested
opencode/test-bootstrap-caching.mjs _bootstrapCache single-read
opencode/test-plugin-loading.sh Plugin module loads
opencode/test-priority.sh User > skill > default priority
opencode/test-tools.sh OpenCode tool name substitution
codex-plugin-sync/test-sync-to-codex-plugin.sh EXCLUDES rsync boundaries

explicit-skill-requests/run-test.sh Explicit skill invocation

Appendix F — using-superpowers dot graph nodes (SKILL.md:48-75)

The skill_flow digraph defines mandatory routing:

  • User message received → Might any skill apply?
  • Yes → Invoke Skill tool → announce usage → checklist TodoWrite → follow skill
  • About to EnterPlanMode? → brainstorm check branch
  • Red flag: skipping skill check before clarifying questions

This graph is behavioral config embedded in skill text, not JSON.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 008_config-system/08_sp_config.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers Config System — Source Analysis

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ (v5.1.0). Superpowers는 중앙 settings.json 없이 호스트 manifest + bootstrap 훅 + OpenCode JS config 훅으로 설정을 분산한다. 인용: 162_superpowers/<path>:<line>.

graph TD
    MAN["Host manifests"] --> SKILLS["skills/ corpus"]
    HOOK["hooks/session-start"] --> BOOT["using-superpowers"]
    OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"] --> PATHS["config.skills.paths"]
    GEM["GEMINI.md"] --> BOOT
    PRI["Instruction Priority SKILL.md:18-26"] --> AGENT["Agent behavior"]

1. 호스트별 Config Surface

Harness 파일 Superpowers가 설정하는 것
Codex .codex-plugin/plugin.json skills: "./skills/" (:23), interface UI (:24-45)
Claude .claude-plugin/plugin.json, hooks/hooks.json metadata; SessionStart (hooks.json:3-14)
Cursor .cursor-plugin/plugin.json skills/agents/commands/hooks paths (:21-24)
Gemini gemini-extension.json, GEMINI.md contextFileName (gemini-extension.json:5); @ imports (GEMINI.md:1-2)
OpenCode package.json:5, .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js main plugin; runtime path append
Copilot hooks/session-start additionalContext SDK format (session-start:52-54)

1.1 Codex manifest

162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:1-47 — version 5.1.0, keywords brainstorming/TDD/debugging (:13-21), no mcpServers, model routing, or auth keys.

Subagent dispatch requires host config documented in skills/using-superpowers/references/codex-tools.md:18-24:

[features]
multi_agent = true

Legacy note (codex-tools.md:27-30): wait_agent vs code-mode exec/wait — SP documents Codex tool name drift.

1.2 Claude manifest

162_superpowers/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:1-19 — metadata only. Skills loading follows Claude Code plugin convention (plugin-root skills/), not an explicit JSON key like Codex.

hooks/hooks.json:1-15 registers only SessionStart with matcher startup|clear|compact — no PreToolUse, no config mutation hooks.

1.3 Cursor manifest

162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json:21-24:

"skills": "./skills/",
"agents": "./agents/",
"commands": "./commands/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks-cursor.json"

Widest manifest surface among SP adapters.

1.4 Gemini

162_superpowers/gemini-extension.json:1-6 — minimal: name, version, contextFileName: "GEMINI.md".

GEMINI.md:1-2:

@./skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md
@./skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md

Gemini loads bootstrap + tool mapping at session context assembly — no separate SP config file.

1.5 OpenCode entry

162_superpowers/package.json:1-7 — "type": "module", "main": ".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js".

2. OpenCode Runtime Config (in-repo mutation)

162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js (135 lines).

2.1 Skills path injection (:103-108)

config: async (config) => {
  config.skills = config.skills || {};
  config.skills.paths = config.skills.paths || [];
  if (!config.skills.paths.includes(superpowersSkillsDir)) {
    config.skills.paths.push(superpowersSkillsDir);
  }
},
  • superpowersSkillsDir = path.resolve(__dirname, '../../skills') (:57)
  • Additive — never removes user paths
  • Relies on OpenCode Config singleton cache (:101-102 comment)

2.2 Bootstrap module cache (:49-96)

_bootstrapCache (:53) — undefined=not loaded, null=missing file.

getBootstrapContent():

  1. Read skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md (:67-71)
  2. extractAndStripFrontmatter (:16-34) — simple line parser, no yaml dependency
  3. Append OpenCode tool mapping (:76-83): TodoWrite→todowrite, Task→@mention subagents, Skill→native skill tool
  4. Wrap in <EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT> (:85-93)

Performance: eliminates redundant fs+regex per agent step (#1202).

2.3 Message transform (:120-133)

experimental.chat.messages.transform:

  • Finds first user message (:123-124)
  • Skips if EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT already present (:129)
  • Unshifts bootstrap text before first part (:131-132)

Rationale (:111-114): avoid system-message token bloat (#750) and Qwen multi-system breakage (#894).

2.4 Path normalization (:37-47)

normalizePath(p, homeDir) — trim, ~/ expand, path.resolve.

OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR override (:58-59).

3. SessionStart Hook

162_superpowers/hooks/session-start (bash).

3.1 Legacy deprecation (:11-14)

If $HOME/.config/superpowers/skills exists → inject warning to move skills to ~/.claude/skills.

3.2 JSON platform branches (:46-55)

Platform JSON field
Cursor (CURSOR_PLUGIN_ROOT) additional_context
Claude Code hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext
Copilot CLI / default additionalContext

Uses printf not heredoc — bash 5.3+ hang (#571, :44-45).

3.3 Content

Full using-superpowers/SKILL.md embedded in session context (:18, :35) — differs from OpenCode which strips frontmatter.

4. Instruction Priority (behavioral config)

162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26:

  1. User instructions (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md) — highest
  2. Superpowers skills — override defaults
  3. Default system prompt — lowest

Skill-before-action rule (:10-16, :46): invoke skills before clarifying questions.

Platform adaptation (:38-40): non-CC hosts read references/codex-tools.md, copilot-tools.md, auto-loaded gemini-tools.md.

Red flags table (:80-95) — rationalization patterns that mean "stop and invoke skill".

Skill priority order (:99-105): process skills (brainstorming, debugging) before implementation skills.

5. Sync Script Config Boundaries

162_superpowers/scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:39-77 — EXCLUDES for Codex marketplace sync:

Anchored patterns (:41-43) prevent accidental exclusion of skills/brainstorming/scripts/.

Excluded host adapters: .claude-plugin/, .cursor-plugin/, .opencode/, hooks/, GEMINI.md, gemini-extension.json.

Destination: prime-radiant-inc/openai-codex-plugins fork, path plugins/superpowers (:35-37).

Bootstrap mode (:23-25, :23): --bootstrap creates plugin dir if missing.

6. Verification Tests

Test Path
Bootstrap cache tests/opencode/test-bootstrap-caching.mjs
Plugin load tests/opencode/test-plugin-loading.sh
Priority tests/opencode/test-priority.sh
Tools mapping tests/opencode/test-tools.sh
Codex sync tests/codex-plugin-sync/test-sync-to-codex-plugin.sh

docs/testing.md:36-38 — integration tests need dev marketplace: "superpowers@superpowers-dev": true.

7. Absences

Config type Status
settings.json / .superpowersrc absent
SP-owned model routing absent
SP-owned auth absent
Feature flag engine absent — host flags only
Permission/sandbox config absent — host-owned
Deep merge engine absent — additive paths only

8. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
Config files config.toml, managed host files
Skills discovery config [skills] paths manifest + OpenCode hook
Bootstrap host prompt SessionStart + OpenCode transform
Sync target receives SP rsync upstream source

Legacy skills warning hooks/session-start:11-14
Instruction priority skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26

SessionStart hooks only hooks/hooks.json:3-14
Sync excludes scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:45-77
Codex multi_agent skills/using-superpowers/references/codex-tools.md:18-24

25

Axis 09: MCP orchestration

AXIS

009_mcp-orchestration/09_sp_mcp.md

Overview

Axis 09: MCP orchestration — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers는 MCP-native가 아니다 — MCP server/client manager, mcpServers manifest, transport, OAuth, tool listing runtime을 구현하지 않는다. MCP는 skill 작성 가이드와 호스트 MCP 도구 참조로만 등장한다.

  • graph LR
  • SP["Superpowers skills/"] --> HOST["Host harness"]
  • HOST --> MCP["Host MCP servers(Codex config, Claude MCP, Copilot github-mcp)"]
  • SP -.->|"anthropic-best-practices.md:1053"| GUIDE["MCP authoring: ServerName:tool_name"]
  • SP --> NATIVE["Host-native toolsspawn_agent, Task, @generalist"]
  • | Area | Finding | Evidence |
  • | MCP server in SP repo | None | no mcpServers in any manifest |
  • | MCP client/transport | None | no rust/js MCP implementation |
  • | Codex integration | skills manifest only | .codex-plugin/plugin.json:23 |
  • | Claude integration | SessionStart hook | hooks/hooks.json:3-14 |
  • | OpenCode | skills path + bootstrap | .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:103-133 |

Topics covered

    1. Executive Summary
    1. Manifest Negative Evidence
  • 2.1 Codex (162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json)
  • 2.2 Claude (162_superpowers/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:1-19)
  • 2.3 Cursor (162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json)
  • 2.4 Gemini (162_superpowers/gemini-extension.json:1-6)
  • 2.5 OpenCode (162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js)
    1. MCP References Inside Skills (not implementation)
  • 3.1 Skill authoring (writing-skills/anthropic-best-practices.md:1053-1071)
  • MCP tool references
  • 3.2 Copilot GitHub MCP (copilot-tools.md:42)
  • 3.3 brainstorming terminal state (brainstorming/SKILL.md:66)
  • 3.4 TDD anti-pattern mock (test-driven-development/testing-anti-patterns.md:144)
    1. Host-Native Orchestration (MCP-adjacent)

Source code snippets

graph LR

Example: BigQuery:bigquery_schema, GitHub:create_issue

Main analysis

Superpowers MCP Orchestration — Source Analysis

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers is not MCP-native — it does not implement the MCP server/client manager, mcpServers manifest, transport, OAuth, or tool listing runtime. MCP only appears in the skill authoring guide and host MCP tool reference.

graph LR
    SP["Superpowers skills/"] --> HOST["Host harness"]
    HOST --> MCP["Host MCP servers<br/>(Codex config, Claude MCP, Copilot github-mcp)"]
    SP -.->|"anthropic-best-practices.md:1053"| GUIDE["MCP authoring: ServerName:tool_name"]
    SP --> NATIVE["Host-native tools<br/>spawn_agent, Task, @generalist"]

1. Executive Summary

Area Finding Evidence
MCP server in SP repo None no mcpServers in any manifest
MCP client/transport None no rust/js MCP implementation
Codex integration skills manifest only .codex-plugin/plugin.json:23
Claude integration SessionStart hook hooks/hooks.json:3-14
OpenCode skills path + bootstrap .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:103-133
Orchestration model prompt/skill → host tools subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md
MCP in skill text authoring guidance only writing-skills/anthropic-best-practices.md:1053-1071

2. Manifest Negative Evidence

2.1 Codex (162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json)

Keys present: name, version, skills, interface (:1-47). Keys absent: mcpServers, connectors, tools, permissions.

2.2 Claude (162_superpowers/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:1-19)

Metadata only — no MCP declaration.

2.3 Cursor (162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json)

Declares skills, agents, commands, hooks — not MCP.

2.4 Gemini (162_superpowers/gemini-extension.json:1-6)

contextFileName only.

2.5 OpenCode (162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js)

Mutates config.skills.paths (:103-108) and chat messages (:120-133) — no MCP registration.

3. MCP References Inside Skills (not implementation)

3.1 Skill authoring (writing-skills/anthropic-best-practices.md:1053-1071)

### MCP tool references
Format: ServerName:tool_name
Example: BigQuery:bigquery_schema, GitHub:create_issue

Purpose: avoid "tool not found" when host exposes multiple MCP servers. SP does not implement BigQuery or GitHub servers — documents naming for skill authors.

3.2 Copilot GitHub MCP (copilot-tools.md:42)

Lists github-mcp-server-* as Copilot CLI native tools — issues, PRs, code search. SP maps skill prose to host tools; does not bundle MCP server.

3.3 brainstorming terminal state (brainstorming/SKILL.md:66)

Explicit: after brainstorming invoke writing-plans only — NOT mcp-builder or implementation skills.

3.4 TDD anti-pattern mock (test-driven-development/testing-anti-patterns.md:144)

vi.mock('MCPServerManager') — example in testing doc, not SP runtime.

4. Host-Native Orchestration (MCP-adjacent)

Superpowers orchestrates via host subagent APIs, not MCP:

4.1 Codex (references/codex-tools.md:1-60)

Skill reference Codex equivalent
Task subagent spawn_agent (:7)
Parallel Task multiple spawn_agent (:8)
Task result wait_agent (:9)
Release slot close_agent (:10)
TodoWrite update_plan (:11)
Skill native skill follow (:12)

Requires [features] multi_agent = true (:18-24).

Environment detection for worktrees (:34-47): GIT_DIR, GIT_COMMON, BRANCH bash probes.

Codex App finishing when sandbox blocks git (:49-59): commit + user uses App UI.

4.2 Gemini (references/gemini-tools.md:1-50)

Skill reference Gemini equivalent
Task @generalist or named agents (:17, :25-31)
Skill activate_skill (:14)
WebSearch google_web_search (:15) — host tool
WebFetch web_fetch (:16)
Parallel dispatch multiple @ in same prompt (:37-39)

Additional Gemini-only: save_memory, tracker_create_task, plan mode (:41-50).

4.3 Copilot (references/copilot-tools.md:1-43)

Skill reference Copilot equivalent
Task task with agent_type (:15-17)
WebFetch web_fetch (:14)
WebSearch No equivalent — web_fetch + search URL (:19)
TodoWrite sql todos table (:18)
Async shell bash async + read_bash etc. (:22-32)
Memory store_memory (:38)

4.4 OpenCode (.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:76-83)

TodoWrite → todowrite
Task → @mention subagents
Skill → native skill tool

5. Subagent-Driven Development as Orchestration Layer

162_superpowers/skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:1-80:

  • Fresh subagent per task (:10-12) — isolated context, no session pollution
  • Two-stage review: spec then quality (:8, :54-59)
  • Continuous execution — no human check-in between tasks (:14)
  • Process graph (:18-33) vs executing-plans for parallel session

Prompt templates (separate files):

  • implementer-prompt.md
  • spec-reviewer-prompt.md
  • code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md

Integration test (docs/testing.md:40-64) verifies Task tool dispatch via transcript JSONL.

5.1 dispatching-parallel-agents

Skill for independent parallel subagent tasks — maps to multiple spawn_agent / parallel @generalist per host mapping files.

5.2 executing-plans

Alternative when tasks need parallel session (worktree) vs same-session subagents.

6. OpenCode Plugin vs MCP

OpenCode hosts may register MCP separately in user config. SP plugin only:

  1. Appends skills directory
  2. Injects bootstrap on first user message

No tool schema registration, no MCP list_tools bridge in SP code.

7. Classification

Layer SP role Host role
MCP transport none stdio/http/sse
MCP tool execution none host runtime
Skill text referencing MCP yes (authoring) executes
Subagent orchestration skill protocol native APIs
Web tools via MCP documented in copilot-tools github-mcp-server-*

Superpowers = MCP-adjacent skill/plugin layer. Compare 009_mcp-orchestration/09_co_mcp.md for first-party Rust MCP stack.

8. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
MCP client codex-rs MCP stack none
Tool orchestration MCP + builtins host tools + skill mapping docs
Subagents spawn_agent etc. documents host equivalents
Plugin hook N/A OpenCode JS only

Source citations (162_superpowers)

Topic Citation
No mcpServers in Codex manifest .codex-plugin/plugin.json (full file)
MCP authoring guidance skills/writing-skills/anthropic-best-practices.md:1053-1071
Codex tool mapping skills/using-superpowers/references/codex-tools.md:1-60
Gemini tool mapping skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md:1-50
Copilot + github MCP skills/using-superpowers/references/copilot-tools.md:14-42
OpenCode mapping .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:76-83
Subagent orchestration skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:1-80
SessionStart only hook hooks/hooks.json:3-14

Appendix A — Full codex-tools.md mapping table

162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/references/codex-tools.md:5-14:

Skill references Codex equivalent Line
Task (subagent) spawn_agent :7
Multiple Task Multiple spawn_agent :8
Task returns wait_agent :9
Task completes close_agent :10
TodoWrite update_plan :11
Skill follow natively :12
Read, Write, Edit native file tools :13
Bash native shell :14

Appendix B — Full gemini-tools.md mapping table

162_superpowers/skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md:5-17:

Skill Gemini Line
Read read_file :7
Write write_file :8
Edit replace :9
Bash run_shell_command :10
Grep grep_search :11
Glob glob :12
TodoWrite write_todos :13
Skill activate_skill :14
WebSearch google_web_search :15
WebFetch web_fetch :16
Task @agent-name :17

Subagent table :25-31 maps implementer/spec-reviewer/code-quality-reviewer to @generalist or @code-reviewer.

Appendix C — anthropic-best-practices.md MCP section (full)

162_superpowers/skills/writing-skills/anthropic-best-practices.md:1053-1071:

### MCP tool references
Format: ServerName:tool_name
Use the BigQuery:bigquery_schema tool...
Use the GitHub:create_issue tool...

Without server prefix, Claude may fail with multiple MCP servers — authoring guidance, not SP runtime.

File Role

Script Verifies

Appendix F — dispatching-parallel-agents vs executing-plans

Skill Session Parallelism Citation
dispatching-parallel-agents Same session Multiple host subagents in parallel skill body
executing-plans Parallel session (worktree) Separate Claude session skill body
subagent-driven-development Same session Sequential tasks + review SKILL.md:36-40

Choice is orchestration config expressed in skill graphs, not MCP topic routing.

Appendix — Host orchestration decision tree

Item Detail
Same session + independent tasks subagent-driven-development — SKILL.md:36-40
Parallel session + worktree executing-plans — separate Claude session
Parallel same-session tasks dispatching-parallel-agents — multiple host subagents
No plan yet brainstorming → writing-plans — mandatory chain
Bug fix systematic-debugging before implementation skills
Claim complete verification-before-completion before any success language
Branch finish finishing-a-development-branch after tests green
Codex multi_agent off Subagent skills cannot run — codex-tools.md:18-24
Copilot Task task agent_type general-purpose — copilot-tools.md:15
Gemini parallel Multiple @ in one prompt — gemini-tools.md:37-39
OpenCode subagent @mention per superpowers.js:79
MCP tool in skill text Use ServerName:tool — anthropic-best-practices.md:1057
GitHub API search Copilot github-mcp-server-* — copilot-tools.md:42
No SP MCP server All manifests lack mcpServers
OpenCode MCP User opencode config — not SP plugin
Transcript proof docs/testing.md:57-63 JSONL parse
Skill triggering tests tests/skill-triggering/run-all.sh
Explicit skill UX tests/explicit-skill-requests/
Claude worktree test test-worktree-native-preference.sh
Plan reviewer prompt writing-plans/plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 009_mcp-orchestration/09_sp_mcp.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers MCP Orchestration — Source Analysis

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers는 MCP-native가 아니다 — MCP server/client manager, mcpServers manifest, transport, OAuth, tool listing runtime을 구현하지 않는다. MCP는 skill 작성 가이드와 호스트 MCP 도구 참조로만 등장한다.

graph LR
    SP["Superpowers skills/"] --> HOST["Host harness"]
    HOST --> MCP["Host MCP servers<br/>(Codex config, Claude MCP, Copilot github-mcp)"]
    SP -.->|"anthropic-best-practices.md:1053"| GUIDE["MCP authoring: ServerName:tool_name"]
    SP --> NATIVE["Host-native tools<br/>spawn_agent, Task, @generalist"]

1. Executive Summary

Area Finding Evidence
MCP server in SP repo None no mcpServers in any manifest
MCP client/transport None no rust/js MCP implementation
Codex integration skills manifest only .codex-plugin/plugin.json:23
Claude integration SessionStart hook hooks/hooks.json:3-14
OpenCode skills path + bootstrap .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:103-133
Orchestration model prompt/skill → host tools subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md
MCP in skill text authoring guidance only writing-skills/anthropic-best-practices.md:1053-1071

2. Manifest Negative Evidence

2.1 Codex (162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json)

Keys present: name, version, skills, interface (:1-47). Keys absent: mcpServers, connectors, tools, permissions.

2.2 Claude (162_superpowers/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:1-19)

Metadata only — no MCP declaration.

2.3 Cursor (162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json)

Declares skills, agents, commands, hooks — not MCP.

2.4 Gemini (162_superpowers/gemini-extension.json:1-6)

contextFileName only.

2.5 OpenCode (162_superpowers/.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js)

Mutates config.skills.paths (:103-108) and chat messages (:120-133) — no MCP registration.

3. MCP References Inside Skills (not implementation)

3.1 Skill authoring (writing-skills/anthropic-best-practices.md:1053-1071)

### MCP tool references
Format: ServerName:tool_name
Example: BigQuery:bigquery_schema, GitHub:create_issue

Purpose: avoid "tool not found" when host exposes multiple MCP servers. SP does not implement BigQuery or GitHub servers — documents naming for skill authors.

3.2 Copilot GitHub MCP (copilot-tools.md:42)

Lists github-mcp-server-* as Copilot CLI native tools — issues, PRs, code search. SP maps skill prose to host tools; does not bundle MCP server.

3.3 brainstorming terminal state (brainstorming/SKILL.md:66)

Explicit: after brainstorming invoke writing-plans only — NOT mcp-builder or implementation skills.

3.4 TDD anti-pattern mock (test-driven-development/testing-anti-patterns.md:144)

vi.mock('MCPServerManager') — example in testing doc, not SP runtime.

4. Host-Native Orchestration (MCP-adjacent)

Superpowers orchestrates via host subagent APIs, not MCP:

4.1 Codex (references/codex-tools.md:1-60)

Skill reference Codex equivalent
Task subagent spawn_agent (:7)
Parallel Task multiple spawn_agent (:8)
Task result wait_agent (:9)
Release slot close_agent (:10)
TodoWrite update_plan (:11)
Skill native skill follow (:12)

Requires [features] multi_agent = true (:18-24).

Environment detection for worktrees (:34-47): GIT_DIR, GIT_COMMON, BRANCH bash probes.

Codex App finishing when sandbox blocks git (:49-59): commit + user uses App UI.

4.2 Gemini (references/gemini-tools.md:1-50)

Skill reference Gemini equivalent
Task @generalist or named agents (:17, :25-31)
Skill activate_skill (:14)
WebSearch google_web_search (:15) — host tool
WebFetch web_fetch (:16)
Parallel dispatch multiple @ in same prompt (:37-39)

Additional Gemini-only: save_memory, tracker_create_task, plan mode (:41-50).

4.3 Copilot (references/copilot-tools.md:1-43)

Skill reference Copilot equivalent
Task task with agent_type (:15-17)
WebFetch web_fetch (:14)
WebSearch No equivalent — web_fetch + search URL (:19)
TodoWrite sql todos table (:18)
Async shell bash async + read_bash etc. (:22-32)
Memory store_memory (:38)

4.4 OpenCode (.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:76-83)

TodoWrite → todowrite
Task → @mention subagents
Skill → native skill tool

5. Subagent-Driven Development as Orchestration Layer

162_superpowers/skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:1-80:

  • Fresh subagent per task (:10-12) — isolated context, no session pollution
  • Two-stage review: spec then quality (:8, :54-59)
  • Continuous execution — no human check-in between tasks (:14)
  • Process graph (:18-33) vs executing-plans for parallel session

Prompt templates (separate files):

  • implementer-prompt.md
  • spec-reviewer-prompt.md
  • code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md

Integration test (docs/testing.md:40-64) verifies Task tool dispatch via transcript JSONL.

5.1 dispatching-parallel-agents

Skill for independent parallel subagent tasks — maps to multiple spawn_agent / parallel @generalist per host mapping files.

5.2 executing-plans

Alternative when tasks need parallel session (worktree) vs same-session subagents.

6. OpenCode Plugin vs MCP

OpenCode hosts may register MCP separately in user config. SP plugin only:

  1. Appends skills directory
  2. Injects bootstrap on first user message

No tool schema registration, no MCP list_tools bridge in SP code.

7. Classification

Layer SP role Host role
MCP transport none stdio/http/sse
MCP tool execution none host runtime
Skill text referencing MCP yes (authoring) executes
Subagent orchestration skill protocol native APIs
Web tools via MCP documented in copilot-tools github-mcp-server-*

Superpowers = MCP-adjacent skill/plugin layer. Compare 009_mcp-orchestration/09_co_mcp.md for first-party Rust MCP stack.

8. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
MCP client codex-rs MCP stack none
Tool orchestration MCP + builtins host tools + skill mapping docs
Subagents spawn_agent etc. documents host equivalents
Plugin hook N/A OpenCode JS only

Axis 10: Memory pipeline

AXIS

010_memory-pipeline/10_sp_memory.md

Overview

Axis 10: Memory pipeline — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers는 learned-memory 엔진이 아니다. 기억은 procedural — reusable SKILL.md, session bootstrap, planning artifacts, subagent context discipline, optional brainstorm session files.

  • graph TD
  • SK["skills/*/SKILL.mdprocedural memory"] --> AGENT["Agent behavior"]
  • BOOT["SessionStart / OpenCode transformsession bootstrap"] --> AGENT
  • SUB["subagent-driven-developmentfresh context per task"] --> AGENT
  • BRAIN[".superpowers/brainstorm/visual companion artifacts"] --> SESSION["Project-local session files"]
  • HOST["Host memory APIssave_memory, store_memory"] --> LONG["Host long-term only"]
  • | Surface | Type | Persistence | Citation |
  • | skills/*/SKILL.md | Procedural | versioned in git | README.md workflow list |
  • | using-superpowers | Bootstrap policy | injected each session | SKILL.md:1-117 |
  • | Subagent prompts | Task-scoped context | ephemeral per dispatch | subagent-driven-development/*.md |
  • | Design docs | Spec artifacts | docs/superpowers/specs/ | brainstorming/SKILL.md:29 |

Topics covered

    1. Memory Taxonomy
    1. Procedural Memory — Skill Corpus
    1. Session Bootstrap Pipeline

  • 3.2 OpenCode
  • 3.3 Gemini
    1. Subagent Context Discipline (anti-memory-leak)
    1. Brainstorm Visual Companion Session Files

    1. Test Transcripts as External Memory
    1. Absences
    1. Parity vs co
  • Source citations (162_superpowers)

Source code snippets

README / docs

NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE

docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md

README / docs

Main analysis

Superpowers Memory / Session Pipeline — Source Analysis

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers is not a learned-memory engine. Memory is procedural — reusable SKILL.md, session bootstrap, planning artifacts, subagent context discipline, optional brainstorm session files.

graph TD
    SK["skills/*/SKILL.md<br/>procedural memory"] --> AGENT["Agent behavior"]
    BOOT["SessionStart / OpenCode transform<br/>session bootstrap"] --> AGENT
    SUB["subagent-driven-development<br/>fresh context per task"] --> AGENT
    BRAIN[".superpowers/brainstorm/<br/>visual companion artifacts"] --> SESSION["Project-local session files"]
    HOST["Host memory APIs<br/>save_memory, store_memory"] --> LONG["Host long-term only"]

1. Memory Taxonomy

Surface Type Persistence Citation
skills/*/SKILL.md Procedural versioned in git README.md workflow list
using-superpowers Bootstrap policy injected each session SKILL.md:1-117
Subagent prompts Task-scoped context ephemeral per dispatch subagent-driven-development/*.md
Design docs Spec artifacts docs/superpowers/specs/ brainstorming/SKILL.md:29
Brainstorm companion HTML + events .superpowers/brainstorm/ visual-companion.md:29-48
Test transcripts Verification host JSONL docs/testing.md:57-63
Host save_memory Learned facts GEMINI.md host gemini-tools.md:48
Host store_memory Codebase facts Copilot host copilot-tools.md:38

2. Procedural Memory — Skill Corpus

14 first-party skills in 162_superpowers/skills/:

brainstorming, dispatching-parallel-agents, executing-plans, finishing-a-development-branch, receiving-code-review, requesting-code-review, subagent-driven-development, systematic-debugging, test-driven-development, using-git-worktrees, using-superpowers, verification-before-completion, writing-plans, writing-skills.

Each SKILL.md = durable how-to memory. No SP vector index — discovery via host skill loader + bootstrap text.

using-superpowers/SKILL.md:91-94 — "I remember this skill" red flag: skills evolve; must re-invoke.

3. Session Bootstrap Pipeline

3.1 Claude/Cursor/Copilot SessionStart

hooks/session-start:17-35:

  • Embeds full using-superpowers/SKILL.md in session_context
  • Legacy path warning if ~/.config/superpowers/skills exists (:11-14)

hooks/hooks.json:5 — fires on startup|clear|compact — re-bootstrap after compact.

3.2 OpenCode

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:120-133 — bootstrap on first user message, not system. Cache _bootstrapCache (:53) — survives per-step hook re-fire.

3.3 Gemini

GEMINI.md:1-2 — static context file imports bootstrap + tool mapping at session start.

4. Subagent Context Discipline (anti-memory-leak)

subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:10-12:

They should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need.

Implications:

  • No automatic transcript forwarding
  • Plan task text copied in full to subagent prompt (:63 process — "extract all tasks with full text")
  • Preserves coordinator context for orchestration

implementer-prompt.md, spec-reviewer-prompt.md, code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md — curated review criteria as externalized memory.

Spec reviewer must read code independently (docs/testing.md:51).

5. Brainstorm Visual Companion Session Files

skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md:29-48:

  • screen_dir — HTML content fragments
  • state_dir — user selection events
  • Default persistence: <project>/.superpowers/brainstorm/<session>/
  • Without --project-dir, /tmp cleanup (:48)

Not long-term memory — design-session artifacts. User should .gitignore .superpowers/ (:48).

server.cjs:76-79 — BRAINSTORM_DIR, CONTENT_DIR env configuration.

Server auto-exits after 30 min inactivity (visual-companion.md:97).

6. Verification Memory (completion gates)

verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:18-38:

NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE

Gate function: IDENTIFY command → RUN → READ output → VERIFY → claim (:26-37).

Does not persist results — requires fresh evidence each claim.

Common failures table (:42-50) — tests/linter/build/regression.

7. Host-Mediated Long-Term Memory

SP does not implement these — documents host equivalents:

Host Tool File Purpose
Gemini save_memory gemini-tools.md:48 Persist to GEMINI.md
Copilot store_memory copilot-tools.md:38 Codebase facts
Claude host memory features not in SP repo CLAUDE.md / host settings

SP GEMINI.md is bootstrap import, not a growing memory file managed by SP.

8. Test Transcripts as External Memory

docs/testing.md:1-64:

  • Integration tests run real Claude Code headless sessions
  • Verification parses .jsonl transcripts (:57-63)
  • Checks: Skill invocation, Task dispatch, TodoWrite, file creation, tests, git commits
  • Token analysis via analyze-token-usage.py (:16)

Transcripts are host artifacts — SP reads for CI, does not own storage.

Requirements (:36-38): run from plugin directory, claude CLI, dev marketplace enabled.

9. Absences

Feature Verdict
SP session database absent
Vector store / embeddings absent
Memory search index absent
Consolidation pipeline absent
MEMORY.md pipeline absent (unlike some agent frameworks)
Cross-session SP fact extractor absent

10. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
Persistent memory codex-rs memory (if enabled) none
Session state host session store stateless skills
Procedural memory bundled skills 14 SKILL.md files
Verification discipline host + tools verification-before-completion

Source citations (162_superpowers)

Topic Citation
Bootstrap policy skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:1-117
SessionStart injection hooks/session-start:17-35
Re-bootstrap on compact hooks/hooks.json:5
OpenCode bootstrap cache .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:49-96
Subagent fresh context skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:10-12
Brainstorm artifacts skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md:29-48
Verification gate skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:18-50
Transcript tests docs/testing.md:55-63
Gemini save_memory skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md:48
Legacy skills path hooks/session-start:11-14

  • Prompt/bootstrap: 002_prompt-context/02_sp_prompt.md
  • Guardian gates: 012_guardian-approval/12_sp_guardian.md
  • Codex memory: 010_memory-pipeline/10_co_memory.md

Change history

date change
2026-06-27 WP54 deepen to ≥12 KB with path:line citations from 162_superpowers
2026-06-27 WP38 source citations + corpus links

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot (162_superpowers v5.1.0) Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs
Plugin version 5.1.0 in manifests Check .codex-plugin/plugin.json on upgrade

Appendix A — Bootstrap re-injection triggers

Event Mechanism Citation
Session startup SessionStart hook hooks/hooks.json:5 matcher startup
Context clear SessionStart hook hooks/hooks.json:5 matcher clear
Compact handoff SessionStart hook hooks/hooks.json:5 matcher compact
OpenCode first message message transform .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:120-133
Gemini session GEMINI.md import GEMINI.md:1-2

After compact, SP re-injects full using-superpowers — session memory reset compensated by bootstrap, not by SP DB.

Appendix B — verification-before-completion extended patterns

162_superpowers/skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:76-140 region:

Test verification pattern (:78-80 style): run command → cite output → claim.

Build verification: must see exit 0, not linter alone.

Agent completion: VCS diff required, not agent report.

Regression: red-green cycle required for new tests.

Appendix C — Design doc artifact path

brainstorming/SKILL.md:29:

docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md

Committed to git — durable procedural artifact, not SP index.

Plans from writing-plans similarly produce plan documents reviewed before execution.

Appendix D — using-git-worktrees memory of isolation

Worktree skill Step 0 (using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md) — environment probes from codex-tools.md:37-47:

GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)

Agent must re-read git state — no SP cache of worktree layout.

Appendix E — Token analysis as session forensics

docs/testing.md:64 — analyze-token-usage.py breaks down tokens by subagent from transcript.

Used for eval, not persistent memory — one-off analysis per test run.

Appendix F — Copilot store_memory vs SP skills

copilot-tools.md:38 — store_memory persists codebase facts for future Copilot sessions.

SP does not call or implement store_memory — skill text may mention memory concept; Copilot host executes.

Contrast: SP skills are git-versioned procedural memory always available when plugin installed.

Appendix G — Brainstorm event file loop

visual-companion.md:94-110 (loop section):

  1. Check $STATE_DIR/server-info alive
  2. Write HTML to screen_dir with semantic filename
  3. Read state_dir/events on next turn for user clicks
  4. Never reuse filenames — each screen fresh file

Event files = short-lived interaction memory between agent turns.

Appendix — Session state vs SP ownership

Item Detail
Skill file content Git-versioned — survives all sessions
SessionStart bootstrap Re-injected on startup/clear/compact
OpenCode bootstrap cache Per-process module cache — not cross-session DB
Brainstorm HTML files Project .superpowers/brainstorm/ until deleted
Brainstorm /tmp mode Ephemeral if no --project-dir
Design docs docs/superpowers/specs/*.md in git
Claude JSONL transcript Host stores — SP tests read only
Gemini save_memory Host writes GEMINI.md — not SP pipeline
Copilot store_memory Host session DB — not SP
TodoWrite / update_plan Per-session task list — host tool
Subagent context Constructed per dispatch — not inherited
verification skill No persistence — fresh run each claim
Legacy skills dir Deprecated ~/.config/superpowers/skills
SP vector index Does not exist
SP memory search Does not exist
SP consolidation Does not exist
README workflow memory Procedural — skills auto-trigger
writing-skills meta How to author durable skill text
persuasion-principles.md Skill authoring psychology
Token analyze script Post-hoc transcript analysis only

Appendix — executing-plans vs session memory

162_superpowers/skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — parallel session execution in worktree. Memory model:

  • Parent session retains plan context
  • Child session gets worktree-isolated copy — no automatic SP memory bridge
  • Plan text must be re-readable from committed plan file in worktree

162_superpowers/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md — same-session parallel subagents share parent transcript but each subagent gets constructed prompt only.

Appendix — writing-plans artifact discipline

Plans must cite exact file paths and test commands — agents re-read plan file rather than SP memory index. Plan reviewer prompt (writing-plans/plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md) gates plan quality before execution begins.

Appendix — OpenCode double-injection guard

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:129 — if first user message already contains EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT, skip re-injection. Prevents bootstrap memory bloat when in-memory message arrays replay through hook on each agent step (:116-118 comment).

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 010_memory-pipeline/10_sp_memory.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers Memory / Session Pipeline — Source Analysis

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers는 learned-memory 엔진이 아니다. 기억은 procedural — reusable SKILL.md, session bootstrap, planning artifacts, subagent context discipline, optional brainstorm session files.

graph TD
    SK["skills/*/SKILL.md<br/>procedural memory"] --> AGENT["Agent behavior"]
    BOOT["SessionStart / OpenCode transform<br/>session bootstrap"] --> AGENT
    SUB["subagent-driven-development<br/>fresh context per task"] --> AGENT
    BRAIN[".superpowers/brainstorm/<br/>visual companion artifacts"] --> SESSION["Project-local session files"]
    HOST["Host memory APIs<br/>save_memory, store_memory"] --> LONG["Host long-term only"]

1. Memory Taxonomy

Surface Type Persistence Citation
skills/*/SKILL.md Procedural versioned in git README.md workflow list
using-superpowers Bootstrap policy injected each session SKILL.md:1-117
Subagent prompts Task-scoped context ephemeral per dispatch subagent-driven-development/*.md
Design docs Spec artifacts docs/superpowers/specs/ brainstorming/SKILL.md:29
Brainstorm companion HTML + events .superpowers/brainstorm/ visual-companion.md:29-48
Test transcripts Verification host JSONL docs/testing.md:57-63
Host save_memory Learned facts GEMINI.md host gemini-tools.md:48
Host store_memory Codebase facts Copilot host copilot-tools.md:38

2. Procedural Memory — Skill Corpus

14 first-party skills in 162_superpowers/skills/:

brainstorming, dispatching-parallel-agents, executing-plans, finishing-a-development-branch, receiving-code-review, requesting-code-review, subagent-driven-development, systematic-debugging, test-driven-development, using-git-worktrees, using-superpowers, verification-before-completion, writing-plans, writing-skills.

Each SKILL.md = durable how-to memory. No SP vector index — discovery via host skill loader + bootstrap text.

using-superpowers/SKILL.md:91-94 — "I remember this skill" red flag: skills evolve; must re-invoke.

3. Session Bootstrap Pipeline

3.1 Claude/Cursor/Copilot SessionStart

hooks/session-start:17-35:

  • Embeds full using-superpowers/SKILL.md in session_context
  • Legacy path warning if ~/.config/superpowers/skills exists (:11-14)

hooks/hooks.json:5 — fires on startup|clear|compact — re-bootstrap after compact.

3.2 OpenCode

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:120-133 — bootstrap on first user message, not system. Cache _bootstrapCache (:53) — survives per-step hook re-fire.

3.3 Gemini

GEMINI.md:1-2 — static context file imports bootstrap + tool mapping at session start.

4. Subagent Context Discipline (anti-memory-leak)

subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:10-12:

They should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need.

Implications:

  • No automatic transcript forwarding
  • Plan task text copied in full to subagent prompt (:63 process — "extract all tasks with full text")
  • Preserves coordinator context for orchestration

implementer-prompt.md, spec-reviewer-prompt.md, code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md — curated review criteria as externalized memory.

Spec reviewer must read code independently (docs/testing.md:51).

5. Brainstorm Visual Companion Session Files

skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md:29-48:

  • screen_dir — HTML content fragments
  • state_dir — user selection events
  • Default persistence: <project>/.superpowers/brainstorm/<session>/
  • Without --project-dir, /tmp cleanup (:48)

Not long-term memory — design-session artifacts. User should .gitignore .superpowers/ (:48).

server.cjs:76-79 — BRAINSTORM_DIR, CONTENT_DIR env configuration.

Server auto-exits after 30 min inactivity (visual-companion.md:97).

6. Verification Memory (completion gates)

verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:18-38:

NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE

Gate function: IDENTIFY command → RUN → READ output → VERIFY → claim (:26-37).

Does not persist results — requires fresh evidence each claim.

Common failures table (:42-50) — tests/linter/build/regression.

7. Host-Mediated Long-Term Memory

SP does not implement these — documents host equivalents:

Host Tool File Purpose
Gemini save_memory gemini-tools.md:48 Persist to GEMINI.md
Copilot store_memory copilot-tools.md:38 Codebase facts
Claude host memory features not in SP repo CLAUDE.md / host settings

SP GEMINI.md is bootstrap import, not a growing memory file managed by SP.

8. Test Transcripts as External Memory

docs/testing.md:1-64:

  • Integration tests run real Claude Code headless sessions
  • Verification parses .jsonl transcripts (:57-63)
  • Checks: Skill invocation, Task dispatch, TodoWrite, file creation, tests, git commits
  • Token analysis via analyze-token-usage.py (:16)

Transcripts are host artifacts — SP reads for CI, does not own storage.

Requirements (:36-38): run from plugin directory, claude CLI, dev marketplace enabled.

9. Absences

Feature Verdict
SP session database absent
Vector store / embeddings absent
Memory search index absent
Consolidation pipeline absent
MEMORY.md pipeline absent (unlike some agent frameworks)
Cross-session SP fact extractor absent

10. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
Persistent memory codex-rs memory (if enabled) none
Session state host session store stateless skills
Procedural memory bundled skills 14 SKILL.md files
Verification discipline host + tools verification-before-completion

Topic Citation

SessionStart injection hooks/session-start:17-35
Re-bootstrap on compact hooks/hooks.json:5
OpenCode bootstrap cache .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:49-96
Subagent fresh context skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:10-12
Brainstorm artifacts skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md:29-48
Verification gate skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:18-50
Transcript tests docs/testing.md:55-63
Gemini save_memory skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md:48
Legacy skills path hooks/session-start:11-14

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-06-27 WP54 deepen to ≥12 KB with path:line citations from 162_superpowers
2026-06-27 WP38 source citations + corpus links

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot (162_superpowers v5.1.0) Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs
Plugin version 5.1.0 in manifests Check .codex-plugin/plugin.json on upgrade

Appendix A — Bootstrap re-injection triggers

Event Mechanism Citation
Session startup SessionStart hook hooks/hooks.json:5 matcher startup
Context clear SessionStart hook hooks/hooks.json:5 matcher clear
Compact handoff SessionStart hook hooks/hooks.json:5 matcher compact
OpenCode first message message transform .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:120-133
Gemini session GEMINI.md import GEMINI.md:1-2

After compact, SP re-injects full using-superpowers — session memory reset compensated by bootstrap, not by SP DB.

Appendix B — verification-before-completion extended patterns

162_superpowers/skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:76-140 region:

Test verification pattern (:78-80 style): run command → cite output → claim.

Build verification: must see exit 0, not linter alone.

Agent completion: VCS diff required, not agent report.

Regression: red-green cycle required for new tests.

Appendix C — Design doc artifact path

brainstorming/SKILL.md:29:

docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md

Committed to git — durable procedural artifact, not SP index.

Plans from writing-plans similarly produce plan documents reviewed before execution.

Appendix D — using-git-worktrees memory of isolation

Worktree skill Step 0 (using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md) — environment probes from codex-tools.md:37-47:

GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)

Agent must re-read git state — no SP cache of worktree layout.

Appendix E — Token analysis as session forensics

docs/testing.md:64 — analyze-token-usage.py breaks down tokens by subagent from transcript.

Used for eval, not persistent memory — one-off analysis per test run.

Appendix F — Copilot store_memory vs SP skills

copilot-tools.md:38 — store_memory persists codebase facts for future Copilot sessions.

SP does not call or implement store_memory — skill text may mention memory concept; Copilot host executes.

Contrast: SP skills are git-versioned procedural memory always available when plugin installed.

Appendix G — Brainstorm event file loop

visual-companion.md:94-110 (loop section):

  1. Check $STATE_DIR/server-info alive
  2. Write HTML to screen_dir with semantic filename
  3. Read state_dir/events on next turn for user clicks
  4. Never reuse filenames — each screen fresh file

Event files = short-lived interaction memory between agent turns.

Appendix — Session state vs SP ownership

Item Detail
Skill file content Git-versioned — survives all sessions
SessionStart bootstrap Re-injected on startup/clear/compact
OpenCode bootstrap cache Per-process module cache — not cross-session DB
Brainstorm HTML files Project .superpowers/brainstorm/ until deleted
Brainstorm /tmp mode Ephemeral if no --project-dir
Design docs docs/superpowers/specs/*.md in git
Claude JSONL transcript Host stores — SP tests read only
Gemini save_memory Host writes GEMINI.md — not SP pipeline
Copilot store_memory Host session DB — not SP
TodoWrite / update_plan Per-session task list — host tool
Subagent context Constructed per dispatch — not inherited
verification skill No persistence — fresh run each claim
Legacy skills dir Deprecated ~/.config/superpowers/skills
SP vector index Does not exist
SP memory search Does not exist
SP consolidation Does not exist
README workflow memory Procedural — skills auto-trigger
writing-skills meta How to author durable skill text
persuasion-principles.md Skill authoring psychology
Token analyze script Post-hoc transcript analysis only

Appendix — executing-plans vs session memory

162_superpowers/skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md — parallel session execution in worktree. Memory model:

  • Parent session retains plan context
  • Child session gets worktree-isolated copy — no automatic SP memory bridge
  • Plan text must be re-readable from committed plan file in worktree

162_superpowers/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md — same-session parallel subagents share parent transcript but each subagent gets constructed prompt only.

Appendix — writing-plans artifact discipline

Plans must cite exact file paths and test commands — agents re-read plan file rather than SP memory index. Plan reviewer prompt (writing-plans/plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md) gates plan quality before execution begins.

Appendix — OpenCode double-injection guard

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:129 — if first user message already contains EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT, skip re-injection. Prevents bootstrap memory bloat when in-memory message arrays replay through hook on each agent step (:116-118 comment).

27

Axis 11: Plugin system

AXIS

011_plugin-system/11_sp_plugin.md

Overview

Axis 11: Plugin system — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ v5.1.0. Superpowers = portable skills/ corpus + per-host adapters (manifests, hooks, JS plugin, sync scripts). Universal plugin manager 없음.

  • graph TB
  • SKILLS["skills/ (14 SKILL.md)"] --> CODEX[".codex-plugin/plugin.json"]
  • SKILLS --> CLAUDE[".claude-plugin + hooks/"]
  • SKILLS --> CURSOR[".cursor-plugin + hooks-cursor.json"]
  • SKILLS --> GEMINI["gemini-extension.json + GEMINI.md"]
  • SKILLS --> OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"]
  • SKILLS --> SYNC["scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh"]
  • SYNC --> MARKET["openai-codex-plugins fork PR"]
  • | Harness | Manifest | Hooks / runtime | Skills exposure |
  • | Codex | .codex-plugin/plugin.json | marketplace install | "skills": "./skills/" :23 |
  • | Claude | .claude-plugin/plugin.json | hooks/hooks.json SessionStart | plugin skills/ convention |

Main analysis

Superpowers Plugin System — Source Analysis

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/ v5.1.0. Superpowers = portable skills/ corpus + per-host adapters (manifests, hooks, JS plugin, sync scripts). No universal plugin manager.

graph TB
    SKILLS["skills/ (14 SKILL.md)"] --> CODEX[".codex-plugin/plugin.json"]
    SKILLS --> CLAUDE[".claude-plugin + hooks/"]
    SKILLS --> CURSOR[".cursor-plugin + hooks-cursor.json"]
    SKILLS --> GEMINI["gemini-extension.json + GEMINI.md"]
    SKILLS --> OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"]
    SKILLS --> SYNC["scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh"]
    SYNC --> MARKET["openai-codex-plugins fork PR"]

1. Host Adapter Matrix

Harness Manifest Hooks / runtime Skills exposure
Codex .codex-plugin/plugin.json marketplace install "skills": "./skills/" :23
Claude .claude-plugin/plugin.json hooks/hooks.json SessionStart plugin skills/ convention
Cursor .cursor-plugin/plugin.json hooks/hooks-cursor.json explicit paths :21-24
Gemini gemini-extension.json GEMINI.md context @ file imports
OpenCode package.json main → JS SuperpowersPlugin export config.skills.paths
Copilot (uses shared session-start) SDK additionalContext plugin install

Version locked 5.1.0 across manifests (plugin.json, gemini-extension.json, package.json).

2. Codex Plugin Detail

162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:1-47:

  • interface.displayName: "Superpowers" (:25)
  • capabilities: Interactive, Read, Write (:30-33)
  • defaultPrompt seeds (:35-37)
  • brandColor, logos (:42-44)
  • No MCP, connectors, or tool declarations

UI category: Coding (:29). License MIT (:12).

3. Claude Plugin + Hooks

.claude-plugin/plugin.json:1-19 — keywords skills, tdd, debugging, collaboration.

hooks/hooks.json:1-15:

"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "... session-start", "async": false }]
}]

hooks/session-start — cross-platform bootstrap (see config axis doc).

hooks/run-hook.cmd — Windows wrapper referenced in hooks.json :9.

4. Cursor Plugin

.cursor-plugin/plugin.json:21-24 — most complete path declarations:

"skills": "./skills/",
"agents": "./agents/",
"commands": "./commands/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks-cursor.json"

displayName field (:3) — Cursor-specific naming.

5. Gemini Extension

gemini-extension.json:1-6:

{ "name": "superpowers", "version": "5.1.0", "contextFileName": "GEMINI.md" }

GEMINI.md:1-2 imports bootstrap + gemini-tools.md.

6. OpenCode Plugin

package.json:1-7 — ESM module entry .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js.

SuperpowersPlugin async factory (superpowers.js:55):

  • Returns { config, 'experimental.chat.messages.transform' }
  • No other hook types registered in SP

.opencode/INSTALL.md — install documentation (read for host setup).

7. Marketplace Sync Pipeline

scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh (463 lines):

7.1 Target (:35-37)

  • Fork: prime-radiant-inc/openai-codex-plugins
  • Dest: plugins/superpowers
  • Opens PR via gh

7.2 Excludes (:45-77)

Host-specific and dev-only paths stripped from Codex payload:

  • .claude-plugin/, .cursor-plugin/, .opencode/, hooks/, tests/, docs/, scripts/
  • Root ceremony: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, RELEASE-NOTES.md, GEMINI.md

Anchored /scripts/ pattern (:41-43) — avoids excluding skills/brainstorming/scripts/.

7.3 Determinism (:12-13)

Same upstream SHA → identical PR diffs for verification.

7.4 Test

tests/codex-plugin-sync/test-sync-to-codex-plugin.sh — sync script regression.

8. Version + Release Hygiene

.version-bump.json — version bump automation config.

scripts/bump-version.sh — release versioning (referenced in etc axis).

RELEASE-NOTES.md (66731 bytes) — changelog corpus.

.claude-plugin/marketplace.json — marketplace metadata for Claude distribution.

9. Contributor Harness Rules

AGENTS.md:1-60 — 94% PR rejection rate warning for agents.

New harness support requires (AGENTS.md harness section):

  • Real transcript proof
  • PR template completion
  • Target dev branch not main (:32)

Zero-dependency design (:36-38) — third-party deps only for new harness support.

10. Boundary

SP provides SP does not provide
skills corpus universal plugin registry
per-host manifests plugin dependency resolver
SessionStart bootstrap tool permission runtime
Codex sync script marketplace ownership (OpenAI fork)

Compare 011_plugin-system/11_co_plugin.md for Codex plugin runtime in codex-rs.

11. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
Plugin format .codex-plugin consumer .codex-plugin producer
Skills path config + manifest manifest :23
Multi-host Codex primary 5+ adapters
Sync receives rsync upstream source

Appendix A — Repository top-level inventory (plugin-relevant)

Path Plugin role
.codex-plugin/plugin.json Codex marketplace entry
.claude-plugin/plugin.json Claude metadata
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json Claude marketplace bundle
.cursor-plugin/plugin.json Cursor paths
.cursor-plugin/ Cursor plugin root
gemini-extension.json Gemini extension manifest
GEMINI.md Gemini context bootstrap
hooks/hooks.json Claude SessionStart
hooks/hooks-cursor.json Cursor hooks

hooks/run-hook.cmd Windows hook wrapper
.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js OpenCode ESM plugin
