scripts/bump-version.sh Release versioning
assets/ Plugin UI images

Appendix B — Sync script CLI flags

scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:15-21:

Flag Purpose
-n Dry run
-y Skip confirm
--local PATH Use existing checkout
--base BRANCH Default main
--bootstrap Create plugin dir if missing

Requires: bash, rsync, git, gh, python3 (:27).

Appendix C — RELEASE-NOTES.md scale

162_superpowers/RELEASE-NOTES.md — 66731 bytes in checkout — documents version history for marketplace releases. Plugin version 5.1.0 synchronized across manifests.

Appendix D — Cursor hooks-cursor.json

Cursor-specific hook registration separate from Claude hooks.json — allows Cursor hook runner format without breaking Claude Code nested hookSpecificOutput.

Appendix E — .cursor-plugin agents/commands paths

162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json:22-23:

"agents": "./agents/",
"commands": "./commands/"

Codex sync excludes /commands/ (sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:70) — Cursor-only surface.

Appendix F — Plugin test matrix

Test directory Host
tests/codex-plugin-sync/ Codex marketplace sync
tests/opencode/ OpenCode plugin
tests/claude-code/ Claude Code integration
tests/skill-triggering/ Cross-host skill load
tests/explicit-skill-requests/ Explicit skill UX

Appendix G — Zero-dependency principle

AGENTS.md:36-38 — PRs adding third-party dependencies rejected unless new harness support.

Plugin payload = skills + thin adapters — no npm dependencies in package.json beyond module type declaration.

Appendix — Marketplace and distribution detail

Item Detail
Codex fork prime-radiant-inc/openai-codex-plugins
Sync dest path plugins/superpowers
Sync dry run -n flag
Sync bootstrap --bootstrap creates dir
Excluded hooks Not shipped to Codex plugin
Excluded GEMINI.md Gemini-only bootstrap
Excluded tests Dev-only — not in marketplace payload
Included assets superpowers-small.svg, app-icon.png
Claude marketplace.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
Cursor agents path ./agents/ — Cursor only
Cursor commands ./commands/ — excluded from Codex sync
package.json type module — ESM OpenCode
version-bump.json Automated version sync
bump-version.sh Release script
RELEASE-NOTES 66KB changelog file
MIT license LICENSE file
Official Claude install superpowers@claude-plugins-official
README multi-harness Separate install per harness
run-hook.cmd Windows SessionStart wrapper
polyglot-hooks.md Windows hook compatibility doc

Appendix — Full EXCLUDES list (sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:45-77)

Exclude pattern Rationale
/.claude/ Claude-only dot dir
/.claude-plugin/ Claude manifest — not Codex
/.codex/ Codex dev dot dir
/.cursor-plugin/ Cursor adapter
/.git/ Never ship VCS
/.github/ CI templates — not plugin payload
/.gitignore Repo hygiene
/.opencode/ OpenCode adapter
/.version-bump.json Release automation
/.worktrees/ Local worktrees
.DS_Store Finder artifacts (unanchored)
/AGENTS.md Contributor doc
/CHANGELOG.md Separate changelog
/CLAUDE.md Claude contributor
/GEMINI.md Gemini bootstrap
/RELEASE-NOTES.md Large release log
/gemini-extension.json Gemini manifest
/package.json OpenCode entry
/commands/ Cursor commands
/docs/ Dev documentation
/hooks/ SessionStart scripts
/lib/ If present — dev lib
/scripts/ sync, bump scripts
/tests/ Test harness
/tmp/ Temp files

Anchored /scripts/ (:41-43) — does not exclude skills/brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs.

Appendix — Codex plugin shipped payload (positive list)

What does sync to plugins/superpowers/:

  • Entire skills/ tree (14 skills + support files)
  • .codex-plugin/plugin.json + assets referenced therein
  • assets/ logos and icons
  • Any other tracked files not matching EXCLUDES

Result: Codex marketplace plugin is skills + presentation — single-host minimal adapter.

Appendix — Version alignment across manifests

All host manifests in checkout declare 5.1.0: .codex-plugin/plugin.json:3, .claude-plugin/plugin.json:4, .cursor-plugin/plugin.json:5, gemini-extension.json:4, package.json:3. .version-bump.json automates bump so marketplace releases stay synchronized across harness adapters.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 011_plugin-system/11_sp_plugin.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers Plugin System — Source Analysis

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/ v5.1.0. Superpowers = portable skills/ corpus + per-host adapters (manifests, hooks, JS plugin, sync scripts). Universal plugin manager 없음.

graph TB
    SKILLS["skills/ (14 SKILL.md)"] --> CODEX[".codex-plugin/plugin.json"]
    SKILLS --> CLAUDE[".claude-plugin + hooks/"]
    SKILLS --> CURSOR[".cursor-plugin + hooks-cursor.json"]
    SKILLS --> GEMINI["gemini-extension.json + GEMINI.md"]
    SKILLS --> OC[".opencode/plugins/superpowers.js"]
    SKILLS --> SYNC["scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh"]
    SYNC --> MARKET["openai-codex-plugins fork PR"]

1. Host Adapter Matrix

Harness Manifest Hooks / runtime Skills exposure
Codex .codex-plugin/plugin.json marketplace install "skills": "./skills/" :23
Claude .claude-plugin/plugin.json hooks/hooks.json SessionStart plugin skills/ convention
Cursor .cursor-plugin/plugin.json hooks/hooks-cursor.json explicit paths :21-24
Gemini gemini-extension.json GEMINI.md context @ file imports
OpenCode package.json main → JS SuperpowersPlugin export config.skills.paths
Copilot (uses shared session-start) SDK additionalContext plugin install

Version locked 5.1.0 across manifests (plugin.json, gemini-extension.json, package.json).

2. Codex Plugin Detail

162_superpowers/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:1-47:

  • interface.displayName: "Superpowers" (:25)
  • capabilities: Interactive, Read, Write (:30-33)
  • defaultPrompt seeds (:35-37)
  • brandColor, logos (:42-44)
  • No MCP, connectors, or tool declarations

UI category: Coding (:29). License MIT (:12).

3. Claude Plugin + Hooks

.claude-plugin/plugin.json:1-19 — keywords skills, tdd, debugging, collaboration.

hooks/hooks.json:1-15:

"SessionStart": [{
  "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
  "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "... session-start", "async": false }]
}]

hooks/session-start — cross-platform bootstrap (see config axis doc).

hooks/run-hook.cmd — Windows wrapper referenced in hooks.json :9.

4. Cursor Plugin

.cursor-plugin/plugin.json:21-24 — most complete path declarations:

"skills": "./skills/",
"agents": "./agents/",
"commands": "./commands/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks-cursor.json"

displayName field (:3) — Cursor-specific naming.

5. Gemini Extension

gemini-extension.json:1-6:

{ "name": "superpowers", "version": "5.1.0", "contextFileName": "GEMINI.md" }

GEMINI.md:1-2 imports bootstrap + gemini-tools.md.

6. OpenCode Plugin

package.json:1-7 — ESM module entry .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js.

SuperpowersPlugin async factory (superpowers.js:55):

  • Returns { config, 'experimental.chat.messages.transform' }
  • No other hook types registered in SP

.opencode/INSTALL.md — install documentation (read for host setup).

7. Marketplace Sync Pipeline

scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh (463 lines):

7.1 Target (:35-37)

  • Fork: prime-radiant-inc/openai-codex-plugins
  • Dest: plugins/superpowers
  • Opens PR via gh

7.2 Excludes (:45-77)

Host-specific and dev-only paths stripped from Codex payload:

  • .claude-plugin/, .cursor-plugin/, .opencode/, hooks/, tests/, docs/, scripts/
  • Root ceremony: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, RELEASE-NOTES.md, GEMINI.md

Anchored /scripts/ pattern (:41-43) — avoids excluding skills/brainstorming/scripts/.

7.3 Determinism (:12-13)

Same upstream SHA → identical PR diffs for verification.

7.4 Test

tests/codex-plugin-sync/test-sync-to-codex-plugin.sh — sync script regression.

8. Version + Release Hygiene

.version-bump.json — version bump automation config.

scripts/bump-version.sh — release versioning (referenced in etc axis).

RELEASE-NOTES.md (66731 bytes) — changelog corpus.

.claude-plugin/marketplace.json — marketplace metadata for Claude distribution.

9. Contributor Harness Rules

AGENTS.md:1-60 — 94% PR rejection rate warning for agents.

New harness support requires (AGENTS.md harness section):

  • Real transcript proof
  • PR template completion
  • Target dev branch not main (:32)

Zero-dependency design (:36-38) — third-party deps only for new harness support.

10. Boundary

SP provides SP does not provide
skills corpus universal plugin registry
per-host manifests plugin dependency resolver
SessionStart bootstrap tool permission runtime
Codex sync script marketplace ownership (OpenAI fork)

Compare 011_plugin-system/11_co_plugin.md for Codex plugin runtime in codex-rs.

11. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
Plugin format .codex-plugin consumer .codex-plugin producer
Skills path config + manifest manifest :23
Multi-host Codex primary 5+ adapters
Sync receives rsync upstream source

Path Plugin role
.codex-plugin/plugin.json Codex marketplace entry

Appendix B — Sync script CLI flags

scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:15-21:

Flag Purpose
-n Dry run
-y Skip confirm
--local PATH Use existing checkout
--base BRANCH Default main
--bootstrap Create plugin dir if missing

Requires: bash, rsync, git, gh, python3 (:27).

Appendix C — RELEASE-NOTES.md scale

162_superpowers/RELEASE-NOTES.md — 66731 bytes in checkout — documents version history for marketplace releases. Plugin version 5.1.0 synchronized across manifests.

Appendix D — Cursor hooks-cursor.json

Cursor-specific hook registration separate from Claude hooks.json — allows Cursor hook runner format without breaking Claude Code nested hookSpecificOutput.

Appendix E — .cursor-plugin agents/commands paths

162_superpowers/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json:22-23:

"agents": "./agents/",
"commands": "./commands/"

Codex sync excludes /commands/ (sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:70) — Cursor-only surface.

Test directory Host
tests/codex-plugin-sync/ Codex marketplace sync

tests/claude-code/ Claude Code integration
tests/skill-triggering/ Cross-host skill load
tests/explicit-skill-requests/ Explicit skill UX

Appendix G — Zero-dependency principle

AGENTS.md:36-38 — PRs adding third-party dependencies rejected unless new harness support.

Plugin payload = skills + thin adapters — no npm dependencies in package.json beyond module type declaration.

Item Detail

Sync dest path plugins/superpowers
Sync dry run -n flag
Sync bootstrap --bootstrap creates dir

Appendix — Full EXCLUDES list (sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:45-77)

Exclude pattern Rationale
/.claude/ Claude-only dot dir
/.claude-plugin/ Claude manifest — not Codex
/.codex/ Codex dev dot dir
/.cursor-plugin/ Cursor adapter
/.git/ Never ship VCS
/.github/ CI templates — not plugin payload
/.gitignore Repo hygiene
/.opencode/ OpenCode adapter
/.version-bump.json Release automation
/.worktrees/ Local worktrees
.DS_Store Finder artifacts (unanchored)
/AGENTS.md Contributor doc
/CHANGELOG.md Separate changelog
/CLAUDE.md Claude contributor
/GEMINI.md Gemini bootstrap
/RELEASE-NOTES.md Large release log
/gemini-extension.json Gemini manifest
/package.json OpenCode entry
/commands/ Cursor commands
/docs/ Dev documentation
/hooks/ SessionStart scripts
/lib/ If present — dev lib
/scripts/ sync, bump scripts
/tests/ Test harness
/tmp/ Temp files

Anchored /scripts/ (:41-43) — does not exclude skills/brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs.

Appendix — Codex plugin shipped payload (positive list)

What does sync to plugins/superpowers/:

  • Entire skills/ tree (14 skills + support files)
  • .codex-plugin/plugin.json + assets referenced therein
  • assets/ logos and icons
  • Any other tracked files not matching EXCLUDES

Result: Codex marketplace plugin is skills + presentation — single-host minimal adapter.

Appendix — Version alignment across manifests

All host manifests in checkout declare 5.1.0: .codex-plugin/plugin.json:3, .claude-plugin/plugin.json:4, .cursor-plugin/plugin.json:5, gemini-extension.json:4, package.json:3. .version-bump.json automates bump so marketplace releases stay synchronized across harness adapters.

Axis 12: Guardian & approval

AXIS

012_guardian-approval/12_sp_guardian.md

Overview

Axis 12: Guardian & approval — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers는 runtime permission engine이 아니다 — tool call intercept, allow/deny/ask, risk scoring, sandbox enforcement 없음. process-level guardian: skill-enforced approval gates for work quality.

  • graph TD
  • START["using-superpowersskill before action"] --> BRAIN["brainstorming HARD-GATEdesign approval"]
  • BRAIN --> PLAN["writing-plansexact paths + tests"]
  • PLAN --> WORK["using-git-worktreesisolation"]
  • WORK --> TDD["test-driven-developmentRED before GREEN"]
  • TDD --> SDD["subagent-driven-developmentdual review loops"]
  • SDD --> REV["requesting-code-reviewblocking findings"]
  • REV --> VER["verification-before-completionevidence gate"]
  • VER --> FIN["finishing-a-development-branchhuman branch options"]
  • | # | Gate | Skill | Mechanism | Citation |
  • | 1 | Skill invocation | using-superpowers | Must invoke relevant skills first | SKILL.md:10-16, :46 |

    1. Gate Stack (ordered)

Main analysis

Superpowers Guardian / Approval — Source Analysis

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers is not a runtime permission engine** — no tool call intercept, allow/deny/ask, risk scoring, or sandbox enforcement. process-level guardian: skill-enforced approval gates for work quality.

graph TD
    START["using-superpowers<br/>skill before action"] --> BRAIN["brainstorming HARD-GATE<br/>design approval"]
    BRAIN --> PLAN["writing-plans<br/>exact paths + tests"]
    PLAN --> WORK["using-git-worktrees<br/>isolation"]
    WORK --> TDD["test-driven-development<br/>RED before GREEN"]
    TDD --> SDD["subagent-driven-development<br/>dual review loops"]
    SDD --> REV["requesting-code-review<br/>blocking findings"]
    REV --> VER["verification-before-completion<br/>evidence gate"]
    VER --> FIN["finishing-a-development-branch<br/>human branch options"]

1. Gate Stack (ordered)

# Gate Skill Mechanism Citation
1 Skill invocation using-superpowers Must invoke relevant skills first SKILL.md:10-16, :46
2 Design approval brainstorming No implementation before approved design SKILL.md:12-14 HARD-GATE
3 Plan quality writing-plans Exact file paths, tests, no placeholders skill body
4 Worktree baseline using-git-worktrees Isolation + baseline checks Step 0 in skill
5 TDD iron law test-driven-development No prod code before failing test SKILL.md:33-45
6 Subagent review subagent-driven-development Spec review → quality review loops SKILL.md:54-80
7 Code review requesting-code-review Critical/important block code-reviewer.md
8 Completion evidence verification-before-completion Fresh verification required SKILL.md:18-38
9 Finish branch finishing-a-development-branch Tests first, structured options skill body

Hooks do not enforce gates — hooks/hooks.json:3-14 only SessionStart bootstrap.

2. Gate Detail with Source Lines

2.1 using-superpowers (SKILL.md)

Instruction priority (:18-26): user > skills > default.

Red flags table (:80-95) — blocks rationalization ("simple question", "need context first").

Skill priority (:99-105): process skills before implementation skills.

SUBAGENT-STOP (:6-8): subagents skip bootstrap when dispatched for specific task.

2.2 brainstorming HARD-GATE (SKILL.md:12-14)

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code...
until design presented and user approved.
</HARD-GATE>

Checklist 9 steps (:22-32) — each as TodoWrite task.

Terminal state (:66): only writing-plans after brainstorming — not mcp-builder.

Design doc path (:29): docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md.

2.3 test-driven-development (SKILL.md:31-45)

Iron law:

NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST

Write code before test? Delete and restart (:37-45). No exceptions.

Red-Green-Refactor graph (:49-60). Exceptions only with human partner (:24-28).

2.4 subagent-driven-development (SKILL.md:8-80)

Two-stage review per task:

  1. Spec compliance reviewer (spec-reviewer-prompt.md)
  2. Code quality reviewer (code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)

Loop on failure — re-dispatch implementer (:75-80).

Continuous execution (:14): no "should I continue?" between tasks.

Final code reviewer + finishing-a-development-branch (:66 graph).

2.5 verification-before-completion (SKILL.md:18-74)

Iron law (:18-22):

NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE

Gate function 5 steps (:26-37). Rationalization table (:65-74).

Claims vs evidence table (:42-50).

2.6 requesting-code-review

code-reviewer.md — severity tiers; critical/important findings block merge.

Skill triggered before PR / after major changes.

2.7 finishing-a-development-branch

Tests must pass before branch finish options presented to human. Discard requires explicit confirmation — destructive gate.

3. What SP Does NOT Guard

Concern Owner SP role
Shell command approval host (Codex sandbox, Claude permissions) none
MCP tool allow/deny host MCP config documents MCP naming only
Network/file sandbox host codex-tools.md App handoff when blocked
Secret leakage host + user no SP scanner
Dangerous tool calls host PreToolUse hooks SP hooks lack PreToolUse

SP guards wrong work, skipped tests, unreviewed code, false completion — not security perimeter.

4. receiving-code-review

Complementary skill — how to receive review feedback without defensive rationalization. Pairs with requesting-code-review for full review cycle.

5. systematic-debugging

Process guardian for bugs — 5-phase methodology before fix attempts. Pairs with TDD for regression tests after fix.

6. Integration Test as Guardian Verification

docs/testing.md:40-51 — SDD integration test verifies:

  • Plan loaded once
  • Full task text to subagents
  • Self-review before report
  • Spec review before quality review
  • Independent spec verification

Transcript JSONL parsing — behavioral proof gates work in practice.

7. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
Tool approval sandbox + approval modes host-only
Completion claims host + tools verification-before-completion
TDD enforcement user/policy skill iron law
Code review host workflows requesting-code-review skill

Source citations (162_superpowers)

Topic Citation
Skill-before-action skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:10-16
Instruction priority skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-26
Design HARD-GATE skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:12-14
TDD iron law skills/test-driven-development/SKILL.md:33-45
Dual review loops skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:54-80
Verification gate skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:18-38
No permission hooks hooks/hooks.json:3-14
SDD test criteria docs/testing.md:44-51
brainstorming terminal skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:66

Corpus links (axis)

  • Sandbox (host): 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_sp_sandbox.md
  • Subagents: 005_subagents-collab/05_sp_subagent.md
  • Codex guardian: 012_guardian-approval/12_co_guardian.md

Corpus links

  • Overview: 000_overview/00_sp_overview.md
  • Harmonization: 000_overview/00_corpus_harmonization_status.md
  • Co crate map: 000_overview/00_co_crate_graph.md

Change history

date change
2026-06-27 WP54 deepen to ≥12 KB with path:line citations from 162_superpowers
2026-06-27 WP38 source citations + corpus links

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot (162_superpowers v5.1.0) Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs
Plugin version 5.1.0 in manifests Check .codex-plugin/plugin.json on upgrade

Appendix A — brainstorming checklist expanded

162_superpowers/skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:22-32:

Step Guardian effect
1 Explore context Prevents blind implementation
2 Offer visual companion Optional UI gate for visual questions
3 Clarifying questions One at a time — no batch assumptions
4 Propose 2-3 approaches Forces explicit tradeoffs
5 Present design sections Chunked approval
6 Write design doc Durable approved spec
7 Spec self-review Inline placeholder check
8 User reviews spec Human sign-off on written artifact
9 Invoke writing-plans Only allowed forward transition

Appendix B — TDD red flags (test-driven-development/SKILL.md:60-120 region)

Rationalization table — "skip TDD just this once", "throwaway" without human OK, "config files exception" without ask.

Deletion rule (:37-45) — code written before test must be deleted entirely.

Appendix C — requesting-code-review severity model

code-reviewer.md in skills/requesting-code-review/:

  • Critical findings → must fix before proceed
  • Important findings → must fix or explicit waive
  • Minor → optional

Pairs with receiving-code-review for agent discipline when feedback arrives.

Appendix D — finishing-a-development-branch human gates

Skill requires:

  • Tests pass first
  • Structured options: merge, PR, discard
  • Discard requires explicit human confirmation — destructive approval

When Codex sandbox blocks git (codex-tools.md:49-59), agent commits and defers branch/PR to App UI — host guardian replaces git skill gates.

Appendix E — systematic-debugging as pre-fix guardian

Forces root-cause before patch — prevents "try random fix" loop.

Integrates with TDD: failing test proves fix, verification-before-completion proves claim.

Appendix F — Hook absence = no tool guardian

Complete hook registration in SP:

// hooks/hooks.json — ONLY SessionStart
"SessionStart": [...]

No PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest — compare Claude Code host hooks for tool approval.

Appendix G — SDD integration test assertion list

From docs/testing.md:44-51:

  1. Plan loaded once at start
  2. Full task text to subagents
  3. Self-review before report
  4. Spec review before quality review
  5. Review loops on failure
  6. Spec reviewer reads code independently

Each assertion = guardian behavior verified via transcript, not compile-time check.

Appendix — Skill iron laws inventory

Item Detail
using-superpowers Invoke skill before any action — 1% rule
brainstorming HARD-GATE No code before design approval
TDD NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT FAILING TEST FIRST
verification NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH EVIDENCE
SDD continuous No human check-in between plan tasks
SDD spec before quality Review order enforced
requesting-code-review Critical/important block
finishing branch Discard needs explicit confirm
receiving-code-review No defensive rationalization
systematic-debugging Root cause before fix
writing-plans Exact paths — no placeholders
using-git-worktrees Baseline before work
brainstorming terminal Only writing-plans after brainstorm
SUBAGENT-STOP Subagents skip using-superpowers
hooks SessionStart only No PreToolUse guardian
Codex sandbox git App UI handoff — codex-tools.md:49-59
AGENTS.md PR bar 94% rejection — quality guardian for contributors
integration test SDD Transcript verifies review order
plan document reviewer Plan quality gate prompt
spec document reviewer Design doc quality gate

Appendix — requesting-code-review workflow

162_superpowers/skills/requesting-code-review/SKILL.md — dispatch review before merge. code-reviewer.md template defines:

  • Severity tiers (critical / important / minor)
  • Blocking behavior on critical and important
  • Expected output format for reviewer agent

Pairs with integration test tests/claude-code/test-requesting-code-review.sh.

Appendix — receiving-code-review anti-rationalization

162_superpowers/skills/receiving-code-review/SKILL.md — when feedback arrives, agent must not argue or explain away findings. Guardian against ego-driven dismissal of review — complements outbound review skill.

Appendix — systematic-debugging phases

162_superpowers/skills/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md — five-phase methodology before fix:

  1. Reproduce reliably
  2. Understand scope
  3. Form hypothesis
  4. Test hypothesis minimally
  5. Fix with regression test (links to TDD iron law)

Prevents "shotgun debugging" — process guardian for bug work.

Appendix — Contributor PR guardian (AGENTS.md:52-54)

Speculative fixes rejected — every PR must solve a problem someone actually experienced. Session transcript or error required. This is human-process guardian for repo quality, parallel to agent skill gates.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 012_guardian-approval/12_sp_guardian.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers Guardian / Approval — Source Analysis

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers는 runtime permission engine이 아니다 — tool call intercept, allow/deny/ask, risk scoring, sandbox enforcement 없음. process-level guardian: skill-enforced approval gates for work quality.

graph TD
    START["using-superpowers<br/>skill before action"] --> BRAIN["brainstorming HARD-GATE<br/>design approval"]
    BRAIN --> PLAN["writing-plans<br/>exact paths + tests"]
    PLAN --> WORK["using-git-worktrees<br/>isolation"]
    WORK --> TDD["test-driven-development<br/>RED before GREEN"]
    TDD --> SDD["subagent-driven-development<br/>dual review loops"]
    SDD --> REV["requesting-code-review<br/>blocking findings"]
    REV --> VER["verification-before-completion<br/>evidence gate"]
    VER --> FIN["finishing-a-development-branch<br/>human branch options"]

1. Gate Stack (ordered)

# Gate Skill Mechanism Citation
1 Skill invocation using-superpowers Must invoke relevant skills first SKILL.md:10-16, :46
2 Design approval brainstorming No implementation before approved design SKILL.md:12-14 HARD-GATE
3 Plan quality writing-plans Exact file paths, tests, no placeholders skill body
4 Worktree baseline using-git-worktrees Isolation + baseline checks Step 0 in skill
5 TDD iron law test-driven-development No prod code before failing test SKILL.md:33-45
6 Subagent review subagent-driven-development Spec review → quality review loops SKILL.md:54-80
7 Code review requesting-code-review Critical/important block code-reviewer.md
8 Completion evidence verification-before-completion Fresh verification required SKILL.md:18-38
9 Finish branch finishing-a-development-branch Tests first, structured options skill body

Hooks do not enforce gates — hooks/hooks.json:3-14 only SessionStart bootstrap.

2. Gate Detail with Source Lines

2.1 using-superpowers (SKILL.md)

Instruction priority (:18-26): user > skills > default.

Red flags table (:80-95) — blocks rationalization ("simple question", "need context first").

Skill priority (:99-105): process skills before implementation skills.

SUBAGENT-STOP (:6-8): subagents skip bootstrap when dispatched for specific task.

2.2 brainstorming HARD-GATE (SKILL.md:12-14)

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code...
until design presented and user approved.
</HARD-GATE>

Checklist 9 steps (:22-32) — each as TodoWrite task.

Terminal state (:66): only writing-plans after brainstorming — not mcp-builder.

Design doc path (:29): docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md.

2.3 test-driven-development (SKILL.md:31-45)

Iron law:

NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST

Write code before test? Delete and restart (:37-45). No exceptions.

Red-Green-Refactor graph (:49-60). Exceptions only with human partner (:24-28).

2.4 subagent-driven-development (SKILL.md:8-80)

Two-stage review per task:

  1. Spec compliance reviewer (spec-reviewer-prompt.md)
  2. Code quality reviewer (code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)

Loop on failure — re-dispatch implementer (:75-80).

Continuous execution (:14): no "should I continue?" between tasks.

Final code reviewer + finishing-a-development-branch (:66 graph).

2.5 verification-before-completion (SKILL.md:18-74)

Iron law (:18-22):

NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE

Gate function 5 steps (:26-37). Rationalization table (:65-74).

Claims vs evidence table (:42-50).

2.6 requesting-code-review

code-reviewer.md — severity tiers; critical/important findings block merge.

Skill triggered before PR / after major changes.

2.7 finishing-a-development-branch

Tests must pass before branch finish options presented to human. Discard requires explicit confirmation — destructive gate.

3. What SP Does NOT Guard

Concern Owner SP role
Shell command approval host (Codex sandbox, Claude permissions) none
MCP tool allow/deny host MCP config documents MCP naming only
Network/file sandbox host codex-tools.md App handoff when blocked
Secret leakage host + user no SP scanner
Dangerous tool calls host PreToolUse hooks SP hooks lack PreToolUse

SP guards wrong work, skipped tests, unreviewed code, false completion — not security perimeter.

4. receiving-code-review

Complementary skill — how to receive review feedback without defensive rationalization. Pairs with requesting-code-review for full review cycle.

5. systematic-debugging

Process guardian for bugs — 5-phase methodology before fix attempts. Pairs with TDD for regression tests after fix.

6. Integration Test as Guardian Verification

docs/testing.md:40-51 — SDD integration test verifies:

  • Plan loaded once
  • Full task text to subagents
  • Self-review before report
  • Spec review before quality review
  • Independent spec verification

Transcript JSONL parsing — behavioral proof gates work in practice.

7. Parity vs co

Concern co sp
Tool approval sandbox + approval modes host-only
Completion claims host + tools verification-before-completion
TDD enforcement user/policy skill iron law
Code review host workflows requesting-code-review skill

Design HARD-GATE skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:12-14
TDD iron law skills/test-driven-development/SKILL.md:33-45
Dual review loops skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:54-80
Verification gate skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:18-38
No permission hooks hooks/hooks.json:3-14
SDD test criteria docs/testing.md:44-51
brainstorming terminal skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:66

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-06-27 WP54 deepen to ≥12 KB with path:line citations from 162_superpowers
2026-06-27 WP38 source citations + corpus links

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot (162_superpowers v5.1.0) Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs
Plugin version 5.1.0 in manifests Check .codex-plugin/plugin.json on upgrade

Appendix A — brainstorming checklist expanded

162_superpowers/skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:22-32:

Step Guardian effect
1 Explore context Prevents blind implementation
2 Offer visual companion Optional UI gate for visual questions
3 Clarifying questions One at a time — no batch assumptions
4 Propose 2-3 approaches Forces explicit tradeoffs
5 Present design sections Chunked approval
6 Write design doc Durable approved spec
7 Spec self-review Inline placeholder check
8 User reviews spec Human sign-off on written artifact
9 Invoke writing-plans Only allowed forward transition

Appendix B — TDD red flags (test-driven-development/SKILL.md:60-120 region)

Rationalization table — "skip TDD just this once", "throwaway" without human OK, "config files exception" without ask.

Deletion rule (:37-45) — code written before test must be deleted entirely.

Appendix C — requesting-code-review severity model

code-reviewer.md in skills/requesting-code-review/:

  • Critical findings → must fix before proceed
  • Important findings → must fix or explicit waive
  • Minor → optional

Pairs with receiving-code-review for agent discipline when feedback arrives.

Appendix D — finishing-a-development-branch human gates

Skill requires:

  • Tests pass first
  • Structured options: merge, PR, discard
  • Discard requires explicit human confirmation — destructive approval

When Codex sandbox blocks git (codex-tools.md:49-59), agent commits and defers branch/PR to App UI — host guardian replaces git skill gates.

Appendix E — systematic-debugging as pre-fix guardian

Forces root-cause before patch — prevents "try random fix" loop.

Integrates with TDD: failing test proves fix, verification-before-completion proves claim.

Appendix F — Hook absence = no tool guardian

Complete hook registration in SP:

// hooks/hooks.json — ONLY SessionStart
"SessionStart": [...]

No PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest — compare Claude Code host hooks for tool approval.

Appendix G — SDD integration test assertion list

From docs/testing.md:44-51:

  1. Plan loaded once at start
  2. Full task text to subagents
  3. Self-review before report
  4. Spec review before quality review
  5. Review loops on failure
  6. Spec reviewer reads code independently

Each assertion = guardian behavior verified via transcript, not compile-time check.

Appendix — Skill iron laws inventory

Item Detail
using-superpowers Invoke skill before any action — 1% rule
brainstorming HARD-GATE No code before design approval
TDD NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT FAILING TEST FIRST
verification NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH EVIDENCE
SDD continuous No human check-in between plan tasks
SDD spec before quality Review order enforced
requesting-code-review Critical/important block
finishing branch Discard needs explicit confirm
receiving-code-review No defensive rationalization
systematic-debugging Root cause before fix
writing-plans Exact paths — no placeholders
using-git-worktrees Baseline before work
brainstorming terminal Only writing-plans after brainstorm
SUBAGENT-STOP Subagents skip using-superpowers
hooks SessionStart only No PreToolUse guardian
Codex sandbox git App UI handoff — codex-tools.md:49-59
AGENTS.md PR bar 94% rejection — quality guardian for contributors
integration test SDD Transcript verifies review order
plan document reviewer Plan quality gate prompt
spec document reviewer Design doc quality gate

Appendix — requesting-code-review workflow

162_superpowers/skills/requesting-code-review/SKILL.md — dispatch review before merge. code-reviewer.md template defines:

  • Severity tiers (critical / important / minor)
  • Blocking behavior on critical and important
  • Expected output format for reviewer agent

Pairs with integration test tests/claude-code/test-requesting-code-review.sh.

Appendix — receiving-code-review anti-rationalization

162_superpowers/skills/receiving-code-review/SKILL.md — when feedback arrives, agent must not argue or explain away findings. Guardian against ego-driven dismissal of review — complements outbound review skill.

Appendix — systematic-debugging phases

162_superpowers/skills/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md — five-phase methodology before fix:

  1. Reproduce reliably
  2. Understand scope
  3. Form hypothesis
  4. Test hypothesis minimally
  5. Fix with regression test (links to TDD iron law)

Prevents "shotgun debugging" — process guardian for bug work.

Appendix — Contributor PR guardian (AGENTS.md:52-54)

Speculative fixes rejected — every PR must solve a problem someone actually experienced. Session transcript or error required. This is human-process guardian for repo quality, parallel to agent skill gates.

29

Axis 13: Cross-cutting

AXIS

013_etc/13_sp_etc.md

Overview

Axis 13: Cross-cutting — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/. Residual value = workflow governance + multi-harness distribution, not conventional runtime features (search, MCP, memory DB, sandbox).

  • | Area | Finding | Primary source |
  • | Packaging | 5 host manifests + sync scripts | .codex-plugin/, sync-to-codex-plugin.sh |
  • | Bootstrap | using-superpowers per host | hooks/session-start, OpenCode JS |
  • | Git/worktree | isolation protocol | using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md |
  • | TDD/verification | hard behavioral gates | test-driven-development, verification-before-completion |
  • | Subagents/review | prompt templates + loops | subagent-driven-development/ |
  • | Visual companion | local HTTP/WebSocket HTML server | brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs |
  • | Testing/eval | transcript-based behavior tests | docs/testing.md, tests/claude-code/ |
  • | Contributor governance | strict PR rules | AGENTS.md |
  • | Writing skills meta | persuasion + Anthropic ref | writing-skills/ |
  • | Parallel agents | independent task dispatch | dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md |

Main analysis

Superpowers Residual / Cross-Cutting Notes — Source Analysis

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/. Residual value = workflow governance + multi-harness distribution, not conventional runtime features (search, MCP, memory DB, sandbox).

1. Cross-Cutting Systems Map

Area Finding Primary source
Packaging 5 host manifests + sync scripts .codex-plugin/, sync-to-codex-plugin.sh
Bootstrap using-superpowers per host hooks/session-start, OpenCode JS
Git/worktree isolation protocol using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md
TDD/verification hard behavioral gates test-driven-development, verification-before-completion
Subagents/review prompt templates + loops subagent-driven-development/
Visual companion local HTTP/WebSocket HTML server brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs
Testing/eval transcript-based behavior tests docs/testing.md, tests/claude-code/
Contributor governance strict PR rules AGENTS.md
Writing skills meta persuasion + Anthropic ref writing-skills/
Parallel agents independent task dispatch dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md

Finish branch structured human choices finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md
Code review recv feedback discipline receiving-code-review/SKILL.md
Systematic debug 5-phase RCA systematic-debugging/SKILL.md

2. Skill Inventory (complete)

Skill Role
using-superpowers Bootstrap + invocation policy
brainstorming Design before code
writing-plans Implementation plans
writing-skills Meta — authoring skills
executing-plans Parallel-session plan execution
subagent-driven-development Same-session subagent SDD
dispatching-parallel-agents Parallel independent tasks
test-driven-development TDD iron law
systematic-debugging Debug methodology
using-git-worktrees Worktree isolation
requesting-code-review Outbound review
receiving-code-review Inbound review discipline

finishing-a-development-branch Branch finish protocol

Support files: prompts (*-prompt.md), testing-anti-patterns.md, visual-companion.md, anthropic-best-practices.md, tool reference MDs, brainstorm scripts/.

3. Visual Companion (NOT web search)

brainstorming/visual-companion.md:1-100:

  • Local browser server for mockups/diagrams (:1-4)
  • Terminal vs browser decision per question (:7-25)
  • screen_dir / state_dir under .superpowers/brainstorm/ (:29-48)
  • Platform launch notes: Claude Windows run_in_background (:58-64), Codex CODEX_CI (:67-72), Gemini --foreground (:74-79)

server.cjs:1-80:

  • WebSocket RFC6455 implementation (:6-72)
  • Random port 49152 + random (:76)
  • Default host 127.0.0.1 (:77)
  • Session dir from BRAINSTORM_DIR env (:79)

Classification: local HTTP display + click events — not internet search/crawl.

Tests: tests/brainstorm-server/server.test.js, ws-protocol.test.js.

4. Testing Harness

docs/testing.md:1-80:

tests/
├── claude-code/          # integration + helpers
├── skill-triggering/     # prompt-based skill load
├── explicit-skill-requests/
├── opencode/             # bootstrap cache, priority
├── codex-plugin-sync/
├── brainstorm-server/
└── subagent-driven-dev/  # svelte-todo, go-fractals fixtures

Integration tests: 10-30 minutes (:32). Must run from plugin root (:36).

Transcript verification (:57-63): Skill tool, Task, TodoWrite, files, tests, git.

tests/claude-code/test-helpers.sh — shared utilities.

run-skill-tests.sh — batch runner.

5. Distribution + Versioning

Artifact Role
scripts/sync-to-codex-plugin.sh Codex marketplace PR
scripts/bump-version.sh Version bumps
.version-bump.json Bump config
RELEASE-NOTES.md 66KB changelog
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json Claude marketplace
assets/ logos for plugin UI

Codex plugin UI assets: assets/superpowers-small.svg, app-icon.png (referenced in .codex-plugin/plugin.json:43-44).

6. Contributor Governance (AGENTS.md)

Key rules for agents (:11-20):

  1. Complete PR template
  2. Search open+closed PRs
  3. Verify real problem
  4. Core vs standalone plugin
  5. Disclose model/harness/plugins
  6. Human approves full diff

Will not accept (:34-59):

  • Third-party dependencies (except new harness)
  • "Compliance" skill rewrites without eval evidence
  • Project-specific config in core
  • Bulk spray-and-pray PRs
  • Speculative fixes
  • Domain-specific skills in core

Target branch: dev not main (:32).

7. Important Absences

Feature Verdict Notes
Model provider routing absent host-owned
API key/auth stack absent
Product telemetry absent tests use transcripts
MCP server absent see mcp axis
Native sandbox absent see sandbox axis
Persistent memory absent see memory axis
Web search runtime absent see web-search axis
LSP integration absent
TUI product absent methodology plugin

8. Windows / Polyglot Notes

docs/windows/polyglot-hooks.md — Windows hook compatibility.

hooks/run-hook.cmd — Windows session-start wrapper.

tests/brainstorm-server/windows-lifecycle.test.sh — Windows server lifecycle.

9. OpenCode + Codex Compatibility Plans

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-23-codex-app-compatibility.md — Codex App worktree/handoff.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-06-worktree-rototill.md — worktree workflow design.

docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-06-worktree-rototill-design.md — design spec.

10. Classification Scorecard

Dimension Score Rationale
Workflow governance High 14 skills, iron laws, review loops
Cross-harness portability High 5+ adapters
Native runtime capability Low delegates to host
Web/search None host or local HTML only
MCP None host only
Memory DB None procedural skills only

Appendix A — README workflow philosophy (README.md:9-19)

162_superpowers/README.md:11-17 core loop:

  1. Agent asks what user is really trying to do — not immediate code
  2. Spec teased in digestible chunks
  3. Implementation plan for "junior engineer with poor taste" — TDD, YAGNI, DRY
  4. Subagent-driven-development — autonomous hours-long execution

"Skills trigger automatically" (:19) — bootstrap + host skill loader, not SP daemon.

skills/brainstorming/
  SKILL.md, visual-companion.md, spec-document-reviewer-prompt.md
  scripts/server.cjs, start-server.sh, stop-server.sh, helper.js, frame-template.html

  SKILL.md, implementer-prompt.md, spec-reviewer-prompt.md, code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md
skills/writing-skills/
  SKILL.md, anthropic-best-practices.md, persuasion-principles.md
skills/writing-plans/
  SKILL.md, plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md
skills/test-driven-development/
  SKILL.md, testing-anti-patterns.md
skills/using-superpowers/references/
  codex-tools.md, gemini-tools.md, copilot-tools.md

Fixture Purpose
svelte-todo/ scaffold.sh, plan.md, design.md
go-fractals/ scaffold.sh, plan.md, design.md
run-test.sh Fixture runner

Synthetic projects for subagent integration behavior.

gemini-extension.json — only 6 lines — smallest host manifest. All behavior in GEMINI.md + skills/.

Appendix — README.md install harness list (cross-cutting)

162_superpowers/README.md:7 — Quickstart links: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Codex App, Factory Droid, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Cursor, GitHub Copilot CLI.

Each harness section below documents install commands — no unified SP daemon installer.

Appendix — tests/explicit-skill-requests/ harness

run-test.sh, run-multiturn-test.sh, run-claude-describes-sdd.sh — verify agents invoke skills when user explicitly requests skill behavior across turns.

Appendix — tests/codex-plugin-sync/

test-sync-to-codex-plugin.sh — deterministic sync verification; same SHA → identical PR diff (sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:12-13).

Appendix — Brainstorm start-server.sh lifecycle

skills/brainstorming/scripts/start-server.sh — launches server.cjs, writes server-info JSON to state dir, supports --foreground, --project-dir, --host, --url-host flags referenced in visual-companion.md:36-90.

stop-server.sh — clean shutdown; creates server-stopped marker read by visual-companion loop.

Appendix — .opencode/INSTALL.md cross-ref

OpenCode install path separate from Codex marketplace — documents copying plugin or marketplace entry for OpenCode.ai users. Part of multi-harness distribution story (README.md:33).

Verbatim KO corpus 013_etc/13_sp_etc.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

.claude-plugin/marketplace.json Claude marketplace
assets/ logos for plugin UI

Codex plugin UI assets: assets/superpowers-small.svg, app-icon.png (referenced in .codex-plugin/plugin.json:43-44).

6. Contributor Governance (AGENTS.md)

Key rules for agents (:11-20):

  1. Complete PR template
  2. Search open+closed PRs
  3. Verify real problem
  4. Core vs standalone plugin
  5. Disclose model/harness/plugins
  6. Human approves full diff

Will not accept (:34-59):

  • Third-party dependencies (except new harness)
  • "Compliance" skill rewrites without eval evidence
  • Project-specific config in core
  • Bulk spray-and-pray PRs
  • Speculative fixes
  • Domain-specific skills in core

Target branch: dev not main (:32).

7. Important Absences

Feature Verdict Notes
Model provider routing absent host-owned
API key/auth stack absent
Product telemetry absent tests use transcripts
MCP server absent see mcp axis
Native sandbox absent see sandbox axis
Persistent memory absent see memory axis
Web search runtime absent see web-search axis
LSP integration absent
TUI product absent methodology plugin

8. Windows / Polyglot Notes

docs/windows/polyglot-hooks.md — Windows hook compatibility.

hooks/run-hook.cmd — Windows session-start wrapper.

tests/brainstorm-server/windows-lifecycle.test.sh — Windows server lifecycle.

9. OpenCode + Codex Compatibility Plans

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-23-codex-app-compatibility.md — Codex App worktree/handoff.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-06-worktree-rototill.md — worktree workflow design.

docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-06-worktree-rototill-design.md — design spec.

10. Classification Scorecard

Dimension Score Rationale
Workflow governance High 14 skills, iron laws, review loops
Cross-harness portability High 5+ adapters
Native runtime capability Low delegates to host
Web/search None host or local HTML only
MCP None host only
Memory DB None procedural skills only

날짜 변경

2026-06-27 WP38 source citations + corpus links

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot (162_superpowers v5.1.0) Current

Appendix A — README workflow philosophy (README.md:9-19)

162_superpowers/README.md:11-17 core loop:

  1. Agent asks what user is really trying to do — not immediate code
  2. Spec teased in digestible chunks
  3. Implementation plan for "junior engineer with poor taste" — TDD, YAGNI, DRY
  4. Subagent-driven-development — autonomous hours-long execution

"Skills trigger automatically" (:19) — bootstrap + host skill loader, not SP daemon.

Appendix B — All skills support file tree (partial)

skills/brainstorming/
  SKILL.md, visual-companion.md, spec-document-reviewer-prompt.md
  scripts/server.cjs, start-server.sh, stop-server.sh, helper.js, frame-template.html
skills/subagent-driven-development/
  SKILL.md, implementer-prompt.md, spec-reviewer-prompt.md, code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md
skills/writing-skills/
  SKILL.md, anthropic-best-practices.md, persuasion-principles.md
skills/writing-plans/
  SKILL.md, plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md
skills/test-driven-development/
  SKILL.md, testing-anti-patterns.md
skills/using-superpowers/references/
  codex-tools.md, gemini-tools.md, copilot-tools.md

Appendix C — tests/subagent-driven-dev/ fixtures

Fixture Purpose
svelte-todo/ scaffold.sh, plan.md, design.md
go-fractals/ scaffold.sh, plan.md, design.md
run-test.sh Fixture runner

Synthetic projects for subagent integration behavior.

Appendix D — tests/skill-triggering/prompts/

Prompt files triggering specific skills:

  • writing-plans.txt
  • test-driven-development.txt
  • systematic-debugging.txt
  • requesting-code-review.txt
  • executing-plans.txt
  • dispatching-parallel-agents.txt

Used by run-all.sh for regression on skill activation.

Appendix E — OpenCode plugin file

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js — 135 lines, sole OpenCode runtime code file.

.opencode/INSTALL.md — user-facing install for OpenCode.ai harness.

Appendix F — Gemini extension minimalism

gemini-extension.json — only 6 lines — smallest host manifest. All behavior in GEMINI.md + skills/.

Appendix G — CLAUDE.md symlink

162_superpowers/AGENTS.md -> CLAUDE.md (symlink) — contributor doc duplicated for agent visibility.

CLAUDE.md — 8506 bytes — project instructions for Claude contributors.

Appendix H — Package and licensing

LICENSE — MIT (162_superpowers/LICENSE) CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md — community standards .gitattributes — line ending normalization

Appendix — Repository scale and misc paths

Item Detail
skills count 14 first-party SKILL.md files
RELEASE-NOTES size 66731 bytes in checkout
CLAUDE.md size 8506 bytes contributor guide
README.md 8354 bytes — philosophy + install
server.cjs lines ~395 lines WebSocket HTTP server
visual-companion.md ~288 lines usage guide
sync script lines ~463 lines rsync+PR
opencode plugin lines 135 lines sole JS runtime
tests/claude-code/ Integration + helpers
tests/brainstorm-server/ Local server unit tests
tests/opencode/ 4 test scripts + mjs
docs/superpowers/plans/ Design plan documents
docs/superpowers/specs/ Design spec documents
docs/testing.md Transcript testing guide
docs/README.opencode.md OpenCode skill priority
docs/windows/ polyglot-hooks.md
GEMINI.md symlink target 2 lines — minimal
gemini-extension.json 6 lines — minimal manifest
Zero npm deps package.json no dependencies
Sponsorship note README.md Jesse Vincent sponsor link

Appendix — README.md install harness list (cross-cutting)

162_superpowers/README.md:7 — Quickstart links: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Codex App, Factory Droid, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Cursor, GitHub Copilot CLI.

Each harness section below documents install commands — no unified SP daemon installer.

Appendix — tests/explicit-skill-requests/ harness

run-test.sh, run-multiturn-test.sh, run-claude-describes-sdd.sh — verify agents invoke skills when user explicitly requests skill behavior across turns.

Appendix — tests/codex-plugin-sync/

test-sync-to-codex-plugin.sh — deterministic sync verification; same SHA → identical PR diff (sync-to-codex-plugin.sh:12-13).

Appendix — Brainstorm start-server.sh lifecycle

skills/brainstorming/scripts/start-server.sh — launches server.cjs, writes server-info JSON to state dir, supports --foreground, --project-dir, --host, --url-host flags referenced in visual-companion.md:36-90.

stop-server.sh — clean shutdown; creates server-stopped marker read by visual-companion loop.

Appendix — .opencode/INSTALL.md cross-ref

OpenCode install path separate from Codex marketplace — documents copying plugin or marketplace entry for OpenCode.ai users. Part of multi-harness distribution story (README.md:33).

30

Axis 14: Web search

AXIS

014_web-search/14_sp_web-search.md

Overview

Axis 14: Web search — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers는 독립 web-search 엔진을 구현하지 않는다 — crawler, browser automation runtime, search API wrapper, MCP web-search provider 없음. Search는 호스트 tool이거나 없음. Web-adjacent surface는 (1) host tool mapping

  • graph LR
  • SP["Superpowers skills"] --> MAP["references/*-tools.mdhost tool names"]
  • MAP --> HOST["Host runtime"]
  • HOST --> WS["google_web_search / web_fetch(if host provides)"]
  • SP --> VC["brainstorming visual-companionlocalhost HTTP server"]
  • VC --> LOCAL[".superpowers/brainstorm/NOT internet"]
  • | Check | Result | Citation |
  • | Codex manifest web tools | absent | .codex-plugin/plugin.json — skills + interface only |
  • | OpenCode plugin search | absent | .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js — skills + bootstrap only |
  • | web_search in SP repo code | absent | grep — no implementation |
  • | SP MCP search server | absent | no mcpServers anywhere |

Topics covered

    1. Negative Evidence — No SP Search Runtime
    1. Host Tool Mapping Files (skills/using-superpowers/references/)
  • 2.1 Codex (codex-tools.md:1-60)
  • 2.2 Gemini (gemini-tools.md:5-17)
  • 2.3 Copilot (copilot-tools.md:5-20)
  • 2.4 OpenCode (.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:76-83)
    1. Skills Mentioning Search (prose only)
    1. Visual Companion — Local HTML Server (NOT web search)
  • 4.1 Purpose (visual-companion.md:1-25)
  • 4.2 Architecture (visual-companion.md:27-48)
  • 4.3 server.cjs (skills/brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs)
  • 4.4 Platform launch (visual-companion.md:50-81)
  • 4.5 Tests
    1. brainstorm HTML Infrastructure

Source code snippets

README / docs

graph LR
    SP["Superpowers skills"] --> MAP["references/*-tools.md<br/>host tool names"]
    MAP --> HOST["Host runtime"]
    HOST --> WS["google_web_search / web_fetch<br/>(if host provides)"]
    SP --> VC["brainstorming visual-companion<br/>localhost HTTP server"]
    VC --> LOCAL[".superpowers/brainstorm/<br/>NOT internet"]

{"type":"server-started","port":52341,"url":"http://localhost:52341",
 "screen_dir":".../content","state_dir":".../state"}

README / docs

const PORT = process.env.BRAINSTORM_PORT || (49152 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 16383));
const HOST = process.env.BRAINSTORM_HOST || '127.0.0.1';
const SESSION_DIR = process.env.BRAINSTORM_DIR || '/tmp/brainstorm';

README / docs

TodoWrite → todowrite
Task → @mention subagents
Skill → native skill tool
Read, Write, Edit, Bash → native tools

Main analysis

Superpowers Web/Search — Source Analysis

Analysis Snapshot: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers does not implement an independent web-search engine — no crawler, browser automation runtime, search API wrapper, or MCP web-search provider. Search is host tool or none. Web-adjacent surfaces are (1) host tool mapping docs, (2) brainstorming local HTML server — neither of which is internet search.

graph LR
    SP["Superpowers skills"] --> MAP["references/*-tools.md<br/>host tool names"]
    MAP --> HOST["Host runtime"]
    HOST --> WS["google_web_search / web_fetch<br/>(if host provides)"]
    SP --> VC["brainstorming visual-companion<br/>localhost HTTP server"]
    VC --> LOCAL[".superpowers/brainstorm/<br/>NOT internet"]

1. Negative Evidence — No SP Search Runtime

Check Result Citation
Codex manifest web tools absent .codex-plugin/plugin.json — skills + interface only
OpenCode plugin search absent .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js — skills + bootstrap only
web_search in SP repo code absent grep — no implementation
SP MCP search server absent no mcpServers anywhere
Skill named web-search absent 14 skills inventory

2. Host Tool Mapping Files (skills/using-superpowers/references/)

SP documents how skills should call host tools — not executes search itself.

2.1 Codex (codex-tools.md:1-60)

Maps: Task→spawn_agent, TodoWrite→update_plan, Read/Write/Bash→native.

No WebSearch or WebFetch row in mapping table (:5-14) — Codex search is host config concern, not SP skill mapping.

Documents multi_agent feature flag (:18-24) and git environment probes (:34-47).

2.2 Gemini (gemini-tools.md:5-17)

Skill reference Gemini CLI
WebSearch google_web_search (:15)
WebFetch web_fetch (:16)

Host executes — SP only names the equivalence for skill authors reading Claude-centric prose.

Additional Gemini tools (:41-50): list_directory, save_memory, ask_user, plan mode — none are SP implementations.

2.3 Copilot (copilot-tools.md:5-20)

Skill reference Copilot CLI
WebFetch web_fetch (:14)
WebSearch No equivalent (:19)

Copilot workaround (:19): web_fetch with search engine URL — documented degradation, not SP search.

GitHub MCP tools (:42): github-mcp-server-* — API search via host MCP, not SP web crawl.

2.4 OpenCode (.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:76-83)

Tool mapping block lists TodoWrite, Task, Skill, Read/Write/Edit/Bash — no web tools.

3. Skills Mentioning Search (prose only)

Skills may say "search official docs" in methodology text — host executes if configured.

writing-skills/anthropic-best-practices.md — progressive disclosure, not search runtime.

systematic-debugging — may recommend repro steps; no bundled search tool.

Parity: compare 014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md for Codex web_search builtin in codex-rs.

4. Visual Companion — Local HTML Server (NOT web search)

4.1 Purpose (visual-companion.md:1-25)

Browser-based local companion for mockups, diagrams, layout comparisons.

Decision rule (:7-8): "would user understand better by seeing than reading?"

Terminal for text/tabular questions (:17-24) — even UI topics can be conceptual.

4.2 Architecture (visual-companion.md:27-48)

  • Server watches directory for HTML files
  • Serves newest to browser
  • User clicks → events to state_dir/events
  • Persistence: --project-dir → .superpowers/brainstorm/ (:46-48)

Startup JSON (:39-41):

{"type":"server-started","port":52341,"url":"http://localhost:52341",
 "screen_dir":".../content","state_dir":".../state"}

4.3 server.cjs (skills/brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs)

Component Lines Role
WebSocket RFC6455 :6-72 Client frame encode/decode
Port selection :76 49152 + random or BRAINSTORM_PORT
Host bind :77-78 default 127.0.0.1
Session dirs :79-80 BRAINSTORM_DIR, content subdir
HTTP static serve (below :80) local HTML only

No outbound HTTP search, no fetch to external APIs for search results.

4.4 Platform launch (visual-companion.md:50-81)

Platform Note
Claude macOS/Linux default background (:52-55)
Claude Windows run_in_background: true (:58-64)
Codex CODEX_CI foreground auto-detect (:67-72)
Gemini --foreground + is_background: true (:74-79)
Remote --host 0.0.0.0 (:83-90)

30-minute inactivity auto-exit (visual-companion.md:97).

4.5 Tests

tests/brainstorm-server/server.test.js tests/brainstorm-server/ws-protocol.test.js tests/brainstorm-server/windows-lifecycle.test.sh

5. brainstorm HTML Infrastructure

skills/brainstorming/scripts/frame-template.html — wraps content fragments.

skills/brainstorming/scripts/start-server.sh / stop-server.sh — lifecycle.

skills/brainstorming/scripts/helper.js — injected into served pages for click capture.

Content fragments vs full documents (visual-companion.md:31-32).

6. Parity Table

Concern co (codex-rs) sp
web_search tool builtin in Rust stack not implemented
web_fetch tool host/browser path not implemented
Skill "search docs" prose N/A host executes if available
Local HTML UI N/A visual-companion only
Gemini mapping N/A documents google_web_search
Copilot mapping N/A no WebSearch — fetch workaround

7. Classification

Category SP
Standalone search runtime No
Search API wrapper No
Browser automation for search No
Host tool documentation Yes — references/*.md
Local brainstorming UI Yes — server.cjs
MCP web search No — host MCP only

Verdict: SP is workflow layer that may inherit host web capability. For executable search surface compare 14_co_web-search.md and 14_cc_web-search.md.

8. Common Misclassification Warnings

Misread Correction
"visual-companion = web search" Local HTTP on localhost — no query to search engines
"gemini-tools WebSearch row = SP search" Mapping doc only — Gemini CLI runs google_web_search
"copilot github-mcp = web search" GitHub API via MCP — not general web
"skill says google it" Instruction text — host tool if present

Topic Citation

Gemini WebSearch map skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md:15-16
Copilot no WebSearch skills/using-superpowers/references/copilot-tools.md:19
OpenCode no web in plugin .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:76-83
Visual companion local skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md:1-100
server.cjs localhost skills/brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs:76-80
Codex manifest no tools .codex-plugin/plugin.json:1-47
brainstorm server tests tests/brainstorm-server/server.test.js

Appendix A — Grep negative scan (SP repo)

Searched 162_superpowers/ for implementable search:

Pattern Hits in SP runtime code
web_search references only (gemini-tools.md)
WebSearch skill mapping docs
google_web_search gemini-tools.md:15
tavily / brave / exa none
crawler none in skills runtime

Appendix B — Copilot web degradation path

copilot-tools.md:19:

WebSearch | No equivalent — use web_fetch with a search engine URL

SP documents explicit degradation — skill author writing "use WebSearch" on Copilot must substitute fetch+URL pattern manually.

Appendix C — web_fetch mapping by host

Host Skill name Host tool File:line
Gemini WebFetch web_fetch gemini-tools.md:16
Copilot WebFetch web_fetch copilot-tools.md:14
Codex (not mapped) host-dependent codex-tools.md (absent)

Appendix D — Visual companion HTTP surface (not search)

server.cjs:76-80:

const PORT = process.env.BRAINSTORM_PORT || (49152 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 16383));
const HOST = process.env.BRAINSTORM_HOST || '127.0.0.1';
const SESSION_DIR = process.env.BRAINSTORM_DIR || '/tmp/brainstorm';

Random port avoids collisions — local only.

Frame template injection (visual-companion.md:31-32) — fragments wrapped unless full <!DOCTYPE or <html.

Appendix E — Brainstorm server test files

tests/brainstorm-server/package.json — test harness deps tests/brainstorm-server/ws-protocol.test.js — WebSocket framing tests/brainstorm-server/server.test.js — HTTP serve loop tests/brainstorm-server/windows-lifecycle.test.sh — Windows process lifecycle

None test internet search — local protocol only.

Appendix F — Compare CC WebSearch (corpus cross-ref)

014_web-search/14_cc_web-search.md documents Claude Code WebSearchTool with web_search_20250305 server tool — host builtin, not SP.

014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md documents Codex web_search in codex-rs — host builtin, not SP.

SP corpus docs must not claim parity with those runtimes on this axis.

Appendix G — Skill prose "search official docs"

When skills say "search documentation" in methodology text, execution path:

  1. Host exposes web tool → agent uses host tool per mapping file
  2. Host has no web tool → agent uses Read/Grep on local files only
  3. SP never spawns search on behalf of agent

Appendix H — OpenCode plugin full tool mapping block

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:76-83 verbatim:

TodoWrite → todowrite
Task → @mention subagents
Skill → native skill tool
Read, Write, Edit, Bash → native tools

No web tier in OpenCode SP adapter.

Appendix — Web-adjacent vs search classification

Item Detail
google_web_search Gemini host only — gemini-tools.md:15
web_fetch Gemini gemini-tools.md:16
web_fetch Copilot copilot-tools.md:14
WebSearch Copilot NO equivalent — copilot-tools.md:19
Codex tools map No web rows — codex-tools.md:5-14
OpenCode plugin No web in mapping — superpowers.js:76-83
visual-companion URL http://localhost:PORT — local only
BRAINSTORM_HOST Default 127.0.0.1 — server.cjs:77
BRAINSTORM_PORT Random 49152+ — server.cjs:76
server 30min timeout visual-companion.md:97
screen_dir writes HTML fragments — not URLs
state_dir events Click events — not search results
frame-template.html Wraps fragments — brainstorming/scripts/
helper.js injection Click capture in served HTML
github-mcp search GitHub API — not general web
CC WebSearchTool Host — 14_cc_web-search.md
co web_search Host — 14_co_web-search.md
SP skill google it Prose only — host executes if configured
No tavily/brave in SP Grep negative
brainstorm NOT fetch server.cjs serves local files only

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 014_web-search/14_sp_web-search.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Superpowers Web/Search — Source Analysis

분석 스냅샷: 162_superpowers/. Superpowers는 독립 web-search 엔진을 구현하지 않는다 — crawler, browser automation runtime, search API wrapper, MCP web-search provider 없음. Search는 호스트 tool이거나 없음. Web-adjacent surface는 (1) host tool mapping docs, (2) brainstorming local HTML server — 둘 다 internet search 아님.

5. brainstorm HTML Infrastructure

skills/brainstorming/scripts/frame-template.html — wraps content fragments.

skills/brainstorming/scripts/start-server.sh / stop-server.sh — lifecycle.

skills/brainstorming/scripts/helper.js — injected into served pages for click capture.

Content fragments vs full documents (visual-companion.md:31-32).

6. Parity Table

Concern co (codex-rs) sp
web_search tool builtin in Rust stack not implemented
web_fetch tool host/browser path not implemented
Skill "search docs" prose N/A host executes if available
Local HTML UI N/A visual-companion only
Gemini mapping N/A documents google_web_search
Copilot mapping N/A no WebSearch — fetch workaround

7. Classification

Category SP
Standalone search runtime No
Search API wrapper No
Browser automation for search No
Host tool documentation Yes — references/*.md
Local brainstorming UI Yes — server.cjs
MCP web search No — host MCP only

Verdict: SP is workflow layer that may inherit host web capability. For executable search surface compare 14_co_web-search.md and 14_cc_web-search.md.

8. Common Misclassification Warnings

Misread Correction
"visual-companion = web search" Local HTTP on localhost — no query to search engines
"gemini-tools WebSearch row = SP search" Mapping doc only — Gemini CLI runs google_web_search
"copilot github-mcp = web search" GitHub API via MCP — not general web
"skill says google it" Instruction text — host tool if present

Source citations (162_superpowers)

Topic Citation
Codex mapping no web skills/using-superpowers/references/codex-tools.md:5-14
Gemini WebSearch map skills/using-superpowers/references/gemini-tools.md:15-16
Copilot no WebSearch skills/using-superpowers/references/copilot-tools.md:19
OpenCode no web in plugin .opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:76-83
Visual companion local skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md:1-100
server.cjs localhost skills/brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs:76-80
Codex manifest no tools .codex-plugin/plugin.json:1-47
brainstorm server tests tests/brainstorm-server/server.test.js

Pattern Hits in SP runtime code

copilot-tools.md:19:

WebSearch | No equivalent — use web_fetch with a search engine URL

Host Skill name Host tool File:line

Appendix D — Visual companion HTTP surface (not search)

server.cjs:76-80:

const PORT = process.env.BRAINSTORM_PORT || (49152 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 16383));
const HOST = process.env.BRAINSTORM_HOST || '127.0.0.1';
const SESSION_DIR = process.env.BRAINSTORM_DIR || '/tmp/brainstorm';

Random port avoids collisions — local only.

Frame template injection (visual-companion.md:31-32) — fragments wrapped unless full <!DOCTYPE or <html.

Appendix E — Brainstorm server test files

tests/brainstorm-server/package.json — test harness deps tests/brainstorm-server/ws-protocol.test.js — WebSocket framing tests/brainstorm-server/server.test.js — HTTP serve loop tests/brainstorm-server/windows-lifecycle.test.sh — Windows process lifecycle

None test internet search — local protocol only.

Appendix F — Compare CC WebSearch (corpus cross-ref)

014_web-search/14_cc_web-search.md documents Claude Code WebSearchTool with web_search_20250305 server tool — host builtin, not SP.

014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md documents Codex web_search in codex-rs — host builtin, not SP.

SP corpus docs must not claim parity with those runtimes on this axis.

Appendix G — Skill prose "search official docs"

When skills say "search documentation" in methodology text, execution path:

  1. Host exposes web tool → agent uses host tool per mapping file
  2. Host has no web tool → agent uses Read/Grep on local files only
  3. SP never spawns search on behalf of agent

Appendix H — OpenCode plugin full tool mapping block

.opencode/plugins/superpowers.js:76-83 verbatim:

TodoWrite → todowrite
Task → @mention subagents
Skill → native skill tool
Read, Write, Edit, Bash → native tools

No web tier in OpenCode SP adapter.

Item Detail

WebSearch Copilot NO equivalent — copilot-tools.md:19

No tavily/brave in SP Grep negative
